The converted policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

## Conversion summary

| Old (`kyverno.io/v1 ClusterPolicy`) | New (`policies.kyverno.io/v1 GeneratingPolicy`) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: GeneratingPolicy` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds/selector` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` (Kubernetes VAP-style) |
| `match.any[].resources.selector.matchExpressions` (label filter) | `spec.matchConditions[]` — CEL expression checking `object.metadata.labels['dataprotection'] in ['gold','silver','bronze']` |
| `spec.rules[].context[].apiCall.urlPath + jmesPath` | `spec.variables[]` with `resource.List('config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1', 'policies', 'kasten-io')` and a CEL `.filter().size()` chain |
| `spec.rules[].preconditions.any` (`existingPolicy == 0`) | CEL ternary in `generate[].expression`: `variables.existingPolicyCount == 0 ? generator.Apply(...) : true` |
| `spec.rules[].generate.data` (inline YAML) | `spec.generate[].expression` with `generator.Apply("kasten-io", [{...}])` using `dyn()` for heterogeneous CEL objects |
| `{{ request.namespace }}` / `{{ request.object.metadata.labels.dataprotection }}` | `variables.nsName` / `variables.dpLabel` (named CEL variables) |
| `generate.namespace: kasten-io` (static field) | First argument to `generator.Apply("kasten-io", ...)` |

# This example assumes that Kasten policy presets named "gold", "silver", and "bronze" have been pre-created and Kasten was deployed into the `kasten-io` namespace.
#
# NOTE: Use of this policy will require granting the Kyverno background-controller additional privileges required to generate Kasten resources. An example ClusterRole to provide required privileges is provided within the comments of the policy manifest.
#
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:
# - apiGroups:
#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-policy-by-preset-label
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Policy from Preset
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24-1.30"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a new namespace that includes a valid "dataprotection" label, if the policy does not already exist.
      Use with "kasten-validate-ns-by-preset-label" policy to require "dataprotection" labeling on new namespaces.
spec:
  evaluation:
    synchronize:
      enabled: false

  # Trigger on Namespace CREATE or UPDATE events
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources: ['namespaces']

  # Gate execution: namespace must carry a valid dataprotection label
  matchConditions:
    - name: has-valid-dataprotection-label
      expression: >-
        has(object.metadata.labels) &&
        'dataprotection' in object.metadata.labels &&
        object.metadata.labels['dataprotection'] in ['gold', 'silver', 'bronze']

  variables:
    # Name of the triggering namespace
    - name: nsName
      expression: "object.metadata.name"

    # Value of the dataprotection label (e.g. "gold", "silver", "bronze")
    - name: dataprotectionLabel
      expression: "object.metadata.labels['dataprotection']"

    # Fetch all existing Kasten policies from the kasten-io namespace
    - name: existingPolicies
      expression: "resource.List('config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1', 'policies', 'kasten-io')"

    # True when a Kasten policy already covers this namespace with the same preset
    # Mirrors the original JMESPath precondition: existingPolicy == 0
    - name: policyExists
      expression: >-
        has(variables.existingPolicies.items) &&
        variables.existingPolicies.items.exists(p,
          has(p.spec) && has(p.spec.presetRef) &&
          p.spec.presetRef.name == variables.dataprotectionLabel &&
          has(p.spec.selector) && has(p.spec.selector.matchExpressions) &&
          p.spec.selector.matchExpressions.exists(e,
            e.key == 'k10.kasten.io/appNamespace' &&
            e.values.exists(v, v == variables.nsName)
          )
        )

    # Full Kasten Policy resource to be generated
    - name: kastenPolicy
      expression: >-
        [
          {
            "kind": dyn("Policy"),
            "apiVersion": dyn("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name": variables.nsName + "-" + variables.dataprotectionLabel + "-backup",
              "namespace": "kasten-io"
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "comment": "Auto-generated by Kyverno",
              "paused": false,
              "actions": dyn([{"action": "backup"}]),
              "presetRef": dyn({
                "name": variables.dataprotectionLabel,
                "namespace": "kasten-io"
              }),
              "selector": dyn({
                "matchExpressions": dyn([{
                  "key": "k10.kasten.io/appNamespace",
                  "operator": "In",
                  "values": dyn([variables.nsName])
                }])
              })
            })
          }
        ]

  # Only call generator.Apply when no matching Kasten policy exists yet
  generate:
    - expression: >-
        !variables.policyExists
          ? generator.Apply("kasten-io", variables.kastenPolicy)
          : true

runs.raw_log:
Written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

---

### ClusterPolicy → GeneratingPolicy translation

| Original (`kyverno.io/v1 ClusterPolicy`) | Converted (`policies.kyverno.io/v1 GeneratingPolicy`) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: GeneratingPolicy` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Namespace]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with `resources: ['namespaces']` |
| `selector.matchExpressions` on `dataprotection` label | `matchConditions` CEL expression checking `object.metadata.labels['dataprotection'] in ['gold', 'silver', 'bronze']` |
| `context[].apiCall` (JMESPath query against Kasten API) | `variables.existingPolicies` using `resource.List('config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1', 'policies', 'kasten-io')` |
| `preconditions: existingPolicy == 0` (JMESPath) | `variables.policyExists` (CEL `.exists()` filter) + conditional in `generate` expression |
| `{{ request.namespace }}` / `{{ request.object.metadata.labels.dataprotection }}` (JMESPath) | `object.metadata.name` / `object.metadata.labels['dataprotection']` (CEL via `variables`) |
| `generate.data: {spec: {...}}` (partial template) | `variables.kastenPolicy` — full resource object with `dyn()` for mixed-type fields |
| `generate.synchronize` absent (no sync) | `evaluation.synchronize.enabled: false` (explicit) |
| `generate: {...}` block | `generate: [{expression: "!variables.policyExists ? generator.Apply(...) : true"}]` |

**Key behavioral notes:**
- The `!variables.policyExists ? generator.Apply(...) : true` expression is the CEL equivalent of the original `preconditions: existingPolicy == 0` — it skips generation without error if the Kasten policy already exists.
- `matchConditions` runs before variables are evaluated, so the label check is a cheap early gate; the expensive `resource.List()` call only happens for namespaces that pass that gate.
- The background-controller ClusterRole comment from the original is preserved — those RBAC permissions are still required.

# This example assumes that Kasten policy presets named "gold", "silver", and "bronze" have been pre-created and Kasten was deployed into the `kasten-io` namespace.
#
# NOTE: Use of this policy will require granting the Kyverno background-controller additional privileges required to generate Kasten resources. An example ClusterRole to provide required privileges is provided within the comments of the policy manifest.
#
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:
# - apiGroups:
#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-policy-by-preset-label
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Policy from Preset
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24-1.30"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a new namespace that includes a valid "dataprotection" label, if the policy does not already exist.
      Use with "kasten-validate-ns-by-preset-label" policy to require "dataprotection" labeling on new namespaces.
spec:
  # Trigger on any Namespace that carries a "dataprotection" label valued gold, silver, or bronze.
  # objectSelector replaces spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.selector.matchExpressions.
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations:  ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources:   ['namespaces']
    objectSelector:
      matchExpressions:
        - key: dataprotection
          operator: In
          values:
            - gold
            - silver
            - bronze

  # variables are named CEL expressions evaluated lazily; they replace the old context[] block.
  variables:
    # Replaces {{ request.object.metadata.labels.dataprotection }} JMESPath references.
    - name: dpLabel
      expression: "object.metadata.labels.dataprotection"

    # Replaces {{ request.namespace }} JMESPath references.
    - name: nsName
      expression: "object.metadata.name"

    # Replaces the context apiCall + JMESPath existingPolicy count.
    # resource.List returns a Kubernetes List object; .items is the slice of Policy resources.
    # Filters to policies whose presetRef matches the label value AND whose selector already
    # covers this namespace — mirroring the original JMESPath query exactly.
    - name: existingPolicyCount
      expression: >-
        resource.List("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1", "policies", "kasten-io")
          .items
          .filter(p,
            has(p.spec) && has(p.spec.presetRef) &&
            p.spec.presetRef.name == object.metadata.labels.dataprotection &&
            has(p.spec.selector) && has(p.spec.selector.matchExpressions) &&
            p.spec.selector.matchExpressions.exists(e,
              has(e.key) && e.key == "k10.kasten.io/appNamespace" &&
              has(e.values) && e.values.exists(v, v == object.metadata.name)
            )
          ).size()

    # The Kasten Policy resource to create, expressed as a CEL list of resource maps.
    # dyn() is required around nested objects and lists because CEL is strongly typed.
    # Replicates spec.rules[].generate.data from the original ClusterPolicy.
    - name: kastenPolicy
      expression: >-
        [
          {
            "apiVersion": dyn("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1"),
            "kind":       dyn("Policy"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name":      variables.nsName + "-" + variables.dpLabel + "-backup",
              "namespace": "kasten-io"
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "comment": "Auto-generated by Kyverno",
              "paused":  false,
              "actions": dyn([{"action": "backup"}]),
              "presetRef": dyn({
                "name":      variables.dpLabel,
                "namespace": "kasten-io"
              }),
              "selector": dyn({
                "matchExpressions": dyn([{
                  "key":      "k10.kasten.io/appNamespace",
                  "operator": "In",
                  "values":   dyn([variables.nsName])
                }])
              })
            })
          }
        ]

  # generate replaces spec.rules[].preconditions + spec.rules[].generate.
  # The ternary mirrors the original precondition "only generate if existingPolicy count == 0".
  # generator.Apply(namespace, resources) creates the resources in the target namespace.
  generate:
    - expression: >-
        variables.existingPolicyCount == 0
          ? generator.Apply("kasten-io", variables.kastenPolicy)
          : true

runs.raw_log:
Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

---

**`apiVersion` / `kind`**
`kyverno.io/v1 ClusterPolicy` → `policies.kyverno.io/v1 GeneratingPolicy`

**Trigger matching** (`spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.selector.matchExpressions`)
→ `spec.matchConstraints.objectSelector.matchExpressions` — the `In: [gold, silver, bronze]` label selector maps directly; the resource type (Namespace, core group) goes into `matchConstraints.resourceRules`.

**Context / API call** (`spec.rules[].context[].apiCall` + JMESPath)
→ `spec.variables` with CEL. The URL path `/apis/config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1/namespaces/kasten-io/policies` becomes `resource.List("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1", "policies", "kasten-io")`. The JMESPath double-filter (presetRef name + namespace in selector values) is rewritten as a CEL `.filter()` + `.exists()` chain on `.items`. Safe `has()` guards handle optional fields.

**Preconditions** (`existingPolicy == 0`)
→ Ternary in the `generate` expression: `variables.existingPolicyCount == 0 ? generator.Apply(...) : true`. This preserves the "only generate if no matching policy exists" invariant.

**Generate data block** (`spec.rules[].generate.data`)
→ `spec.variables.kastenPolicy` — a CEL list-of-maps using `dyn()` wrappers for all nested objects and lists (required because CEL is strongly typed). `generator.Apply("kasten-io", variables.kastenPolicy)` creates the resource(s) in the target namespace.

**JMESPath variable interpolation** (`{{ request.namespace }}`, `{{ request.object.metadata.labels.dataprotection }}`)
→ Named variables `variables.nsName` (`object.metadata.name`) and `variables.dpLabel` (`object.metadata.labels.dataprotection`) referenced directly in CEL string concatenation.

The converted policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

---

## Conversion summary

| Old (`kyverno.io/v1 ClusterPolicy`) | New (`policies.kyverno.io/v1 GeneratingPolicy`) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: GeneratingPolicy` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds/selector` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` (Kubernetes VAP-style) |
| `match.any[].resources.selector.matchExpressions` (label filter) | `spec.matchConditions[]` — CEL expression checking `object.metadata.labels['dataprotection'] in ['gold','silver','bronze']` |
| `spec.rules[].context[].apiCall.urlPath + jmesPath` | `spec.variables[]` with `resource.List('config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1', 'policies', 'kasten-io')` and a CEL `.filter().size()` chain |
| `spec.rules[].preconditions.any` (`existingPolicy == 0`) | CEL ternary in `generate[].expression`: `variables.existingPolicyCount == 0 ? generator.Apply(...) : true` |
| `spec.rules[].generate.data` (inline YAML) | `spec.generate[].expression` with `generator.Apply("kasten-io", [{...}])` using `dyn()` for heterogeneous CEL objects |
| `{{ request.namespace }}` / `{{ request.object.metadata.labels.dataprotection }}` | `variables.nsName` / `variables.dpLabel` (named CEL variables) |
| `generate.namespace: kasten-io` (static field) | First argument to `generator.Apply("kasten-io", ...)` |

prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_limit_configmap_for_sa.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy this policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific ConfigMaps by ServiceAccounts. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_limit_configmap_for_sa.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: limit-configmap-for-sa
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Limit ConfigMap to ServiceAccounts for a User
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.20-1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: ConfigMap, ServiceAccount
    policies.kyverno.io/description: This policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific ConfigMaps by ServiceAccounts.
spec:
  background: false
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  rules:
  - name: limit-configmap-for-sa-developer
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - ConfigMap
        # subjects:
        # - kind: ServiceAccount
        #   name: developer
        #   namespace: kube-system
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - ConfigMap
        subjects:
        - kind: ServiceAccount
          name: another-developer
          namespace: another-namespace
    preconditions:
      all:
      - key: "{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}"
        operator: AnyIn
        value:
        - "any-namespace"
        - "another-namespace"
      - key: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
        operator: AnyIn
        value:
        - "any-configmap-name-good"
        - "another-configmap-name"
    validate:
      message: "{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}/{{request.object.kind}}/{{request.object.metadata.name}} resource is protected. Admin or allowed users can change the resource"
      deny:
        conditions:
          all:
          - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
            operator: AnyIn
            value:
            - UPDATE
            - CREATE
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: limit-configmap-for-sa
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Limit ConfigMap to ServiceAccounts for a User
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: ConfigMap, ServiceAccount
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      This policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific
      ConfigMaps by ServiceAccounts.
spec:
  # Replaces validationFailureAction: Audit.
  # Use [Deny] to enforce (block) instead of audit-only.
  validationActions:
    - Audit

  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      # Replaces match.any[].resources.kinds: [ConfigMap].
      # Operations are narrowed to CREATE/UPDATE here, absorbing the original
      # validate.deny.conditions check for request.operation in [CREATE, UPDATE].
      - apiGroups:   [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources:   ["configmaps"]

  # matchConditions replace the original preconditions block.
  # All conditions must evaluate to true for the policy to apply;
  # if any returns false the request is allowed through (not denied).
  matchConditions:
    # Replaces preconditions.all[0]:
    #   key: "{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}"
    #   operator: AnyIn
    #   value: ["any-namespace", "another-namespace"]
    - name: targeted-namespace
      expression: |
        object.metadata.namespace in ["any-namespace", "another-namespace"]

    # Replaces preconditions.all[1]:
    #   key: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
    #   operator: AnyIn
    #   value: ["any-configmap-name-good", "another-configmap-name"]
    - name: targeted-configmap-name
      expression: |
        object.metadata.name in ["any-configmap-name-good", "another-configmap-name"]

    # NOTE — subject (ServiceAccount) filtering:
    # The original policy had two match.any branches OR-ed together:
    #   [0] ConfigMap resources with its ServiceAccount subject commented out
    #       (was: developer / kube-system) — effectively matches ALL requesters.
    #   [1] ConfigMap resources + subjects: another-developer / another-namespace.
    # Because branch [0] matched all requesters, the combined OR also matched all
    # requesters, making branch [1]'s subject filter redundant.
    #
    # To restrict the policy to specific ServiceAccounts (the likely intent),
    # uncomment and adjust the matchCondition below:
    #
    # - name: targeted-serviceaccount
    #   expression: |
    #     request.userInfo.username == "system:serviceaccount:another-namespace:another-developer"
    #     || request.userInfo.username == "system:serviceaccount:kube-system:developer"
    #
    # Kyverno also provides a parseServiceAccount() helper for readability:
    #
    # - name: targeted-serviceaccount
    #   expression: |
    #     parseServiceAccount(request.userInfo.username).Name in
    #       ["another-developer", "developer"]

  validations:
    # Replaces validate.deny.conditions.all:
    #   key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
    #   operator: AnyIn
    #   value: [UPDATE, CREATE]
    #
    # Since matchConstraints.resourceRules.operations already filters to CREATE
    # and UPDATE, any request that reaches this validation has matched. The CEL
    # expression "false" unconditionally fails the validation (i.e., denies the
    # request), mirroring the original deny behaviour.
    - expression: "false"
      # Replaces validate.message with equivalent CEL string concatenation.
      # object.kind is available via the admission request object.
      messageExpression: |
        object.metadata.namespace + "/" + object.kind + "/" + object.metadata.name + " resource is protected. Admin or allowed users can change the resource"

  evaluation:
    # Replaces background: false.
    background:
      enabled: false
raw_log:
Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

---

### Conversion mapping

| Original (`kyverno.io/v1` ClusterPolicy) | Converted (`policies.kyverno.io/v1` ValidatingPolicy) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` | Cluster-scoped; no binding resource needed |
| `validationFailureAction: Audit` | `validationActions: [Audit]` | Change to `[Deny]` to enforce |
| `background: false` | `evaluation.background.enabled: false` | |
| `match.any[].resources.kinds: [ConfigMap]` | `matchConstraints.resourceRules[0].resources: [configmaps]`, `apiGroups: [""]` | Core API group, lowercase plural |
| `validate.deny.conditions: operation in [CREATE, UPDATE]` | `matchConstraints.resourceRules[0].operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` | Operations filter absorbed into matchConstraints; the deny condition becomes trivially `expression: "false"` |
| `preconditions.all[0]` (namespace AnyIn) | `matchConditions[0]` — `object.metadata.namespace in [...]` | CEL `in` replaces JMESPath AnyIn |
| `preconditions.all[1]` (name AnyIn) | `matchConditions[1]` — `object.metadata.name in [...]` | Same pattern |
| `validate.deny` (always deny when reached) | `validations[0].expression: "false"` | `false` fails validation → request denied/audited |
| `validate.message` (JMESPath template) | `validations[0].messageExpression` (CEL string concat) | |
| `match.any[].subjects` (one commented out, one active) | Commented-out `matchConditions` block | The first branch's SA was commented out, making the OR match all requesters — faithfully preserved with a note. Use `parseServiceAccount(request.userInfo.username)` or `request.userInfo.username == "system:serviceaccount:<ns>:<name>"` to restore SA filtering. |

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: limit-configmap-for-sa
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Limit ConfigMap to ServiceAccounts for a User
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: ConfigMap, ServiceAccount
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      This policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific
      ConfigMaps by ServiceAccounts.
spec:
  # Replaces spec.validationFailureAction: Audit
  validationActions:
    - Audit

  # Replaces spec.rules[].match — resourceRules define which resource
  # operations trigger this policy.
  #
  # The original match.any had two branches:
  #   1. All ConfigMap resources (subject restriction was commented out —
  #      originally intended for SA "developer" in namespace "kube-system")
  #   2. ConfigMaps requested by SA "another-developer" in "another-namespace"
  #
  # Because branch 1 has no subject restriction it matches all users, which
  # subsumes branch 2. The converted policy therefore matches all users.
  #
  # To restrict to specific ServiceAccounts instead, add a matchCondition such
  # as:
  #   - name: target-service-accounts
  #     expression: >-
  #       request.userInfo.username in [
  #         'system:serviceaccount:kube-system:developer',
  #         'system:serviceaccount:another-namespace:another-developer'
  #       ]
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources:   ["configmaps"]

  # Replaces spec.rules[].preconditions — all conditions must be true for the
  # validations block to be evaluated.
  matchConditions:
    # Replaces: key: "{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}" AnyIn [...]
    - name: target-namespace
      expression: >-
        object.metadata.namespace in ['any-namespace', 'another-namespace']

    # Replaces: key: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}" AnyIn [...]
    - name: target-configmap-name
      expression: >-
        object.metadata.name in ['any-configmap-name-good', 'another-configmap-name']

  # Replaces spec.background: false
  evaluation:
    admission:
      enabled: true
    background:
      enabled: false  # deny rules keyed on request.operation are meaningless in background scans

  # Replaces spec.rules[].validate.deny — in ValidatingPolicy the expression
  # must return true to PASS; returning false denies the request.
  #
  # The original denied when request.operation was CREATE or UPDATE.
  # matchConstraints already limits evaluation to those two operations, so
  # the validation unconditionally denies every matched request.
  validations:
    - expression: "false"
      messageExpression: >-
        object.metadata.namespace + '/ConfigMap/' + object.metadata.name +
        ' resource is protected. Admin or allowed users can change the resource'
runs.raw_log:
The converted policy is at `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every mapping applied:

| Original (ClusterPolicy) | Converted (ValidatingPolicy) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy` |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `spec.background: false` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: false` |
| `spec.rules[].match` (ConfigMap kind) | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` — ConfigMaps, apiGroup `""`, CREATE+UPDATE operations |
| `spec.rules[].preconditions` (JMESPath `AnyIn`) | `spec.matchConditions` (CEL `in` operator) — one condition per precondition |
| `spec.rules[].validate.deny.conditions` | `spec.validations[].expression: "false"` — logic is inverted (true = pass, false = deny); since matchConstraints already gates on CREATE/UPDATE, an unconditional `false` is correct |
| `{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}` / `{{request.object.metadata.name}}` | `object.metadata.namespace` / `object.metadata.name` (plain CEL, no braces) |
| Dynamic `validate.message` with JMESPath | `messageExpression` using CEL string concatenation |

**Key structural note on subject matching:** The original `match.any` had two branches — one with a commented-out subject (intended for SA `developer`/`kube-system`) and one explicitly matching SA `another-developer`/`another-namespace`. Because the first branch had no active subject restriction, it matched all users, making the second branch redundant. The converted policy preserves that all-users behavior, with a comment showing how to add a `matchConditions` entry to restrict to specific ServiceAccounts if that was the intended future direction.

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: limit-configmap-for-sa
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Limit ConfigMap to ServiceAccounts for a User
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: ConfigMap, ServiceAccount
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      This policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific ConfigMaps
      by ServiceAccounts.
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: "1.16.0"
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.26+"
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false  # request.userInfo is not available during background scans
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        resources: ["configmaps"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]  # from validate.deny: operation AnyIn [UPDATE, CREATE]
  matchConditions:
    # Replaces precondition: request.object.metadata.namespace AnyIn [any-namespace, another-namespace]
    - name: namespace-filter
      expression: >-
        object.metadata.namespace in ['any-namespace', 'another-namespace']
    # Replaces precondition: request.object.metadata.name AnyIn [any-configmap-name-good, another-configmap-name]
    - name: configmap-name-filter
      expression: >-
        object.metadata.name in ['any-configmap-name-good', 'another-configmap-name']
    # Replaces match.any[1].subjects: ServiceAccount another-developer/another-namespace
    # (The original also had a commented-out subject for developer/kube-system in match.any[0];
    #  add another matchCondition or broaden this expression if that SA should also be restricted.)
    - name: serviceaccount-filter
      expression: >-
        parseServiceAccount(request.userInfo.username).Namespace == 'another-namespace' &&
        parseServiceAccount(request.userInfo.username).Name == 'another-developer'
  validations:
    # Deny pattern: expression "false" always fails when matchConditions are all satisfied.
    # Replaces validate.deny with conditions checking operation AnyIn [UPDATE, CREATE]
    # (operations are now enforced at the matchConstraints level above).
    - expression: "false"
      messageExpression: >-
        object.metadata.namespace + '/' + request.kind.kind + '/' + object.metadata.name +
        ' resource is protected. Admin or allowed users can change the resource'
runs.raw_log:
Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

| Original (ClusterPolicy) | Converted (ValidatingPolicy) | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy` | New API for Kyverno 1.16+ |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` | Renamed field; now a list |
| `spec.background: false` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: false` | `request.userInfo` is unavailable in background scans |
| `match.any[*].resources.kinds: [ConfigMap]` + `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` (from deny conditions) | `matchConstraints.resourceRules` with `resources: [configmaps]`, `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]`, `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: [v1]` | Coarse webhook-level filter; operations moved here from `validate.deny` |
| `preconditions.all` — namespace `AnyIn [...]` | `matchConditions[namespace-filter]` CEL: `object.metadata.namespace in [...]` | CEL replaces JMESPath preconditions |
| `preconditions.all` — name `AnyIn [...]` | `matchConditions[configmap-name-filter]` CEL: `object.metadata.name in [...]` | Same |
| `match.any[1].subjects: SA another-developer/another-namespace` | `matchConditions[serviceaccount-filter]` using `parseServiceAccount(request.userInfo.username)` | Kyverno CEL library function for SA identity matching |
| `validate.deny` (always blocks when reached) | `validations[0].expression: "false"` | The "deny" pattern in ValidatingPolicy — always fails when all `matchConditions` pass |
| `validate.message` with JMESPath interpolation | `validations[0].messageExpression` with CEL string concatenation | CEL replaces JMESPath template expressions |

**Note on the commented-out subject** (`developer`/`kube-system` in `match.any[0]`): the original's first `any` arm had no effective SA restriction (the subject was commented out), making it a superset of the second arm. The converted policy preserves the explicitly active SA restriction (`another-developer`/`another-namespace`). If the intent is to also restrict `developer`/`kube-system`, add a second `matchConditions` expression with `||` or add it as a separate ValidatingPolicy rule.

yaml_preview:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: pdb-minavailable-check
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Check PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.28"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: PodDisruptionBudget, Deployment, StatefulSet
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      When a Pod controller which can run multiple replicas is subject to an active PodDisruptionBudget,
      if the replicas field has a value equal to the minAvailable value of the PodDisruptionBudget
      it may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains which may impact routine maintenance
      tasks and disrupt operations. This policy checks incoming Deployments and StatefulSets which have
      a matching PodDisruptionBudget to ensure these two values do not match.
spec:
  # Audit mirrors the original validationFailureAction: Audit.
  # Change to Deny to enforce hard blocking.
  validationActions:
    - Audit

  evaluation:
    # Mirrors original background: false — live API calls via resource.List()
    # are not available during background scans.
    background:
      enabled: false

  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        # Mirrors original precondition: operation must be CREATE or UPDATE.
        operations:  [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources:   ["deployments", "statefulsets"]

  # matchConditions are evaluated before variables and validations.
  # A false result exempts the resource from this policy entirely.
  matchConditions:
    # Mirrors original precondition:
    #   key: "{{ request.object.spec.replicas || `1` }}"
    #   operator: GreaterThan
    #   value: 0
    # If replicas is not set the Kubernetes default is 1 (> 0), so those
    # resources still proceed to the validation step.
    - name: replicas-greater-than-zero
      expression: >-
        !has(object.spec.replicas) || object.spec.replicas > 0

  variables:
    # Capture pod-template labels once so they can be reused in the filter below.
    - name: podLabels
      expression: >-
        object.spec.template.metadata.?labels.orValue({})

    # Mirrors original context apiCall:
    #   urlPath: "/apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{{request.namespace}}/poddisruptionbudgets"
    # resource.List() makes a live API call at admission time.
    - name: pdbs
      expression: >-
        resource.List("policy/v1", "poddisruptionbudgets", object.metadata.namespace).items

    # Mirrors original jmesPath label_match filter:
    #   items[?label_match(spec.selector.matchLabels, `{{...labels}}`)]
    # A PDB matches when every key in its matchLabels selector is present in
    # the workload's pod-template labels with the same value (i.e. the selector
    # is a subset of the pod labels).
    - name: matchingPdbs
      expression: >-
        variables.pdbs.filter(pdb,
          has(pdb.spec.selector) &&
          has(pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels) &&
          pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels.all(k,
            variables.podLabels[?k].orValue("") == pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels[k]
          )
        )

    # Mirrors original jmesPath tail: | [0] | spec.minAvailable || `0`
    # Returns 0 when:
    #   - no PDB matches (no matching PDB found)
    #   - the first matching PDB has no minAvailable set
    #   - minAvailable is a percentage string (e.g. "50%") — a string cannot
    #     equal an integer replica count, so those PDBs are intentionally
    #     excluded from this integer-comparison check.
    - name: minAvailable
      expression: >-
        variables.matchingPdbs.size() > 0 &&
        has(variables.matchingPdbs[0].spec.minAvailable) &&
        type(variables.matchingPdbs[0].spec.minAvailable) == int ?
        variables.matchingPdbs[0].spec.minAvailable : 0

    # Replica count with the same default-to-1 fallback as the original
    # precondition, used in both the validation expression and the message.
    - name: replicaCount
      expression: >-
        has(object.spec.replicas) ? object.spec.replicas : 1

  validations:
    # Mirrors original deny condition:
    #   key: "{{ request.object.spec.replicas }}"
    #   operator: Equals
    #   value: "{{ minavailable }}"
    #
    # The expression must evaluate to TRUE for the resource to be ALLOWED.
    # So the rule passes (no violation) when:
    #   - minAvailable == 0  → no applicable PDB was found
    #   - replicaCount != minAvailable → the values differ (safe configuration)
    - expression: >-
        variables.minAvailable == 0 || variables.replicaCount != variables.minAvailable
      # Static fallback message (used when messageExpression cannot be evaluated).
      message: >-
        The matching PodDisruptionBudget for this resource has its minAvailable
        value equal to the replica count which is not permitted.
      # Dynamic message with runtime values for easier diagnosis.
      messageExpression: >-
        object.kind + "/" + object.metadata.name +
        ": the matching PodDisruptionBudget has minAvailable=" +
        string(variables.minAvailable) +
        " which equals the replica count=" +
        string(variables.replicaCount) +
        ". This may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains" +
        " and impact routine maintenance tasks."
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: pdb-minavailable-check
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Check PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.9.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: PodDisruptionBudget, Deployment, StatefulSet
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      When a Pod controller which can run multiple replicas is subject to an active PodDisruptionBudget,
      if the replicas field has a value equal to the minAvailable value of the PodDisruptionBudget
      it may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains which may impact routine maintenance
      tasks and disrupt operations. This policy checks incoming Deployments and StatefulSets which have
      a matching PodDisruptionBudget to ensure these two values do not match.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: false
  rules:
    - name: pdb-minavailable
      match:
        any:
          - resources:
              kinds:
                - Deployment
                - StatefulSet
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: AnyIn
          value:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        - key: "{{ request.object.spec.replicas || `1` }}"
          operator: GreaterThan
          value: 0
      context:
        - name: minavailable
          apiCall:
            urlPath: "/apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{{request.namespace}}/poddisruptionbudgets"
            jmesPath: "items[?label_match(spec.selector.matchLabels, `{{request.object.spec.template.metadata.labels}}`)] | [0] | spec.minAvailable || `0`"
      validate:
        message: >-
          The matching PodDisruptionBudget for this resource has its minAvailable value equal to the replica count
          which is not permitted.
        deny:
          conditions:
            any:
              - key: "{{ request.object.spec.replicas }}"
                operator: Equals
                value: "{{ minavailable }}"

raw_log:
Here is a summary of every conversion decision made:

---

### Key changes: `ClusterPolicy` → `ValidatingPolicy`

| Old (`kyverno.io/v1`) | New (`policies.kyverno.io/v1`) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` | New cluster-scoped type |
| `validationFailureAction: Audit` | `validationActions: [Audit]` | Same semantics |
| `background: false` | `evaluation.background.enabled: false` | Required — `resource.List()` makes live API calls unavailable during background scans |
| `match.any[].resources.kinds` + `preconditions` operation filter | `matchConstraints.resourceRules[].operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` | Operations moved to the resource rule |
| `preconditions` replicas > 0 (JMESPath) | `matchConditions` CEL boolean | `!has(object.spec.replicas) \|\| object.spec.replicas > 0` preserves the `\|\| 1` default from the original |
| `context[].apiCall.urlPath` + `jmesPath: label_match(...)` | `variables[].expression: resource.List(...)` + `.filter(...)` | CEL `resource.List("policy/v1", "poddisruptionbudgets", object.metadata.namespace)` replaces the raw URL call; CEL `.filter()` + `.all()` replaces `label_match` |
| JMESPath `\| [0] \| spec.minAvailable \|\| '0'` | `variables.matchingPdbs[0].spec.minAvailable` with `type() == int` guard | The `type()` check correctly skips percentage-string `minAvailable` values (e.g. `"50%"`), which cannot be meaningfully compared to an integer replica count |
| `validate.deny.conditions` (deny when equal) | `validations[].expression` must be **true to allow** | Logic is inverted: `minAvailable == 0 \|\| replicaCount != minAvailable` |
| No dynamic message | `messageExpression` | CEL string expression embeds runtime values for diagnosis |

runs.yaml_preview:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: pdb-minavailable-check
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Check PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.30"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: PodDisruptionBudget, Deployment, StatefulSet
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      When a Pod controller which can run multiple replicas is subject to an active PodDisruptionBudget,
      if the replicas field has a value equal to the minAvailable value of the PodDisruptionBudget
      it may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains which may impact routine maintenance
      tasks and disrupt operations. This policy checks incoming Deployments and StatefulSets which have
      a matching PodDisruptionBudget to ensure these two values do not match.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit

  evaluation:
    admission:
      enabled: true
    background:
      enabled: false

  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
          - statefulsets

  # Equivalent to the original precondition: skip if replicas is explicitly 0.
  # Replicas defaults to 1 when unset, so this guard only fires for an edge case.
  matchConditions:
    - name: has-nonzero-replicas
      expression: "object.spec.?replicas.orValue(1) > 0"

  # Replaces spec.rules[].context[].apiCall.
  # podLabels: pod template labels used to match against PDB selectors.
  # allPdbs: all PDBs in the same namespace as the workload.
  # matchingPdbs: PDBs whose matchLabels are a subset of the pod template labels
  #               (mirrors the original label_match() JMESPath function behaviour).
  # minAvailable: the integer minAvailable from the first matching PDB, or 0 if
  #               none exists or the field is absent/a percentage string.
  variables:
    - name: podLabels
      expression: "object.spec.template.metadata.?labels.orValue({})"
    - name: allPdbs
      expression: >-
        resource.List("policy/v1", "poddisruptionbudgets", object.metadata.namespace).items
    - name: matchingPdbs
      expression: >-
        variables.allPdbs.filter(pdb,
          has(pdb.spec.selector) &&
          has(pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels) &&
          pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels.all(k,
            k in variables.podLabels &&
            variables.podLabels[k] == pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels[k]
          )
        )
    - name: minAvailable
      expression: >-
        variables.matchingPdbs.size() > 0 && has(variables.matchingPdbs[0].spec.minAvailable) ?
          variables.matchingPdbs[0].spec.minAvailable : 0

  # Replaces spec.rules[].validate.deny (logic inverted: expression must be TRUE to pass).
  # Pass when:
  #   - No matching PDB was found (minAvailable == 0, the safe default), OR
  #   - The matching PDB's minAvailable is a percentage string (type != int, != replicas), OR
  #   - replicas does not equal minAvailable.
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.minAvailable == 0 ||
        object.spec.?replicas.orValue(1) != variables.minAvailable
      message: >-
        The matching PodDisruptionBudget for this resource has its minAvailable value equal to the replica count
        which is not permitted.

---

### Conversion mapping

| Old `ClusterPolicy` field | New `ValidatingPolicy` field | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy` | Cluster-scoped; no namespace restriction |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` | Audit = allow but record in PolicyReport |
| `spec.background: false` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: false` | Admission-only scanning |
| `spec.rules[].match` (Deployment, StatefulSet) | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` with `apiGroups: [apps]`, `resources: [deployments, statefulsets]` | Structural resource filter |
| Precondition: `operation in [CREATE, UPDATE]` | `matchConstraints.resourceRules[].operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` | Moved into routing layer |
| Precondition: `replicas \|\| 1 > 0` | `spec.matchConditions[has-nonzero-replicas]` as CEL `object.spec.?replicas.orValue(1) > 0` | CEL boolean guard; `?` does safe optional traversal |
| `spec.rules[].context[].apiCall` (PDB list + JMESPath `label_match`) | `spec.variables[]` using `resource.List("policy/v1", "poddisruptionbudgets", ...)` + CEL `.filter()` | `resource.List()` replaces URL-based API calls; `pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels.all(k, ...)` reproduces `label_match()` semantics |
| `spec.rules[].validate.deny.conditions[replicas == minavailable]` | `spec.validations[].expression: replicas != minAvailable` | Deny logic is **inverted** — expression must return `true` to pass |

### Key CEL behaviours to note

- If no matching PDB exists, `variables.minAvailable` evaluates to `0`. Since `replicas > 0` (ensured by `matchConditions`), the expression `replicas != 0` is always `true` — the validation passes cleanly with no false positives.
- If `minAvailable` is a percentage string (e.g. `"50%"`), the CEL `!=` comparison between a `string` and the `int` replicas count will not match, so the validation passes — same effective behaviour as the original JMESPath comparison.

runs.yaml_preview:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: pdb-minavailable-check
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Check PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.26"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: PodDisruptionBudget, Deployment, StatefulSet
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      When a Pod controller which can run multiple replicas is subject to an active PodDisruptionBudget,
      if the replicas field has a value equal to the minAvailable value of the PodDisruptionBudget
      it may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains which may impact routine maintenance
      tasks and disrupt operations. This policy checks incoming Deployments and StatefulSets which have
      a matching PodDisruptionBudget to ensure these two values do not match.
spec:
  # Audit mode — mirrors the original validationFailureAction: Audit
  validationActions:
    - Audit

  # Disable background scanning; this policy requires live admission context
  # for the resource.List() API lookup (mirrors original background: false)
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false

  # Match Deployments and StatefulSets on CREATE and UPDATE (mirrors original match + preconditions)
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
          - statefulsets

  # Only evaluate when the replica count is greater than zero
  # (mirrors original precondition: request.object.spec.replicas > 0)
  matchConditions:
    - name: replicas-greater-than-zero
      expression: "object.spec.?replicas.orValue(1) > 0"

  variables:
    # Fetch all PodDisruptionBudgets in the same namespace as the resource
    # (mirrors original apiCall to /apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{{request.namespace}}/poddisruptionbudgets)
    - name: pdbs
      expression: >-
        resource.List("policy/v1", "poddisruptionbudgets", object.metadata.namespace).items

    # Filter to PDBs whose selector.matchLabels are a subset of the pod template labels
    # (mirrors original jmesPath label_match filter; vacuously matches if matchLabels is empty)
    - name: matchingPdb
      expression: >-
        variables.pdbs.filter(pdb,
          has(pdb.spec.selector) &&
          has(pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels) &&
          pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels.all(k,
            k in object.spec.template.metadata.?labels.orValue({}) &&
            object.spec.template.metadata.?labels.orValue({})[k] == pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels[k]
          )
        )

  validations:
    # Pass when:
    #   - No PDB matches this resource, OR
    #   - The first matching PDB has no minAvailable set, OR
    #   - minAvailable is a percentage string (e.g. "50%") which cannot be compared to an integer replica count, OR
    #   - The replica count does not equal minAvailable
    # Fail (audit) when replicas == minAvailable (integer), mirroring the original deny rule.
    - expression: >-
        variables.matchingPdb.size() == 0 ||
        !has(variables.matchingPdb[0].spec.minAvailable) ||
        type(variables.matchingPdb[0].spec.minAvailable) != type(0) ||
        object.spec.?replicas.orValue(1) != variables.matchingPdb[0].spec.minAvailable
      messageExpression: >-
        "The matching PodDisruptionBudget '" + variables.matchingPdb[0].metadata.name +
        "' has its minAvailable value (" + string(variables.matchingPdb[0].spec.minAvailable) +
        ") equal to the replica count (" + string(object.spec.?replicas.orValue(1)) +
        ") which is not permitted."

runs.raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of all the conversion decisions made:

---

## Conversion Summary

### API / Kind
| Before | After |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` |

### Enforcement mode
| Before | After |
|---|---|
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `spec.background: false` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: false` |

### Matching (`match` → `matchConstraints`)
The old `match.any[].resources.kinds: [Deployment, StatefulSet]` maps to `matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` with explicit `apiGroups: [apps]`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, and `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]`. Operations were previously handled by a `preconditions` block and are now a first-class field.

### Preconditions → `matchConditions`
The `replicas > 0` precondition becomes a CEL `matchConditions` entry:
```cel
object.spec.?replicas.orValue(1) > 0
```

### Context API call → `variables` with `resource.List()`
The old JMESPath `apiCall` is replaced by two CEL variables:
- **`pdbs`** — calls `resource.List("policy/v1", "poddisruptionbudgets", object.metadata.namespace).items`
- **`matchingPdb`** — filters with `pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels.all(...)` (equivalent of `label_match`)

### Validation (`deny.conditions` → `validations[]`)
The logic is inverted from a deny-condition to a pass-condition in CEL. The expression passes when no matching PDB exists, or when `minAvailable` is absent, or when it is a percentage string (can't be compared to an integer replica count), or when the values differ. It fails (audits) only when all conditions align — matching PDB with integer `minAvailable` equal to `spec.replicas`. The `messageExpression` dynamically includes the PDB name and the conflicting values.

runs.yaml_preview:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: pdb-minavailable-check
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Check PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.28"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: PodDisruptionBudget, Deployment, StatefulSet
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      When a Pod controller which can run multiple replicas is subject to an active PodDisruptionBudget,
      if the replicas field has a value equal to the minAvailable value of the PodDisruptionBudget
      it may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains which may impact routine maintenance
      tasks and disrupt operations. This policy checks incoming Deployments and StatefulSets which have
      a matching PodDisruptionBudget to ensure these two values do not match.
spec:
  # Audit mirrors the original validationFailureAction: Audit.
  # Change to Deny to enforce hard blocking.
  validationActions:
    - Audit

  evaluation:
    # Mirrors original background: false — live API calls via resource.List()
    # are not available during background scans.
    background:
      enabled: false

  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        # Mirrors original precondition: operation must be CREATE or UPDATE.
        operations:  [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources:   ["deployments", "statefulsets"]

  # matchConditions are evaluated before variables and validations.
  # A false result exempts the resource from this policy entirely.
  matchConditions:
    # Mirrors original precondition:
    #   key: "{{ request.object.spec.replicas || `1` }}"
    #   operator: GreaterThan
    #   value: 0
    # If replicas is not set the Kubernetes default is 1 (> 0), so those
    # resources still proceed to the validation step.
    - name: replicas-greater-than-zero
      expression: >-
        !has(object.spec.replicas) || object.spec.replicas > 0

  variables:
    # Capture pod-template labels once so they can be reused in the filter below.
    - name: podLabels
      expression: >-
        object.spec.template.metadata.?labels.orValue({})

    # Mirrors original context apiCall:
    #   urlPath: "/apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{{request.namespace}}/poddisruptionbudgets"
    # resource.List() makes a live API call at admission time.
    - name: pdbs
      expression: >-
        resource.List("policy/v1", "poddisruptionbudgets", object.metadata.namespace).items

    # Mirrors original jmesPath label_match filter:
    #   items[?label_match(spec.selector.matchLabels, `{{...labels}}`)]
    # A PDB matches when every key in its matchLabels selector is present in
    # the workload's pod-template labels with the same value (i.e. the selector
    # is a subset of the pod labels).
    - name: matchingPdbs
      expression: >-
        variables.pdbs.filter(pdb,
          has(pdb.spec.selector) &&
          has(pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels) &&
          pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels.all(k,
            variables.podLabels[?k].orValue("") == pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels[k]
          )
        )

    # Mirrors original jmesPath tail: | [0] | spec.minAvailable || `0`
    # Returns 0 when:
    #   - no PDB matches (no matching PDB found)
    #   - the first matching PDB has no minAvailable set
    #   - minAvailable is a percentage string (e.g. "50%") — a string cannot
    #     equal an integer replica count, so those PDBs are intentionally
    #     excluded from this integer-comparison check.
    - name: minAvailable
      expression: >-
        variables.matchingPdbs.size() > 0 &&
        has(variables.matchingPdbs[0].spec.minAvailable) &&
        type(variables.matchingPdbs[0].spec.minAvailable) == int ?
        variables.matchingPdbs[0].spec.minAvailable : 0

    # Replica count with the same default-to-1 fallback as the original
    # precondition, used in both the validation expression and the message.
    - name: replicaCount
      expression: >-
        has(object.spec.replicas) ? object.spec.replicas : 1

  validations:
    # Mirrors original deny condition:
    #   key: "{{ request.object.spec.replicas }}"
    #   operator: Equals
    #   value: "{{ minavailable }}"
    #
    # The expression must evaluate to TRUE for the resource to be ALLOWED.
    # So the rule passes (no violation) when:
    #   - minAvailable == 0  → no applicable PDB was found
    #   - replicaCount != minAvailable → the values differ (safe configuration)
    - expression: >-
        variables.minAvailable == 0 || variables.replicaCount != variables.minAvailable
      # Static fallback message (used when messageExpression cannot be evaluated).
      message: >-
        The matching PodDisruptionBudget for this resource has its minAvailable
        value equal to the replica count which is not permitted.
      # Dynamic message with runtime values for easier diagnosis.
      messageExpression: >-
        object.kind + "/" + object.metadata.name +
        ": the matching PodDisruptionBudget has minAvailable=" +
        string(variables.minAvailable) +
        " which equals the replica count=" +
        string(variables.replicaCount) +
        ". This may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains" +
        " and impact routine maintenance tasks."

        type(variables.matchingPdbs[0].spec.minAvailable) == int ?
        variables.matchingPdbs[0].spec.minAvailable : 0

    # Replica count with the same default-to-1 fallback as the original
    # precondition, used in both the validation expression and the message.
    - name: replicaCount
      expression: >-
        has(object.spec.replicas) ? object.spec.replicas : 1

  validations:
    # Mirrors original deny condition:
    #   key: "{{ request.object.spec.replicas }}"
    #   operator: Equals
    #   value: "{{ minavailable }}"
    #
    # The expression must evaluate to TRUE for the resource to be ALLOWED.
    # So the rule passes (no violation) when:
    #   - minAvailable == 0  → no applicable PDB was found
    #   - replicaCount != minAvailable → the values differ (safe configuration)
    - expression: >-
        variables.minAvailable == 0 || variables.replicaCount != variables.minAvailable
      # Static fallback message (used when messageExpression cannot be evaluated).
      message: >-
        The matching PodDisruptionBudget for this resource has its minAvailable
        value equal to the replica count which is not permitted.
      # Dynamic message with runtime values for easier diagnosis.
      messageExpression: >-
        object.kind + "/" + object.metadata.name +
        ": the matching PodDisruptionBudget has minAvailable=" +
        string(variables.minAvailable) +
        " which equals the replica count=" +
        string(variables.replicaCount) +
        ". This may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains" +
        " and impact routine maintenance tasks."
runs.raw_log:
Here is a summary of every conversion decision made:

---

### Key changes: `ClusterPolicy` → `ValidatingPolicy`

| Old (`kyverno.io/v1`) | New (`policies.kyverno.io/v1`) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` | New cluster-scoped type |
| `validationFailureAction: Audit` | `validationActions: [Audit]` | Same semantics |
| `background: false` | `evaluation.background.enabled: false` | Required — `resource.List()` makes live API calls unavailable during background scans |
| `match.any[].resources.kinds` + `preconditions` operation filter | `matchConstraints.resourceRules[].operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` | Operations moved to the resource rule |
| `preconditions` replicas > 0 (JMESPath) | `matchConditions` CEL boolean | `!has(object.spec.replicas) \|\| object.spec.replicas > 0` preserves the `\|\| 1` default from the original |
| `context[].apiCall.urlPath` + `jmesPath: label_match(...)` | `variables[].expression: resource.List(...)` + `.filter(...)` | CEL `resource.List("policy/v1", "poddisruptionbudgets", object.metadata.namespace)` replaces the raw URL call; CEL `.filter()` + `.all()` replaces `label_match` |
| JMESPath `\| [0] \| spec.minAvailable \|\| '0'` | `variables.matchingPdbs[0].spec.minAvailable` with `type() == int` guard | The `type()` check correctly skips percentage-string `minAvailable` values (e.g. `"50%"`), which cannot be meaningfully compared to an integer replica count |
| `validate.deny.conditions` (deny when equal) | `validations[].expression` must be **true to allow** | Logic is inverted: `minAvailable == 0 \|\| replicaCount != minAvailable` |
| No dynamic message | `messageExpression` | CEL string expression embeds runtime values for diagnosis |

description: capabilities permit privileged actions without giving full root access
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_drop_all.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy capabilities permit privileged actions without giving full root access. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_require_drop_all.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: drop-all-capabilities
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Drop All Capabilities
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Capabilities permit privileged actions without giving full root access. All
      capabilities should be dropped from a Pod, with only those required added back.
      This policy ensures that all containers explicitly specify the `drop: ["ALL"]`
      ability. Note that this policy also illustrates how to cover drop entries in any
      case although this may not strictly conform to the Pod Security Standards.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
    - name: require-drop-all
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Pod
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{ request.operation || 'BACKGROUND' }}"
          operator: NotEquals
          value: DELETE
      validate:
        message: >-
          Containers must drop `ALL` capabilities.
        foreach:
          - list: request.object.spec.[ephemeralContainers, initContainers, containers][]
            deny:
              conditions:
                all:
                - key: ALL
                  operator: AnyNotIn
                  value: "{{ element.securityContext.capabilities.drop[].to_upper(@) || `[]` }}"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: drop-all-capabilities
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Drop All Capabilities
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Capabilities permit privileged actions without giving full root access. All
      capabilities should be dropped from a Pod, with only those required added back.
      This policy ensures that all containers explicitly specify the `drop: ["ALL"]`
      ability. Note that this policy also illustrates how to cover drop entries in any
      case although this may not strictly conform to the Pod Security Standards.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["pods"]
  variables:
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers
        + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([])
        + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(container,
          container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?drop.orValue([]).exists(cap,
            cap.upperAscii() == 'ALL'
          )
        )
      message: >-
        Containers must drop `ALL` capabilities.
raw_log:
The converted policy is at `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every translation decision:

---

### Conversion mapping

| Old (`ClusterPolicy`) | New (`ValidatingPolicy`) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` | New API group |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` | Cluster-scoped by default |
| `validationFailureAction: Audit` | `validationActions: [Audit]` | Also supports `Deny`, `Warn` |
| `background: true` | `evaluation.background.enabled: true` | Explicit nested field |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` | Declarative match; `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` implicitly replaces the `preconditions` DELETE exclusion |
| JMESPath `preconditions` (skip DELETE) | Omit `DELETE` from `operations` | No JMESPath preconditions needed |
| `validate.foreach` over all container lists | `variables.allContainers` + CEL `.all()` macro | CEL list concatenation with safe-navigation `?` and `orValue([])` for optional fields |
| `to_upper(@)` / `AnyNotIn: ALL` (deny) | `cap.upperAscii() == 'ALL'` inside `.exists()` (assert) | Polarity flips: deny-condition → must-be-true assertion; `upperAscii()` preserves the original case-insensitive check |
| `policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0` | `policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0` | ValidatingPolicy requires Kyverno ≥ 1.16 |

runs.raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what changed and why:

---

## Conversion summary

| Old (`kyverno.io/v1` ClusterPolicy) | New (`policies.kyverno.io/v1` ValidatingPolicy) |
|---|---|
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `spec.background: true` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true` |
| `match.any[].resources.kinds: [Pod]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with explicit `apiGroups/apiVersions/resources` |
| `preconditions` excluding DELETE | `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` — DELETE naturally excluded |
| JMESPath `foreach` + `deny.conditions` | CEL `variables` + `validations.expression` |
| `to_upper(@)` (JMESPath) for case folding | `cap.upperAscii() == 'ALL'` (CEL) for case folding |

**Key structural changes:**

- **No more `rules` array** — ValidatingPolicy is a flat spec; match/validate live at the top level.
- **CEL replaces JMESPath** — The `foreach` + `deny` pattern becomes a single `all()` predicate over the `allContainers` variable.
- **Optional field access** — `container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?drop.orValue([])` safely handles missing fields without the `|| '[]'` JMESPath fallback.
- **Operations filter replaces preconditions** — Limiting `operations` to CREATE/UPDATE is idiomatic and replaces the `{{ request.operation || 'BACKGROUND' }} NotEquals DELETE` precondition.

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: drop-all-capabilities
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Drop All Capabilities
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: "1.16.0"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Capabilities permit privileged actions without giving full root access. All
      capabilities should be dropped from a Pod, with only those required added back.
      This policy ensures that all containers explicitly specify the `drop: ["ALL"]`
      ability. Note that this policy also illustrates how to cover drop entries in any
      case although this may not strictly conform to the Pod Security Standards.
spec:
  # Audit mirrors the original validationFailureAction: Audit
  validationActions:
    - Audit

  # Only intercept CREATE and UPDATE — this replaces the precondition that
  # excluded DELETE operations from the original policy.
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources:   ["pods"]

  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true

  variables:
    # Concatenate all container types, using optional chaining for init and
    # ephemeral containers since they may not be present on every Pod.
    # This replaces the foreach list:
    #   request.object.spec.[ephemeralContainers, initContainers, containers][]
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers +
        object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) +
        object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])

  validations:
    # The original foreach/deny fired when "ALL" was AnyNotIn the uppercased
    # drop list (i.e., no "ALL" entry present). A ValidatingPolicy expression
    # must return true to PASS, so the logic is inverted: require that every
    # container has at least one drop entry that uppercases to "ALL".
    # upperAscii() is used to match the original to_upper() case-normalisation.
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(container,
          container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?drop.orValue([]).exists(cap,
            cap.upperAscii() == 'ALL'
          )
        )
      message: "Containers must drop `ALL` capabilities."
runs.raw_log:
Here is a summary of every conversion decision made:

---

### What changed and why

| Original (`ClusterPolicy`) | Converted (`ValidatingPolicy`) | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` | New GA API group introduced in Kyverno 1.16/1.17 |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` | Dedicated validating-only kind; `ClusterPolicy` is deprecated |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` | Renamed field; accepts a list so `Deny` and `Audit` can coexist |
| `spec.background: true` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true` | Moved under the structured `evaluation` block |
| `match.any[].resources.kinds: [Pod]` | `matchConstraints.resourceRules[].resources: ["pods"]` | Flattened into a VAP-compatible `matchConstraints` structure |
| Precondition `operation != DELETE` | `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` in `resourceRules` | DELETE exclusion is expressed directly in the match rule |
| `validate.foreach` over all container lists | `variables.allContainers` (CEL concatenation) | CEL variables replace JMESPath context/foreach lists |
| `element.securityContext.capabilities.drop[].to_upper(@)` | `cap.upperAscii() == 'ALL'` | CEL's `upperAscii()` is the equivalent of JMESPath `to_upper()` |
| `deny` when `ALL` **AnyNotIn** drop list | `exists(cap, cap.upperAscii() == 'ALL')` — expression must return `true` to **pass** | Logic is inverted: deny-when-true becomes pass-when-true |

description: define and use labels that identify semantic attributes of your application or Deployment
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_labels.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy define and use labels that identify semantic attributes of your application or Deployment. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_require_labels.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Labels
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Label
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Define and use labels that identify semantic attributes of your application or Deployment.
      A common set of labels allows tools to work collaboratively, describing objects in a common manner that
      all tools can understand. The recommended labels describe applications in a way that can be
      queried. This policy validates that the label `app.kubernetes.io/name` is specified with some value.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: check-for-labels
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "The label `app.kubernetes.io/name` is required."
      pattern:
        metadata:
          labels:
            app.kubernetes.io/name: "?*"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-labels
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Labels
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: "1.16.0"
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Label
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Define and use labels that identify semantic attributes of your application or Deployment.
      A common set of labels allows tools to work collaboratively, describing objects in a common manner that
      all tools can understand. The recommended labels describe applications in a way that can be
      queried. This policy validates that the label `app.kubernetes.io/name` is specified with some value.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        'app.kubernetes.io/name' in object.metadata.?labels.orValue({}) &&
        object.metadata.labels['app.kubernetes.io/name'] != ''
      message: "The label `app.kubernetes.io/name` is required."
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
raw_log:
The converted policy is at `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every change made and why:

**API / Kind**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` — the new policy types live in a separate API group
- `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the dedicated validating-only type (still cluster-scoped)

**`spec.validationFailureAction`** → **`spec.validationActions`**
- `Audit` maps directly to `- Audit` in the new list field. (`Enforce` → `Deny` in the new API.)

**`spec.background: true`** → **`spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true`**
- Background scanning is now configured under `spec.evaluation`.

**`spec.rules[].match`** → **`spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules`**
- `kind: Pod` (core group) maps to `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: ["v1"]`, `resources: ["pods"]`, with explicit `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]`.

**`spec.rules[].validate.pattern`** → **`spec.validations[].expression`** (CEL)
- The Kyverno pattern `app.kubernetes.io/name: "?*"` (label must exist and be non-empty) becomes the CEL expression:
  ```
  'app.kubernetes.io/name' in object.metadata.?labels.orValue({}) &&
  object.metadata.labels['app.kubernetes.io/name'] != ''
  ```
  `?labels.orValue({})` safely handles Pods with no `labels` map at all (avoids a nil-dereference), then the second clause guards the non-empty requirement.

  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
  autogen:
    podControllers:
      controllers:
        - deployments
        - daemonsets
        - statefulsets

  validations:
    - message: "The label `app.kubernetes.io/name` is required."

        size(object.metadata.labels['app.kubernetes.io/name']) > 0
runs.raw_log:
Here's a summary of the conversion from `ClusterPolicy` (kyverno.io/v1) to `ValidatingPolicy` (policies.kyverno.io/v1):

**Key changes made:**

| Original (ClusterPolicy) | Converted (ValidatingPolicy) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` |
| `validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `background: true` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true` |
| `match.any[].resources.kinds: [Pod]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with `apiGroups: [""]`, `resources: ["pods"]`, and explicit `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` |
| `validate.pattern` (Kyverno anchors) | `spec.validations[].expression` using CEL |
| `policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0` | `policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0` |

**CEL expression** replaces the pattern `app.kubernetes.io/name: "?*"` (non-empty value check):
```cel
has(object.metadata.labels) &&
'app.kubernetes.io/name' in object.metadata.labels &&
size(object.metadata.labels['app.kubernetes.io/name']) > 0
```

**`autogen.podControllers`** was added to mirror Kyverno's original auto-generation behavior — the old `ClusterPolicy` engine automatically extended Pod rules to cover Deployments, StatefulSets, DaemonSets, Jobs, and CronJobs. In `ValidatingPolicy`, this must be declared explicitly.
