runs.raw_log:
Written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every conversion decision:

### Structural changes

| Original (ClusterPolicy) | Converted (ValidatingPolicy) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `spec.background: false` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: false` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Ingress]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with `apiGroups: [networking.k8s.io]`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, `resources: [ingresses]` |
| `spec.rules[].preconditions` (operation filter) | Folded into `matchConstraints.resourceRules.operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` |

### Rule consolidation

The three original rules collapse into **two `validations` entries**:

1. **Uniqueness check** (merges `check-single-host-create` + `check-single-host-update`): The `context[].apiCall` is replaced by a `resource.List()` variable. The UID filter that was only in the UPDATE rule now handles both cases: on CREATE `object.metadata.uid` is unset (`""`), so no existing ingress matches it and the full cluster list is used; on UPDATE the current ingress is excluded by its non-empty UID.

2. **Single-host check** (`deny-multiple-hosts`): `size(rules filtered to those with a host) <= 1` replaces the JMESPath precondition + `deny: {}`.

### Expression logic inversion

Kyverno `validate.deny.conditions` logic is inverted in CEL — a `validations` expression must return **`true` to pass**. The original "deny if any host is AnyIn existing hosts" becomes "all incoming hosts must NOT exist in any other ingress".

description: a Kubernetes Service of type NodePort uses a host port to receive traffic from any source
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_restrict_nodeport.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy a Kubernetes Service of type NodePort uses a host port to receive traffic from any source. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/cp_restrict_nodeport.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-nodeport
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow NodePort
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Service
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A Kubernetes Service of type NodePort uses a host port to receive traffic from
      any source. A NetworkPolicy cannot be used to control traffic to host ports.
      Although NodePort Services can be useful, their use must be limited to Services
      with additional upstream security checks. This policy validates that any new Services
      do not use the `NodePort` type.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-nodeport
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Service
    validate:
      message: "Services of type NodePort are not allowed."
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(type): "!NodePort"

raw_log:
The converted policy is at `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every translation made:

| Old (ClusterPolicy) | New (ValidatingPolicy) | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` | New CEL-native API group |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` | Cluster-scoped by default |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` | Renamed field, now a list |
| `spec.background: true` | *(omitted — default)* | Default is `true`; no need to repeat |
| `match.any[].resources.kinds: [Service]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` | CEL policies use Kubernetes admission-style match rules with explicit `apiGroups`, `apiVersions`, `operations`, and `resources` |
| `validate.pattern: spec.=(type): "!NodePort"` | `!has(object.spec.type) \|\| object.spec.type != 'NodePort'` | The `=()` pattern means "if present, must match". The CEL equivalent checks: if `type` is absent → pass (defaults to ClusterIP); if present → must not be `NodePort` |
| `validate.message` | `validations[].message` | Same string, moved to the validation entry |
| `minversion: 1.6.0` | `minversion: 1.16.0` | `ValidatingPolicy` requires Kyverno ≥ 1.16 |

| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` | New API group for 1.16+ policy types |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` | Dedicated validation-only type; cluster-scoped by default |
| `minversion: 1.6.0` | `minversion: 1.16.0` | ValidatingPolicy requires Kyverno ≥ 1.16 |
| `validationFailureAction: Audit` | `validationActions: [Audit]` | `Audit` → allows admission but logs violations to PolicyReports |
| `background: true` | `evaluation.background.enabled: true` | Equivalent field in the new spec structure |
| `match.any[].resources.kinds: [Service]` | `matchConstraints.resourceRules` with `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, `resources: [services]` | Kubernetes-native admission rule format; must specify group/version/resource explicitly |
| `operations` (implicit) | `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` | Explicit; DELETE/CONNECT excluded as irrelevant to this check |
| `validate.pattern: spec: =(type): "!NodePort"` | `expression: "!has(object.spec.type) \|\| object.spec.type != 'NodePort'"` | `=(field)` means "validate only if present" — CEL equivalent uses `!has()` guard so an absent `spec.type` (defaults to ClusterIP) passes; explicit NodePort is rejected |

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-nodeport
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow NodePort
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Service
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A Kubernetes Service of type NodePort uses a host port to receive traffic from
      any source. A NetworkPolicy cannot be used to control traffic to host ports.
      Although NodePort Services can be useful, their use must be limited to Services
      with additional upstream security checks. This policy validates that any new Services
      do not use the NodePort type.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - services
  validations:
    - expression: "!has(object.spec.type) || object.spec.type != 'NodePort'"
      message: "Services of type NodePort are not allowed."
runs.raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key changes from `ClusterPolicy` to `ValidatingPolicy`:

| Old (`kyverno.io/v1` ClusterPolicy) | New (`policies.kyverno.io/v1` ValidatingPolicy) |
|---|---|
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `spec.background: true` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: true` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds: [Service]` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` with explicit `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]`, `resources: [services]` |
| `validate.pattern.spec.=(type): "!NodePort"` (pattern matching) | `validations[].expression: "!has(object.spec.type) \|\| object.spec.type != 'NodePort'"` (CEL) |
| `validate.message` | `validations[].message` |

**Notable details:**
- The `=(type)` optional-field syntax from the old pattern is replaced with `!has(object.spec.type) || ...` in CEL to safely handle resources where `spec.type` may be absent.
- `validationActions` accepts a list (`Audit`, `Deny`, `Warn`) instead of the single `Audit`/`Enforce` string.
- Resource matching now requires explicit `apiGroups`, `apiVersions`, and `operations` — no `kinds` shorthand.
- The `policies.kyverno.io/minversion` annotation is dropped as it has no equivalent in the new type.

description: mutates Pods and Services to add the label 'foo: bar' to metadata.labels. The label must be added if absent and left unchanged if already present.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that mutates Pods and Services to add the label 'foo: bar' to metadata.labels. The label must be added if absent and left unchanged if already present. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/gen_add_default_labels.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-foo-bar-label
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        resources: ["pods", "services"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
  mutations:
    - patchType: JSONPatch
      jsonPatch:
        expression: |
          (has(object.metadata.labels) && has(object.metadata.labels.foo)) ?
            [] :
          !has(object.metadata.labels) ?
            [JSONPatch{op: "add", path: "/metadata/labels", value: {"foo": "bar"}}] :
            [JSONPatch{op: "add", path: "/metadata/labels/foo", value: "bar"}]
raw_log:
The policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's what it does and why each piece is structured this way:

**API version & kind** — `policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `MutatingPolicy` is the Kyverno 1.16+ CEL-native policy type (the legacy `kyverno.io/v1` / `ClusterPolicy` is deprecated).

**`matchConstraints.resourceRules`** — matches core-group (`""`) v1 `pods` and `services` on both `CREATE` and `UPDATE` admission events, so the label is enforced on new resources and re-applied on updates.

**`mutations[].patchType: JSONPatch`** — uses RFC 6902 JSON Patch via a CEL expression that returns a list of `JSONPatch` objects. The three-branch ternary precisely implements the "add if absent, leave unchanged if present" requirement:

| Condition | Patch applied |
|---|---|
| `foo` label already exists | `[]` — no-op, existing value preserved |
| Labels map exists, `foo` absent | `add /metadata/labels/foo = "bar"` |
| No labels map at all | `add /metadata/labels = {foo: bar}` (creates the whole map) |

The policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a breakdown of the key design decisions:

**API version**: `policies.kyverno.io/v1` — the stable GA version introduced in Kyverno 1.17 (1.16 had it as `v1alpha1`, but the task asks for 1.16+).

**`matchConstraints.resourceRules`**: Targets both `pods` and `services` in the core API group (`""`) on `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations.

**`mutations[].patchType: ApplyConfiguration`**: Uses a CEL expression with a ternary guard to implement true "add if absent" semantics:

- `!(has(object.metadata.labels) && has(object.metadata.labels.foo))` — checks whether the key `foo` is already present (handles both a missing `labels` map and a missing key within an existing map)
- If absent → returns an `Object{}` literal that merges `foo: bar` into `metadata.labels` without touching any other labels
- If already present → returns an empty `Object{}`, leaving the resource unchanged

This ensures the label is added if absent and left **completely unchanged** if it already exists — including preserving any non-`bar` value a caller may have explicitly set.

runs.raw_log:
Written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key decisions:

**API shape**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: MutatingPolicy` — the new dedicated policy type introduced in Kyverno 1.16, a superset of the Kubernetes-native `MutatingAdmissionPolicy`.

**Targeting both Pods and Services**
- Both are `core/v1` resources, so they fit in one `resourceRule` entry. The CEL expression only touches `metadata.labels`, which has an identical schema across all Kubernetes objects, so no schema-conflict issue arises.
- `operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]` ensures the label is enforced on both new and updated resources.

**Idempotent label addition**
- `patchType: ApplyConfiguration` uses **merge semantics** (similar to `kubectl apply`): fields in the expression are merged into the live object, so an existing `foo: bar` label is left exactly as-is, and the label is only inserted when absent. No explicit `preconditions` or `test` operations are needed.

description: generates a NetworkPolicy named 'default-deny' in any newly-created Namespace. The generated NetworkPolicy must select all Pods (empty podSelector), include both 'Ingress' and 'Egress' policyTypes, and define no ingress or egress rules so all traffic is denied by default.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ GeneratingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that generates a NetworkPolicy named 'default-deny' in any newly-created Namespace. The generated NetworkPolicy must select all Pods (empty podSelector), include both 'Ingress' and 'Egress' policyTypes, and define no ingress or egress rules so all traffic is denied by default. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/gen_create_networkpolicy.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-networkpolicy
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Default-Deny Network Policy
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Multi-Tenancy, Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: NetworkPolicy
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: "1.16.0"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      By default, Kubernetes allows communications across all Pods within a cluster.
      A default-deny NetworkPolicy should be configured for each Namespace to deny
      all ingress and egress traffic to Pods by default. This policy generates a
      NetworkPolicy named `default-deny` in every newly-created Namespace, selecting
      all Pods via an empty podSelector and specifying both Ingress and Egress
      policyTypes with no rules, effectively denying all traffic.
spec:
  evaluation:
    synchronize:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE"]
        resources: ["namespaces"]
  variables:
    - name: targetNs
      expression: "object.metadata.name"
    - name: downstream
      expression: >-
        [
          {
            "kind": dyn("NetworkPolicy"),
            "apiVersion": dyn("networking.k8s.io/v1"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name": "default-deny"
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "podSelector": dyn({}),
              "policyTypes": dyn(["Ingress", "Egress"])
            })
          }
        ]
  generate:
    - expression: generator.Apply(variables.targetNs, variables.downstream)
raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a breakdown of the key design decisions based on the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation:

**API & Kind**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` — the stable v1 API promoted in Kyverno 1.16 (previously `v1alpha1`)
- `kind: GeneratingPolicy` — the new CEL-based generator type (replaces the `generate:` rule in `ClusterPolicy`)

**`spec.matchConstraints`**
- Watches the core API group (`""`) for `namespaces` resources on `CREATE` only, so it fires exactly when a new Namespace is admitted

**`spec.evaluation.synchronize`**
- `enabled: true` — Kyverno will keep the generated `NetworkPolicy` in sync with the policy definition; if someone deletes or mutates it, Kyverno recreates/restores it

**`spec.variables`**
- `targetNs` — extracts `object.metadata.name` (the new namespace's name) via CEL; passed to `generator.Apply()` so the NetworkPolicy is created in the right namespace
- `downstream` — a CEL list of resource descriptors using `dyn()` to produce typed Kubernetes resource maps; defines the `NetworkPolicy` with:
  - `podSelector: {}` — selects all Pods
  - `policyTypes: ["Ingress", "Egress"]` — activates both directions
  - No `ingress` or `egress` rule arrays — results in a complete deny of all traffic

**`spec.generate`**
- `generator.Apply(variables.targetNs, variables.downstream)` — the built-in CEL generator function that creates the listed resources in the target namespace

runs.raw_log:
Written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's what each section does and why:

**`matchConstraints.resourceRules`** — triggers on `CREATE` of any `namespaces` resource (core API group `""`). This fires whenever a new Namespace is admitted.

**`variables`**
- `nsName` — extracts the created Namespace's name from the admission object via CEL (`object.metadata.name`), used to target the generated resource into the correct namespace.
- `networkPolicies` — a CEL list expression defining the NetworkPolicy to generate. `dyn()` is required for type coercion in CEL expressions; without it Kyverno's type checker rejects the map literals.

**`generate`** — calls `generator.Apply(namespace, resourceList)` to materialize the resources. Kyverno's built-in `generator` function creates or updates the listed objects in the target namespace.

**NetworkPolicy spec**
- `podSelector: {}` — empty selector matches all Pods in the namespace.
- `policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]` — declares both directions are governed.
- No `ingress` or `egress` keys — omitting them entirely (rather than setting `[]`) means zero rules, so all matching traffic is denied by default.

**`evaluation.synchronize.enabled: true`** — keeps the generated NetworkPolicy in sync if the policy is updated or if someone deletes the NetworkPolicy manually (Kyverno will recreate it).

**`evaluation.generateExisting.enabled: true`** — also applies the policy to Namespaces that already exist at the time the policy is installed.

      Generates a default-deny NetworkPolicy named 'default-deny' in every

- `podSelector: {}` — selects all Pods in the namespace
- `policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]` — enforces both directions
- No `ingress` or `egress` rule stanzas — omitting them entirely (not `[]`) produces a true deny-all for both directions

**Lifecycle settings (`evaluation`)**
- `synchronize.enabled: true` — Kyverno keeps the generated NetworkPolicy in sync if the policy changes; recreates it if manually deleted
- `generateExisting.enabled: false` — does not backfill pre-existing namespaces (set `true` to backfill)
- `orphanDownstreamOnPolicyDelete.enabled: false` — generated NetworkPolicies are removed if this policy is deleted

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers add any Linux capability via securityContext.capabilities.add beyond the Pod Security Standards baseline allow-list (AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT).
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers add any Linux capability via securityContext.capabilities.add beyond the Pod Security Standards baseline allow-list (AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT). Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/gen_disallow_capabilities.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-capabilities-beyond-baseline
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Capabilities Beyond PSS Baseline
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: "1.16.0"
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.26+"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Deployment,CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Adding capabilities beyond the Pod Security Standards (PSS) baseline
      allow-list is disallowed. This policy denies Pods, Deployments, and
      CronJobs whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers
      specify securityContext.capabilities.add with any capability not in the
      PSS baseline set: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID,
      KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID,
      SYS_CHROOT.
spec:
  # Deny the admission request outright; change to [Audit] to warn only.
  validationActions:
    - Deny

  evaluation:
    admission:
      enabled: true   # enforced at admission time
    background:
      enabled: true   # periodic re-evaluation of existing resources

  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      # Plain Pods
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
      # Deployments (containers live under spec.template.spec)
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      # CronJobs (containers live under spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec)
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs

  variables:
    # PSS baseline capability allow-list (verbatim from the specification).
    - name: allowedCapabilities
      expression: >-
        ['AUDIT_WRITE', 'CHOWN', 'DAC_OVERRIDE', 'FOWNER', 'FSETID',
         'KILL', 'MKNOD', 'NET_BIND_SERVICE', 'SETFCAP', 'SETGID',
         'SETPCAP', 'SETUID', 'SYS_CHROOT']

    # Resolve the pod spec regardless of resource kind:
    #   Pod      → object.spec
    #   CronJob  → object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
    #   Deployment (and other pod-template resources) → object.spec.template.spec
    - name: podSpec
      expression: >-
        object.kind == 'Pod'
          ? object.spec
          : object.kind == 'CronJob'
            ? object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
            : object.spec.template.spec

    # Merge containers + initContainers + ephemeralContainers into one list.
    # The ?field.orValue([]) pattern safely handles absent optional fields.
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        variables.podSpec.containers
          + variables.podSpec.?initContainers.orValue([])
          + variables.podSpec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])

  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(container,
          container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).all(
            cap,
            cap == '' || cap in variables.allowedCapabilities
          )
        )
      message: >-
        One or more containers add a Linux capability that is not on the PSS
        baseline allow-list. Permitted capabilities are: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN,
        DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP,
        SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT.

The policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a breakdown of every design decision:

---

## Policy design notes

### API & kind
`apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the Kyverno 1.16+ CEL-native policy type (distinct from the older `kyverno.io/v1` `ClusterPolicy`).

### `validationActions: [Deny]`
Rejects the admission request immediately. Swap to `[Audit]` to log-only while rolling out.

### `evaluation`
Both `admission` (request-time webhook) and `background` (periodic cluster scan) are enabled so existing resources are also flagged.

### `matchConstraints.resourceRules`
Three entries cover the three requested resource kinds with their correct API groups:
| Kind | apiGroup | path to containers |
|---|---|---|
| `Pod` | `""` (core) | `spec` |
| `Deployment` | `apps` | `spec.template.spec` |
| `CronJob` | `batch` | `spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec` |

### `variables`

| Variable | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `allowedCapabilities` | The 13-capability PSS baseline list as a CEL list literal |
| `podSpec` | CEL ternary that resolves to the correct `PodSpec` depth for each resource kind using `object.kind` |
| `allContainers` | Concatenates `containers` + optional `initContainers` + optional `ephemeralContainers` into a single iterable list |

### `validations` expression
```cel
variables.allContainers.all(container,
  container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).all(
    cap,
    cap == '' || cap in variables.allowedCapabilities
  )
)
```
- `?field.orValue([])` — CEL optional-field chaining; safely returns `[]` when any intermediate field is absent, so containers with no `securityContext` pass cleanly.
- `cap == ''` — guards against empty-string entries that some tools inject.
- `.all(...)` — the admission is denied if **any** container in **any** of the three container lists carries a capability outside the allow-list.

runs.yaml_preview:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-capabilities-beyond-baseline
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Capabilities Beyond PSS Baseline
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: "1.16.0"
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.30+"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Deployment,CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Adding capabilities beyond those listed in the Pod Security Standards
      baseline allow-list (AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID,
      KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID,
      SYS_CHROOT) is disallowed. This policy checks containers,
      initContainers, and ephemeralContainers across Pods, Deployments, and
      CronJobs.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["pods"]
      - apiGroups:   ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["deployments"]
      - apiGroups:   ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["cronjobs"]
  variables:
    # The full PSS baseline capability allow-list.
    - name: allowedCapabilities
      expression: >-
        [
          'AUDIT_WRITE', 'CHOWN', 'DAC_OVERRIDE', 'FOWNER', 'FSETID',
          'KILL', 'MKNOD', 'NET_BIND_SERVICE', 'SETFCAP', 'SETGID',
          'SETPCAP', 'SETUID', 'SYS_CHROOT'
        ]
    # Resolve the PodSpec regardless of the enclosing resource kind:
    #   Pod        → object.spec
    #   Deployment → object.spec.template.spec
    #   CronJob    → object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
    # CEL short-circuits ternary conditions, so the deeper paths are only
    # evaluated when object.kind matches the expected value.
    - name: podSpec
      expression: >-
        object.kind == 'CronJob'
          ? object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
          : object.kind == 'Deployment'
            ? object.spec.template.spec
            : object.spec
    # Aggregate containers, initContainers, and ephemeralContainers into a
    # single list. The '?' optional-field operator + orValue([]) guards
    # against resources that omit the optional container lists.
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        variables.podSpec.containers
          + variables.podSpec.?initContainers.orValue([])
          + variables.podSpec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(container,
          container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).all(cap,
            cap == '' || cap in variables.allowedCapabilities
          )
        )
      # Static fallback message (used when messageExpression is absent or errors).
      message: >-
        One or more containers add Linux capabilities beyond the Pod Security
        Standards baseline allow-list. Permitted capabilities are:
        AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD,
        NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT.
      # Dynamic message that names the offending containers and capabilities.
      messageExpression: >-
        "The following containers in " + object.kind + " '" + object.metadata.name +
        "' add disallowed Linux capabilities — " +
        variables.allContainers
          .filter(c,
            c.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).exists(cap,
              cap != '' && !(cap in variables.allowedCapabilities)
            )
          )
          .map(c,
            c.name + ": [" +
            c.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([])
              .filter(cap, cap != '' && !(cap in variables.allowedCapabilities))
              .join(", ") +
            "]"
          )
          .join("; ") +
        ". Permitted capabilities: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER," +
        " FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP," +
        " SETUID, SYS_CHROOT."

Written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a breakdown of every decision made:

---

## Policy design

**API / kind**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` — the stable API promoted in Kyverno 1.17; compatible with 1.16+ (the research confirmed v1alpha1 is the file-level version in the kyverno-policies repo, but `v1` is the GA-stable form the docs prescribe for 1.16+).
- `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — cluster-scoped; covers all namespaces automatically.

**`matchConstraints.resourceRules`**
Three entries targeting exactly the three resource types requested:
| Group | Resource | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `""` (core) | `pods` | Direct Pod submissions |
| `apps` | `deployments` | Deployment controller |
| `batch` | `cronjobs` | CronJob controller |

**`variables` — three named CEL expressions evaluated in order**

1. **`allowedCapabilities`** — the 13-item PSS baseline list, defined once and reused.

2. **`podSpec`** — a ternary chain that resolves the PodSpec at the correct path for each resource kind. CEL short-circuits ternary conditions at runtime, so accessing `object.spec.jobTemplate` is only evaluated when `object.kind == 'CronJob'`.
   ```
   CronJob    → object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
   Deployment → object.spec.template.spec
   Pod        → object.spec
   ```

3. **`allContainers`** — concatenates all three container lists. The `?` optional-field operator + `.orValue([])` guards against objects that omit `initContainers` or `ephemeralContainers` (both are optional in the Kubernetes schema).

**`validations`**
- **`expression`**: `all()` iterates every container; `?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([])` safely traverses the optional chain; `cap == '' || cap in variables.allowedCapabilities` passes empty strings (no-op) and any capability in the allow-list.
- **`message`**: static fallback used if `messageExpression` errors.
- **`messageExpression`**: dynamic message that names each offending container and the specific disallowed capabilities it tries to add — makes triage much faster than a generic deny.

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-capabilities-baseline
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Linux Capabilities to PSS Baseline
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Deployment,CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs whose containers, initContainers,
      or ephemeralContainers add any Linux capability beyond the Pod Security
      Standards (PSS) baseline allow-list (AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE,
      FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP,
      SETUID, SYS_CHROOT).
spec:
  # Deny the admission request when any validation fails.
  validationActions:
    - Deny

  # Also evaluate existing resources in the background (for policy reports).
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true

  # Match Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs on create and update.
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["pods"]
      - apiGroups:   ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["deployments"]
      - apiGroups:   ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["cronjobs"]

  variables:
    # The complete PSS baseline capability allow-list.
    - name: allowedCaps
      expression: >-
        [
          'AUDIT_WRITE',
          'CHOWN',
          'DAC_OVERRIDE',
          'FOWNER',
          'FSETID',
          'KILL',
          'MKNOD',
          'NET_BIND_SERVICE',
          'SETFCAP',
          'SETGID',
          'SETPCAP',
          'SETUID',
          'SYS_CHROOT'
        ]

    # Normalise the three resource kinds to a single pod-spec object so that
    # subsequent variables do not need to branch on the resource kind.
    #   Pod        → object.spec
    #   Deployment → object.spec.template.spec
    #   CronJob    → object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
    - name: podSpec
      expression: >-
        object.kind == 'Pod'        ? object.spec :
        object.kind == 'Deployment' ? object.spec.template.spec :
        object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec

    # Combine all three container arrays into one flat list.
    # initContainers and ephemeralContainers are optional fields, so we use
    # the CEL optional-field accessor (?.) and fall back to an empty list.
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        variables.podSpec.containers
        + variables.podSpec.?initContainers.orValue([])
        + variables.podSpec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])

    # For every container, collect the capabilities listed under
    # securityContext.capabilities.add (defaulting to [] when absent),
    # flatten the resulting list-of-lists into a single list, then
    # keep only those capabilities that are NOT in the allow-list.
    - name: violatingCaps
      expression: >-
        variables.allContainers
          .map(c, c.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]))
          .flatten()
          .filter(cap, !(cap in variables.allowedCaps))

  validations:
    # The request is allowed only when no container adds a capability outside
    # the baseline allow-list.  When the check fails, the dynamic
    # messageExpression reports exactly which capabilities triggered the denial.
    - expression: "variables.violatingCaps.size() == 0"
      messageExpression: >-
        'Adding Linux capabilities beyond the PSS baseline is not permitted. ' +
        'Disallowed capability/capabilities: [' + variables.violatingCaps.join(', ') + ']. ' +
        'Permitted capabilities: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, ' +
        'MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT.'

Written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a breakdown of every design decision:

---

## Policy structure

| Field | Choice | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion` | `policies.kyverno.io/v1` | The new Kyverno 1.16+ API (not the legacy `kyverno.io/v1` ClusterPolicy) |
| `validationActions` | `[Deny]` | Hard-blocks the request; change to `[Audit]` for observe-only mode |
| `evaluation.background.enabled` | `true` | Also scans existing resources for policy reports |
| `matchConstraints.resourceRules` | Three entries (Pods, Deployments, CronJobs) | Each resource lives under a different API group and requires separate rules |

---

## CEL variable pipeline

```
allowedCaps          → string list of the 13 PSS baseline caps
podSpec              → ternary on object.kind to normalise all 3 shapes to one pod spec
allContainers        → containers + ?initContainers + ?ephemeralContainers
violatingCaps        → map → flatten → filter to find out-of-list caps
```

**Key CEL idioms used:**

- `object.kind == 'Pod' ? ... : ...` — ternary to handle the three different container-path shapes (`spec`, `spec.template.spec`, `spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec`)
- `?.field.orValue([])` — safe optional-field access so the policy handles containers that omit `initContainers` or `ephemeralContainers` entirely
- `.map(...).flatten()` — collect per-container capability lists then flatten to a single list (Kyverno CEL extension)
- `.filter(cap, !(cap in variables.allowedCaps))` — keep only disallowed caps
- `messageExpression` — dynamic denial message that names the exact offending capabilities so operators know what to fix

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that set spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, or spec.hostIPC to true. All three host namespace fields must be checked.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that set spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, or spec.hostIPC to true. All three host namespace fields must be checked. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/gen_disallow_host_namespaces.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-host-namespaces
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Host Namespaces
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Deployment, CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Sharing host namespaces (hostNetwork, hostPID, hostIPC) grants containers
      direct access to host-level resources and is a well-known privilege
      escalation vector. This policy denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs
      that set spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, or spec.hostIPC to true.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
      - apiGroups: ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["cronjobs"]
  validations:
    # --- Pods: check spec directly ---
    - message: >-
        Pods must not enable host namespaces. spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID,
        and spec.hostIPC must be unset or false.
      expression: >-
        object.kind != 'Pod' || (
          !object.spec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false) &&
          !object.spec.?hostPID.orValue(false) &&
          !object.spec.?hostIPC.orValue(false)
        )

    # --- Deployments: check spec.template.spec ---
    - message: >-
        Deployments must not enable host namespaces in the pod template.
        spec.template.spec.hostNetwork, spec.template.spec.hostPID, and
        spec.template.spec.hostIPC must be unset or false.
      expression: >-
        object.kind != 'Deployment' || (
          !object.spec.template.spec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false) &&
          !object.spec.template.spec.?hostPID.orValue(false) &&
          !object.spec.template.spec.?hostIPC.orValue(false)
        )

    # --- CronJobs: check spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec ---
    - message: >-
        CronJobs must not enable host namespaces in the pod template.
        spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.hostNetwork,
        spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.hostPID, and
        spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.hostIPC must be unset or false.
      expression: >-
        object.kind != 'CronJob' || (
          !object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false) &&
          !object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?hostPID.orValue(false) &&
          !object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?hostIPC.orValue(false)
        )

**API & Kind**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the Kyverno 1.16+ native policy type (extends Kubernetes `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy`).

**`matchConstraints.resourceRules`** — three separate rules covering:
| Resource | apiGroup | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `pods` | `""` (core) | |
| `deployments` | `apps` | |
| `cronjobs` | `batch` | not `apps` |

**`validations`** — three separate entries, one per resource kind, each guarded by `object.kind != 'X'` so it trivially passes for the other two kinds. This pattern gives a precise, resource-specific error message and avoids a complex nested ternary.

**CEL optional-field access** — `object.spec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false)` safely handles the case where the field is absent (treated as `false`), which is the correct Kubernetes default for all three fields.

**Spec paths per resource type:**
- Pod → `object.spec.{hostNetwork,hostPID,hostIPC}`
- Deployment → `object.spec.template.spec.{…}`
- CronJob → `object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.{…}`

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-host-namespaces
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Host Namespaces
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security - Baseline
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Host namespaces (hostNetwork, hostPID, hostIPC) allow containers to share
      the host's network stack, process tree, and IPC resources respectively.
      Enabling them bypasses namespace isolation and can expose the underlying
      node to privilege escalation. This policy denies Pods, Deployments, and
      CronJobs that set any of these fields to true.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: ["pods"]
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: ["deployments"]
      - apiGroups: ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: ["cronjobs"]
  # Extract the pod spec regardless of the enclosing resource type:
  #   CronJob  → spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
  #   Deployment → spec.template.spec
  #   Pod        → spec
  variables:
    - name: podSpec
      expression: >-
        has(object.spec.jobTemplate)
          ? object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
          : has(object.spec.template)
            ? object.spec.template.spec
            : object.spec
  validations:
    - expression: "variables.podSpec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false) == false"
      message: "Setting spec.hostNetwork to true is not allowed."
    - expression: "variables.podSpec.?hostPID.orValue(false) == false"
      message: "Setting spec.hostPID to true is not allowed."
    - expression: "variables.podSpec.?hostIPC.orValue(false) == false"
      message: "Setting spec.hostIPC to true is not allowed."

Written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a breakdown of every decision made:

---

## Policy design

**`apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy`**  
The Kyverno 1.16+ cluster-scoped policy type, a superset of Kubernetes `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy` with extended CEL and autogen support.

**`validationActions: [Deny]`**  
Hard-deny at admission time; no audit-only mode.

**`matchConstraints.resourceRules`**  
Three separate rules cover all target kinds and their correct API groups:

| Resource | apiGroup | apiVersion |
|---|---|---|
| `pods` | `""` (core) | `v1` |
| `deployments` | `apps` | `v1` |
| `cronjobs` | `batch` | `v1` |

Both `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations are included so an existing resource cannot be patched to re-enable a host namespace.

**`variables.podSpec`**  
The three resource kinds nest the pod spec at different paths. A single CEL variable resolves the right location at evaluation time:

```
CronJob   → spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
Deployment → spec.template.spec
Pod        → spec
```

**`validations`**  
Each of the three host-namespace fields gets its own rule with a distinct message so operators know exactly which field triggered the denial:

- `variables.podSpec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false) == false`
- `variables.podSpec.?hostPID.orValue(false) == false`
- `variables.podSpec.?hostIPC.orValue(false) == false`

`?field.orValue(false)` uses CEL's optional-field accessor — if the field is absent (the common case) it defaults to `false`, so resources that omit these fields are not incorrectly denied.

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-host-namespaces
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Host Namespaces
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Deployment,CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Host namespaces (hostNetwork, hostPID, hostIPC) allow access to shared
      host-level resources and can be used to escalate privileges. Pods,
      Deployments, and CronJobs must not enable any host namespace sharing.
      The fields spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, and spec.hostIPC must each
      be unset or explicitly set to false.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: ['pods']
      - apiGroups: ['apps']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: ['deployments']
      - apiGroups: ['batch']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: ['cronjobs']
  # Extract the PodSpec from whichever resource type is being evaluated.
  # - Pod:        spec
  # - Deployment: spec.template.spec
  # - CronJob:    spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
  variables:
    - name: podSpec
      expression: >-
        object.kind == "Pod" ? object.spec :
        object.kind == "Deployment" ? object.spec.template.spec :
        object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
  validations:
    - expression: "!variables.podSpec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false)"
      message: >-
        Sharing the host network namespace is disallowed. The field
        spec.hostNetwork must be unset or set to false.
    - expression: "!variables.podSpec.?hostPID.orValue(false)"
      message: >-
        Sharing the host PID namespace is disallowed. The field
        spec.hostPID must be unset or set to false.
    - expression: "!variables.podSpec.?hostIPC.orValue(false)"
      message: >-
        Sharing the host IPC namespace is disallowed. The field
        spec.hostIPC must be unset or set to false.

The policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the design decisions based on the Kyverno 1.16+ docs:

**API**: `policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `ValidatingPolicy` — the new CEL-based policy type introduced in Kyverno 1.13+ and stable in 1.16+.

**`matchConstraints.resourceRules`**: Three explicit entries covering all three resource kinds with their correct API groups:
- Pods → `apiGroups: ['']` (core group)
- Deployments → `apiGroups: ['apps']`
- CronJobs → `apiGroups: ['batch']`

**`variables.podSpec`**: A CEL variable that normalizes the pod spec path across all three resource types using a ternary chain. This avoids duplicating the three field checks per resource kind:
- Pod → `object.spec`
- Deployment → `object.spec.template.spec`
- CronJob → `object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec`

**`validations`**: Three separate validation rules, one per field, using CEL optional-field access (`.?hostNetwork.orValue(false)`) to safely handle the common case where the field is simply absent from the manifest (which is equivalent to `false` in Kubernetes). The `!` negation means the expression returns `true` (passes) when the field is absent or `false`, and returns `false` (triggers Deny) when the field is `true`.

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that use any hostPath volume. The check must reject any volume in spec.volumes whose hostPath field is set, regardless of the hostPath value.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that use any hostPath volume. The check must reject any volume in spec.volumes whose hostPath field is set, regardless of the hostPath value. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/gen_disallow_host_path.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-hostpath-volumes
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow hostPath Volumes
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that mount hostPath volumes expose the
      host node filesystem to containers. This policy denies any resource whose
      spec.volumes list contains an entry with a hostPath field set, regardless
      of the hostPath value.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
      - apiGroups: ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["cronjobs"]
  validations:
    - message: "hostPath volumes are not allowed"
      expression: >-
        (!has(object.spec.volumes) ||
          object.spec.volumes.all(v, !has(v.hostPath))) &&
        (!has(object.spec.template) ||
          !has(object.spec.template.spec) ||
          !has(object.spec.template.spec.volumes) ||
          object.spec.template.spec.volumes.all(v, !has(v.hostPath))) &&
        (!has(object.spec.jobTemplate) ||
          !has(object.spec.jobTemplate.spec) ||
          !has(object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template) ||
          !has(object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec) ||
          !has(object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes) ||
          object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes.all(v, !has(v.hostPath)))

The policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's what it does and why each decision was made:

---

**API / kind**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the stable GA API introduced in Kyverno 1.17 (promoted from `v1beta1` in 1.16).

**`matchConstraints.resourceRules`**
Three explicit rules cover each resource kind and its API group:

| Resource | apiGroup | apiVersion |
|---|---|---|
| Pod | `""` (core) | `v1` |
| Deployment | `apps` | `v1` |
| CronJob | `batch` | `v1` |

Both `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations are matched so re-applied manifests can't sneak a hostPath in.

**CEL `validations.expression`**
A single expression handles all three resource shapes by guarding each path with `has()` before descending:

| Resource | Volumes path checked |
|---|---|
| Pod | `spec.volumes` |
| Deployment | `spec.template.spec.volumes` |
| CronJob | `spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes` |

For each path, `.all(v, !has(v.hostPath))` iterates every volume entry and rejects any where the `hostPath` field is present — regardless of the path value (even an empty string). The outer `!has(...)` guards make every leg vacuously true when the field doesn't exist, so a Pod rule never trips the Deployment/CronJob checks and vice-versa.

  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow hostPath Volumes
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Volume
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      HostPath volumes allow containers to mount host filesystem paths directly,
      enabling privilege escalation and access to sensitive host data. This
      policy denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that define any volume with
      the hostPath field set, regardless of the path value.
spec:
  # Deny matching resources immediately at admission time.
  validationActions:
    - Deny

  # Base rule: match Pods in the core API group.
  # autogen (below) extends this to Deployments and CronJobs automatically.
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations:  [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources:   ['pods']

  # Kyverno 1.16+ autogen rewrites the CEL expressions for each controller:
  #   Deployment  → object.spec.template.spec.?volumes
  #   CronJob     → object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?volumes
  # It also injects the corresponding matchConstraints entries for those kinds.
  autogen:
    podControllers:
      controllers:
        - deployments
        - cronjobs

  validations:
    - message: >-
        HostPath volumes are forbidden. The field spec.volumes[*].hostPath
        must be unset.
      # object.spec.?volumes   — safely access volumes (nil-safe optional chaining)
      # .orValue([])           — treat a missing volumes field as an empty list
      # .exists(v, has(v.hostPath)) — true if any volume has the hostPath field set
      # !                      — deny when ANY volume carries a hostPath
      expression: >-
        !object.spec.?volumes.orValue([]).exists(v, has(v.hostPath))
runs.raw_log:
The policy is at `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a breakdown of every decision:

---

### Key design choices

**`apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy`**
The new Kyverno 1.16+ API, not the legacy `kyverno.io/v1 ClusterPolicy`.

**`validationActions: [Deny]`**
Hard deny at admission time — no audit-only mode.

**`matchConstraints` targets only Pods**
The `autogen` block takes care of the other two kinds. Targeting all three manually in `matchConstraints` and writing a single CEL expression that works for all three paths would require brittle `object.kind`-based branching. Autogen is the idiomatic 1.16+ approach.

**`autogen.podControllers.controllers: [deployments, cronjobs]`**
Kyverno's autogen engine (`pkg/cel/autogen/data.go`) rewrites the CEL expression and injects new `matchConstraints` entries:
- **Deployments** → replaces `object.spec.` with `object.spec.template.spec.`
- **CronJobs** → replaces `object.spec.` with `object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.`

**CEL expression**
```
!object.spec.?volumes.orValue([]).exists(v, has(v.hostPath))
```
| Part | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `object.spec.?volumes` | Nil-safe optional access — no error if `volumes` is absent |
| `.orValue([])` | Treat missing `volumes` as empty list |
| `.exists(v, has(v.hostPath))` | True if **any** volume has the `hostPath` field set (regardless of its value) |
| `!` | Validation expression must return `true` to pass — negate to deny when hostPath is present |

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-host-path
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow hostPath Volumes
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The hostPath volume type allows Pods to mount directories or files
      directly from the host node's filesystem, breaking container isolation
      and potentially exposing sensitive host data. This policy denies any
      Pod, Deployment, or CronJob that defines a volume with the hostPath
      field set, regardless of the path value.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: ["pods"]
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: ["deployments"]
      - apiGroups: ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: ["cronjobs"]
  validations:
    - message: >-
        hostPath volumes are not permitted. Remove all volumes whose
        hostPath field is set (spec.volumes[*].hostPath must be unset).
      expression: >-
        (object.kind != "Pod" ||
          !has(object.spec.volumes) ||
          object.spec.volumes.all(v, !has(v.hostPath))) &&
        (object.kind != "Deployment" ||
          !has(object.spec.template.spec.volumes) ||
          object.spec.template.spec.volumes.all(v, !has(v.hostPath))) &&
        (object.kind != "CronJob" ||
          !has(object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes) ||
          object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes.all(v, !has(v.hostPath)))

Written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a breakdown of the design choices:

---

## Policy design

**API & kind** — `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy`, the stable CEL-based policy API introduced in Kyverno 1.16.

**`matchConstraints.resourceRules`** — Three explicit entries cover all three resource types across their correct API groups:
| Resource | apiGroup | apiVersion |
|---|---|---|
| Pod | `""` (core) | `v1` |
| Deployment | `apps` | `v1` |
| CronJob | `batch` | `v1` |

Both `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations are intercepted so already-deployed resources can't be updated to add a hostPath.

**CEL expression** — A single `validations` entry with one expression that dispatches by `object.kind`:

```cel
(object.kind != "Pod" ||
  !has(object.spec.volumes) ||
  object.spec.volumes.all(v, !has(v.hostPath))) &&
(object.kind != "Deployment" ||
  !has(object.spec.template.spec.volumes) ||
  object.spec.template.spec.volumes.all(v, !has(v.hostPath))) &&
(object.kind != "CronJob" ||
  !has(object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes) ||
  object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes.all(v, !has(v.hostPath)))
```

Each branch short-circuits to `true` when `object.kind` doesn't match, so the wrong-path checks are never evaluated. Within each branch:
- `!has(object.spec.…volumes)` — passes cleanly when the volumes list is absent entirely.
- `.all(v, !has(v.hostPath))` — the CEL `all()` macro iterates every volume and ensures none has the `hostPath` field present, catching it regardless of the path value (including empty string or `/`).

description: denies any Pod, Deployment, or CronJob whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers set securityContext.privileged to true.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies any Pod, Deployment, or CronJob whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers set securityContext.privileged to true. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/gen_disallow_privileged.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-privileged-containers
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Privileged Containers
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Deployment, CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Privileged mode grants a container nearly all the same access as root on
      the host node, bypassing most kernel security mechanisms. This policy
      denies any Pod, Deployment, or CronJob whose containers,
      initContainers, or ephemeralContainers set
      securityContext.privileged to true.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
      - apiGroups: ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["cronjobs"]
  variables:
    # Resolve the pod spec regardless of the enclosing resource kind.
    # - Pod:        spec
    # - Deployment: spec.template.spec
    # - CronJob:    spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
    - name: podSpec
      expression: >
        object.kind == 'Pod'        ? object.spec :
        object.kind == 'Deployment' ? object.spec.template.spec :
                                      object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
    # Flatten containers, initContainers, and ephemeralContainers into one list.
    # Use the optional-chaining accessor (?.) so missing fields default to [].
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >
        variables.podSpec.containers
          + variables.podSpec.?initContainers.orValue([])
          + variables.podSpec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - message: >-
        Privileged containers are not allowed. Remove or set
        securityContext.privileged to false for every container,
        initContainer, and ephemeralContainer.
      expression: >
        variables.allContainers.all(c,
          !has(c.securityContext) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.privileged) ||
          c.securityContext.privileged == false
        )
raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key design decisions:

**API / kind**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the new CEL-native policy type introduced in Kyverno 1.13 and stabilised in 1.16, distinct from the older `kyverno.io/v1 ClusterPolicy`.

**Resource matching**
Three `resourceRules` entries cover all three resource kinds across their respective API groups (`""` for Pod, `apps` for Deployment, `batch` for CronJob), on both `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations.

**`podSpec` variable**
A ternary CEL expression resolves the right pod-spec path for each kind:
- Pod → `object.spec`
- Deployment → `object.spec.template.spec`
- CronJob → `object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec`

**`allContainers` variable**
Concatenates all three container lists into one, using the optional-chaining accessor (`.?field.orValue([])`) so the expression is safe even when `initContainers` or `ephemeralContainers` are absent.

**Validation expression**
`has()` macros guard each access level so the expression never panics on a missing `securityContext` or missing `privileged` field — a container passes only if `privileged` is absent or explicitly `false`.

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-privileged-containers
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Privileged Containers
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Deployment,CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Privileged mode disables most security mechanisms and must not be allowed.
      This policy ensures that Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs do not set
      securityContext.privileged to true on any containers, initContainers,
      or ephemeralContainers.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
      - apiGroups: ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["cronjobs"]
  variables:
    # Normalize the pod spec path across resource kinds:
    #   Pod        -> spec
    #   Deployment -> spec.template.spec
    #   CronJob    -> spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
    - name: podSpec
      expression: >-
        object.kind == 'Pod' ? object.spec :
        object.kind == 'Deployment' ? object.spec.template.spec :
        object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        (!has(variables.podSpec.containers) ||
          variables.podSpec.containers.all(c,
            !has(c.securityContext) ||
            !has(c.securityContext.privileged) ||
            c.securityContext.privileged == false)) &&
        (!has(variables.podSpec.initContainers) ||
          variables.podSpec.initContainers.all(c,
            !has(c.securityContext) ||
            !has(c.securityContext.privileged) ||
            c.securityContext.privileged == false)) &&
        (!has(variables.podSpec.ephemeralContainers) ||
          variables.podSpec.ephemeralContainers.all(c,
            !has(c.securityContext) ||
            !has(c.securityContext.privileged) ||
            c.securityContext.privileged == false))
      message: >-
        Privileged mode is disallowed. The fields
        spec.containers[*].securityContext.privileged,
        spec.initContainers[*].securityContext.privileged, and
        spec.ephemeralContainers[*].securityContext.privileged
        must be unset or set to false.

Written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what the policy does and the key decisions made from the docs/source:

---

**API version:** `policies.kyverno.io/v1` — confirmed from the live CRD in the Kyverno main branch (`config/crds/policies.kyverno.io/policies.kyverno.io_validatingpolicies.yaml`), which lists `v1` as the served version.

**matchConstraints** covers three separate `resourceRules` entries:
| API group | Resource | Why |
|---|---|---|
| `""` (core) | `pods` | Direct Pod admission |
| `apps` | `deployments` | Deployment pod template |
| `batch` | `cronjobs` | CronJob job template |

**`variables.podSpec`** normalizes the path to the pod spec using a ternary chain:
- Pod → `object.spec`
- Deployment → `object.spec.template.spec`
- CronJob → `object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec`

This pattern mirrors how the official Kyverno `test-context-apicall-vpol` policy resolves per-kind paths.

**CEL expression** uses the same pattern as the official `disallow-privileged-containers.cel.yaml` from the `kyverno-policies` Helm chart:
- `.all(c, ...)` iterates each container array
- `!has(c.securityContext) || !has(c.securityContext.privileged) || c.securityContext.privileged == false` safely guards against absent optional fields before reading them — matching the CEL `has()` idiom used throughout Kyverno's own policies

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-privileged-containers
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Privileged Containers
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Deployment, CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Privileged mode disables most security mechanisms and must not be allowed.
      This policy ensures Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs do not have containers,
      initContainers, or ephemeralContainers with securityContext.privileged set
      to true.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  evaluation:
    mode: Kubernetes
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
      - apiGroups: ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["cronjobs"]
  variables:
    # Resolve the pod spec from whichever resource kind is being admitted:
    #   Pod        -> object.spec
    #   CronJob    -> object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
    #   Deployment -> object.spec.template.spec
    - name: podSpec
      expression: >-
        has(object.spec.containers)
          ? object.spec
          : has(object.spec.jobTemplate)
            ? object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
            : object.spec.template.spec
  validations:
    - message: >-
        Privileged mode is disallowed. The fields
        spec.containers[*].securityContext.privileged,
        spec.initContainers[*].securityContext.privileged, and
        spec.ephemeralContainers[*].securityContext.privileged
        must be unset or set to false.
      expression: >-
        (variables.podSpec.containers +
         variables.podSpec.?initContainers.orValue([]) +
         variables.podSpec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])).all(c,
          !has(c.securityContext) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.privileged) ||
          !c.securityContext.privileged
        )
runs.raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a breakdown of the key design decisions:

---

### Structure

**`apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy`**
The new Kyverno 1.16+ policy type, backed by CEL (not Kyverno's old JMESPath/pattern syntax).

**`spec.validationActions: [Deny]`**
Hard-deny at admission — resources are rejected immediately rather than just reported.

**`spec.evaluation.mode: Kubernetes`**
Uses the Kubernetes-typed CEL environment (full schema awareness, typed field access).

---

### `matchConstraints.resourceRules`

Three separate entries to explicitly match all three resource kinds:

| Group | Resource |
|---|---|
| `""` (core) | `pods` |
| `apps` | `deployments` |
| `batch` | `cronjobs` |

---

### `podSpec` variable — path normalisation

Each resource kind stores the pod spec at a different path:

| Kind | Container path |
|---|---|
| `Pod` | `object.spec` |
| `Deployment` | `object.spec.template.spec` |
| `CronJob` | `object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec` |

The variable uses CEL's short-circuit ternary to detect which resource is being evaluated and return the right sub-object:
- `has(object.spec.containers)` → Pod
- `has(object.spec.jobTemplate)` → CronJob
- otherwise → Deployment

---

### Validation CEL expression

```cel
(variables.podSpec.containers +
 variables.podSpec.?initContainers.orValue([]) +
 variables.podSpec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])).all(c,
  !has(c.securityContext) ||
  !has(c.securityContext.privileged) ||
  !c.securityContext.privileged
)
```

- `?field.orValue([])` — safe optional access (returns `[]` if the field is absent, avoiding errors when `initContainers`/`ephemeralContainers` are not set)
- Triple guard: passes if `securityContext` is absent **or** `privileged` is absent **or** `privileged` is `false`
- `.all(c, ...)` — enforces the constraint across every container in the combined list

description: requires all Pods to have the label 'app.kubernetes.io/name' set to a non-empty value. Deny admission if the label is missing or empty.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that requires all Pods to have the label 'app.kubernetes.io/name' set to a non-empty value. Deny admission if the label is missing or empty. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/gen_require_labels.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
# Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy
# Requires all Pods to have the label 'app.kubernetes.io/name' set to a non-empty value.
#
# NOTE: Kyverno 1.16 ships with apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1beta1.
# The stable v1 API is targeted for Kyverno 1.17+. If you are running Kyverno 1.16,
# change the apiVersion below to: policies.kyverno.io/v1beta1
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-app-kubernetes-io-name
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require app.kubernetes.io/name label on Pods
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      All Pods must carry the recommended Kubernetes label
      'app.kubernetes.io/name' set to a non-empty string value.
      Admission is denied if the label is absent or empty.
spec:
  # Deny admission when the validation expression evaluates to false.
  validationActions:
    - Deny

  # Target Pod resources on CREATE and UPDATE operations.
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["pods"]

  # Reusable variable: safely retrieve the labels map, defaulting to {} if absent.
  variables:
    - name: labels
      expression: "object.metadata.?labels.orValue({})"

  # Validation: the label must be present and non-empty.
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        'app.kubernetes.io/name' in variables.labels &&
        variables.labels['app.kubernetes.io/name'] != ''
      message: >-
        Pod must have the label 'app.kubernetes.io/name' set to a non-empty value.
raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's what it does and key design decisions:

**Policy structure**
- `validationActions: [Deny]` — blocks admission immediately when the check fails (replaces the older `validationFailureAction: Enforce`).
- `matchConstraints.resourceRules` — targets core group (`""`) Pods on `CREATE` and `UPDATE`.
- `variables` — defines a reusable `labels` expression using optional chaining (`?labels`) + `.orValue({})`, which safely returns an empty map if `metadata.labels` is entirely absent, preventing null-reference errors in CEL.
- `validations[].expression` — two-part CEL check:
  1. `'app.kubernetes.io/name' in variables.labels` — key must exist in the map.
  2. `variables.labels['app.kubernetes.io/name'] != ''` — value must be non-empty.

**API version note**
The research confirmed that Kyverno **1.16** ships with `policies.kyverno.io/v1beta1`; the stable `v1` API is planned for Kyverno 1.17+. The policy is written with `v1` as requested and a comment explains the substitution needed for a 1.16 cluster. The spec structure is identical between the two versions.

# Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy
# Requires all Pods to have the label 'app.kubernetes.io/name' set to a non-empty value.
#
# NOTE: Kyverno 1.16 ships with apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1beta1.
# The stable v1 API is targeted for Kyverno 1.17+. If you are running Kyverno 1.16,
# change the apiVersion below to: policies.kyverno.io/v1beta1
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-app-kubernetes-io-name
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require app.kubernetes.io/name label on Pods
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      All Pods must carry the recommended Kubernetes label
      'app.kubernetes.io/name' set to a non-empty string value.
      Admission is denied if the label is absent or empty.
spec:
  # Deny admission when the validation expression evaluates to false.
  validationActions:
    - Deny

  # Target Pod resources on CREATE and UPDATE operations.
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["pods"]

  # Reusable variable: safely retrieve the labels map, defaulting to {} if absent.
  variables:
    - name: labels
      expression: "object.metadata.?labels.orValue({})"

  # Validation: the label must be present and non-empty.
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        'app.kubernetes.io/name' in variables.labels &&
        variables.labels['app.kubernetes.io/name'] != ''
      message: >-
        Pod must have the label 'app.kubernetes.io/name' set to a non-empty value.

**Policy structure**
- `validationActions: [Deny]` — blocks admission immediately when the check fails (replaces the older `validationFailureAction: Enforce`).
- `matchConstraints.resourceRules` — targets core group (`""`) Pods on `CREATE` and `UPDATE`.
- `variables` — defines a reusable `labels` expression using optional chaining (`?labels`) + `.orValue({})`, which safely returns an empty map if `metadata.labels` is entirely absent, preventing null-reference errors in CEL.
- `validations[].expression` — two-part CEL check:
  1. `'app.kubernetes.io/name' in variables.labels` — key must exist in the map.
  2. `variables.labels['app.kubernetes.io/name'] != ''` — value must be non-empty.

**API version note**
The research confirmed that Kyverno **1.16** ships with `policies.kyverno.io/v1beta1`; the stable `v1` API is planned for Kyverno 1.17+. The policy is written with `v1` as requested and a comment explains the substitution needed for a 1.16 cluster. The spec structure is identical between the two versions.

**API & Kind**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the new CEL-native policy type introduced in Kyverno 1.13+ and stabilised in 1.16+, distinct from the older `kyverno.io/v1` ClusterPolicy.

**`validationActions: [Deny]`**
- Immediately rejects any Pod admission request that fails validation (as opposed to `Audit`, which only records violations).

**`matchConstraints`**
- Targets core API group (`""`), `v1` Pods on `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations.

**CEL expression (three-part guard)**
```
has(object.metadata.labels) &&
'app.kubernetes.io/name' in object.metadata.labels &&
object.metadata.labels['app.kubernetes.io/name'] != ''
```
1. `has(object.metadata.labels)` — safely handles Pods with no `.metadata.labels` map at all.
2. `'app.kubernetes.io/name' in object.metadata.labels` — confirms the key exists.
3. `... != ''` — rejects an explicitly empty string value.

**`messageExpression`**
