The policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a breakdown of the key design decisions informed by the Kyverno 1.16+ documentation:

---

**`apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy`**
This is the new CEL-native policy type introduced in Kyverno 1.13+ and stable in 1.16+, distinct from the older `kyverno.io/v1` `ClusterPolicy` kind.

**`spec.validationActions: [Deny]`**
Causes the API server to reject the request immediately when the validation expression is `false`. (`Audit` would only log; `Warn` would warn but allow.)

**`spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules`**
Mirrors the Kubernetes `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy` structure — targets Pods in the core API group on `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations.

**`spec.validations[].expression`** — three-guard CEL expression:
| Guard | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `has(object.metadata.labels)` | Prevents a runtime error if the entire `labels` map is absent |
| `"app.kubernetes.io/name" in object.metadata.labels` | Checks the label key exists (bracket notation required for keys containing `/`) |
| `object.metadata.labels["app.kubernetes.io/name"] != ""` | Rejects an explicitly empty string value |

**`message` + `messageExpression`**
`messageExpression` is a dynamic CEL string (includes the Pod name) shown in the admission response; `message` is the static fallback if the expression itself fails to evaluate.

description: requires every container in a Pod to set spec.containers[].resources.requests.memory, spec.containers[].resources.requests.cpu, and spec.containers[].resources.limits.memory. Deny admission if any container is missing any of these three fields. CPU limit is NOT required.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that requires every container in a Pod to set spec.containers[].resources.requests.memory, spec.containers[].resources.requests.cpu, and spec.containers[].resources.limits.memory. Deny admission if any container is missing any of these three fields. CPU limit is NOT required. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/gen_require_resource_limits.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-container-resources
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Container Resource Requests and Memory Limit
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Every container in a Pod must set resources.requests.memory,
      resources.requests.cpu, and resources.limits.memory. CPU limits
      are not required. Admission is denied if any container is missing
      any of these three fields.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(container,
          has(container.resources) &&
          has(container.resources.requests) &&
          has(container.resources.requests.memory) &&
          has(container.resources.requests.cpu) &&
          has(container.resources.limits) &&
          has(container.resources.limits.memory)
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        object.spec.containers.filter(container,
          !has(container.resources) ||
          !has(container.resources.requests) ||
          !has(container.resources.requests.memory) ||
          !has(container.resources.requests.cpu) ||
          !has(container.resources.limits) ||
          !has(container.resources.limits.memory)
        ).map(container, container.name).join(", ") +
        ": every container must set resources.requests.memory, resources.requests.cpu, and resources.limits.memory (CPU limits are not required)"
raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a breakdown of the key design decisions:

**API & Kind**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the stable Kyverno 1.16+ API (superset of Kubernetes `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy`).

**Match scope**
- Targets `pods` in the core API group (`""`) on `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations only. No namespace selector — cluster-wide enforcement.

**Validation logic (CEL)**
The single `validations` entry uses `object.spec.containers.all(...)` to iterate every container and short-circuit checks at each nesting level to avoid runtime errors if an intermediate field is absent:

```
has(container.resources)                       # resources block exists
&& has(container.resources.requests)           # .requests exists
&& has(container.resources.requests.memory)    # memory request required ✓
&& has(container.resources.requests.cpu)       # cpu request required ✓
&& has(container.resources.limits)             # .limits exists
&& has(container.resources.limits.memory)      # memory limit required ✓
```

CPU limit is intentionally absent — `resources.limits.cpu` is not checked.

**Dynamic error message**
`messageExpression` uses `.filter()` + `.map()` to name the specific offending containers in the denial message, e.g.:
> `myapp, sidecar: every container must set resources.requests.memory, resources.requests.cpu, and resources.limits.memory`

**API & Kind**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the stable Kyverno 1.16+ API (superset of Kubernetes `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy`).

**Match scope**
- Targets `pods` in the core API group (`""`) on `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations only. No namespace selector — cluster-wide enforcement.

**Validation logic (CEL)**
The single `validations` entry uses `object.spec.containers.all(...)` to iterate every container and short-circuit checks at each nesting level to avoid runtime errors if an intermediate field is absent:

```
has(container.resources)                       # resources block exists

&& has(container.resources.requests.memory)    # memory request required ✓
&& has(container.resources.requests.cpu)       # cpu request required ✓

```

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-container-resources
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Container Resource Requests and Memory Limit
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: "1.16.0"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Every container in a Pod must declare resources.requests.memory,
      resources.requests.cpu, and resources.limits.memory.
      A CPU limit is intentionally not required.
spec:
  failurePolicy: Fail
  evaluation:
    mode: "Kubernetes"
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(c,
          has(c.resources) &&
          has(c.resources.requests) &&
          has(c.resources.requests.memory)
        )
      message: >-
        All containers must set resources.requests.memory.
        CPU limit is not required but memory request is mandatory.

    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(c,
          has(c.resources) &&
          has(c.resources.requests) &&
          has(c.resources.requests.cpu)
        )
      message: >-
        All containers must set resources.requests.cpu.

    - expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(c,
          has(c.resources) &&
          has(c.resources.limits) &&
          has(c.resources.limits.memory)
        )
      message: >-
        All containers must set resources.limits.memory.
        A CPU limit is not required.
runs.raw_log:
The policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a breakdown of the key decisions:

**API / Kind**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the Kyverno 1.16+ native policy type backed by the Kubernetes ValidatingAdmissionPolicy engine.

**`spec.evaluation.mode: "Kubernetes"`**
- Tells Kyverno to use the in-cluster Kubernetes CEL evaluation engine (as opposed to Kyverno's own engine). Required for `policies.kyverno.io/v1`.

**`spec.failurePolicy: Fail`**
- If the policy webhook itself errors (timeout, panic), the pod is denied rather than silently admitted.

**`spec.matchConstraints`**
- Targets core API group (`""`), `v1` Pods, on `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations.

**Three separate `validations`** — one per required field — so each rejection message is precise:
1. `resources.requests.memory` missing
2. `resources.requests.cpu` missing
3. `resources.limits.memory` missing

**CEL expression pattern** (identical structure for all three):
```cel
object.spec.containers.all(c,
  has(c.resources) &&
  has(c.resources.requests) &&   # or has(c.resources.limits)
  has(c.resources.requests.memory)
)
```
- `all()` iterates every element of `spec.containers` and requires every one to satisfy the condition.
- The chained `has()` guards against nil-dereference: CEL's `&&` short-circuits, so `has(c.resources.requests.memory)` is never evaluated when the outer `has(c.resources.requests)` is already false.
- CPU limit is deliberately absent from the policy per the requirements.

kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-container-resources
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Container Resources
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Requires every container in a Pod to set memory requests, CPU requests,
      and memory limits. Pods missing any of these fields on any container are
      denied admission. CPU limits are not required.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validations:
    - message: >-
        All containers must define resources.requests.memory,
        resources.requests.cpu, and resources.limits.memory.
        CPU limits are not required but memory limits and both CPU/memory
        requests are mandatory.
      expression: |
        object.spec.containers.all(c,
          has(c.resources) &&
          has(c.resources.requests) &&
          has(c.resources.requests.memory) &&
          has(c.resources.requests.cpu) &&
          has(c.resources.limits) &&
          has(c.resources.limits.memory)
        )

The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the design decisions based on the Kyverno 1.16+ docs:

**API / Kind**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the new cluster-scoped type introduced in Kyverno 1.16, replacing the older `kyverno.io/v1 ClusterPolicy`.

**Enforcement**
- `validationActions: [Deny]` — blocks admission immediately instead of just auditing.

**Matching**
- `matchConstraints.resourceRules` targets core-group (`""`) `pods` resources on `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations.

**Validation (CEL)**
- `object.spec.containers.all(c, ...)` iterates over every container in the Pod.
- `has()` is used at each level to safely check field presence without panicking on missing intermediate objects — CEL's `&&` short-circuits, so `has(c.resources.requests.memory)` is never evaluated if `has(c.resources.requests)` is false.
- The three required checks are `requests.memory`, `requests.cpu`, and `limits.memory`. `limits.cpu` is deliberately omitted per your requirement.

description: only allows Pod container images that begin with the prefix 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'. Deny any Pod whose containers use images from any other registry.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that only allows Pod container images that begin with the prefix 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'. Deny any Pod whose containers use images from any other registry. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/claude/gen_restrict_registries.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-image-registries
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Image Registries
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Only container images whose names begin with 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'
      are permitted. Pods whose containers (including init and ephemeral
      containers) reference images from any other registry are denied.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations:  ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources:   ['pods']
  variables:
    # Collect every container type into a single list so one rule covers all.
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers
        + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([])
        + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(c,
          c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') ||
          c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        "Images must begin with 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'. " +
        "Disallowed image(s): " +
        variables.allContainers
          .filter(c,
            !c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') &&
            !c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')
          )
          .map(c, c.name + "=" + c.image)
          .join(", ")
raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key design choices:

**API / Kind**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the new Kyverno 1.16+ type backed by Kubernetes `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy` machinery, distinct from the legacy `kyverno.io/v1` `ClusterPolicy`.

**Matching**
- `matchConstraints.resourceRules` targets core API (`''`) Pods on `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations.

**Variable: `allContainers`**
- Merges `spec.containers`, `spec.initContainers`, and `spec.ephemeralContainers` into a single list using the optional-chaining `?` operator and `.orValue([])` so the expression is safe even when those optional lists are absent.

**Validation expression**
- Uses CEL `startsWith` to assert that every container image begins with `eu.foo.io/` or `bar.io/`. The trailing `/` is intentional — it prevents a registry like `eu.foo.io.evil.com/` from matching.

**Dynamic error message**
- `messageExpression` filters the offending containers and formats them as `containerName=image`, making it easy to pinpoint which image triggered the denial without inspecting the Pod spec manually.

**Enforcement**
- `validationActions: [Deny]` blocks non-compliant Pods at admission time (synchronous enforcement, no background scan needed).

kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-image-registries

    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices

    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Ensures that all container images (including init and ephemeral containers)
      are sourced exclusively from the approved registries 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'.
      Any Pod whose containers reference images from any other registry will be denied.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:

          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE

          - pods
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        (object.spec.containers +
         object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) +
         object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])).all(c,
           c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') ||
           c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')
         )
      messageExpression: >-
        "All container images must use an approved registry ('eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'). " +
        "Violating images: [" +
        (object.spec.containers +
         object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) +
         object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])).filter(c,

kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-image-registries

           c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')
         )

        "All container images must use an approved registry ('eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'). " +

         object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])).filter(c,

runs.raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's what it does and why each part was chosen:

---

**`apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy`**
The new Kyverno 1.16+ policy type that extends Kubernetes `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy` with CEL expressions, background scanning, and policy exceptions.

**`validationActions: [Deny]`**
Rejects the admission request outright for any Pod that violates the rule.

**`matchConstraints.resourceRules`**
Targets core API group (`""`), `v1` Pods on `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations — covers both new and modified Pods.

**`validations[].expression` (CEL)**
```cel
(object.spec.containers +
 object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) +
 object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])).all(c,
   c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') ||
   c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')
 )
```
- Concatenates all three container lists into one. `?initContainers` / `?ephemeralContainers` use CEL optional chaining — they resolve to an empty list `[]` if the field is absent, avoiding runtime errors.
- `.all()` asserts that **every** container image starts with one of the two allowed prefixes.

**`messageExpression`**
Produces a dynamic denial message listing only the offending image names, making it easy to diagnose which containers triggered the policy.

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-image-registries
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Image Registries
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Only container images whose names begin with 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'
      are permitted. Pods whose containers (including init and ephemeral
      containers) reference images from any other registry are denied.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations:  ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources:   ['pods']
  variables:
    # Collect every container type into a single list so one rule covers all.
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers
        + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([])
        + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(c,
          c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') ||
          c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        "Images must begin with 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'. " +
        "Disallowed image(s): " +
        variables.allContainers
          .filter(c,
            !c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') &&
            !c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')
          )
          .map(c, c.name + "=" + c.image)
          .join(", ")
runs.raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key design choices:

**API / Kind**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the new Kyverno 1.16+ type backed by Kubernetes `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy` machinery, distinct from the legacy `kyverno.io/v1` `ClusterPolicy`.

**Matching**
- `matchConstraints.resourceRules` targets core API (`''`) Pods on `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations.

**Variable: `allContainers`**
- Merges `spec.containers`, `spec.initContainers`, and `spec.ephemeralContainers` into a single list using the optional-chaining `?` operator and `.orValue([])` so the expression is safe even when those optional lists are absent.

**Validation expression**
- Uses CEL `startsWith` to assert that every container image begins with `eu.foo.io/` or `bar.io/`. The trailing `/` is intentional — it prevents a registry like `eu.foo.io.evil.com/` from matching.

**Dynamic error message**
- `messageExpression` filters the offending containers and formats them as `containerName=image`, making it easy to pinpoint which image triggered the denial without inspecting the Pod spec manually.

**Enforcement**
- `validationActions: [Deny]` blocks non-compliant Pods at admission time (synchronous enforcement, no background scan needed).

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-image-registries
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Image Registries
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Requires all Pod container images (containers, initContainers, and
      ephemeralContainers) to originate from one of the approved registries:
      'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'. Pods whose containers reference images from
      any other registry are denied at admission.
spec:
  # Deny admission outright for any violating Pod.
  validationActions:
    - Deny

  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["pods"]

  validations:
    # --- 1. Regular containers (always present on a Pod spec) ---------------
    - message: >-
        All container images must come from an approved registry
        ('eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/').
      messageExpression: >-
        "Image registry not allowed in containers: [" +
        object.spec.containers
          .filter(c, !c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') && !c.image.startsWith('bar.io/'))
          .map(c, c.name + "=" + c.image)
          .join(", ") +
        "]. Only 'eu.foo.io/' and 'bar.io/' are permitted."
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers.all(c,
          c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') ||
          c.image.startsWith('bar.io/'))

    # --- 2. Init containers (optional field) --------------------------------
    - message: >-
        All initContainer images must come from an approved registry
        ('eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/').
      messageExpression: >-
        !has(object.spec.initContainers) ? "" :
        "Image registry not allowed in initContainers: [" +
        object.spec.initContainers
          .filter(c, !c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') && !c.image.startsWith('bar.io/'))
          .map(c, c.name + "=" + c.image)
          .join(", ") +
        "]. Only 'eu.foo.io/' and 'bar.io/' are permitted."
      expression: >-
        !has(object.spec.initContainers) ||
        object.spec.initContainers.all(c,
          c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') ||
          c.image.startsWith('bar.io/'))

    # --- 3. Ephemeral containers (optional field) ---------------------------
    - message: >-
        All ephemeralContainer images must come from an approved registry
        ('eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/').
      messageExpression: >-
        !has(object.spec.ephemeralContainers) ? "" :
        "Image registry not allowed in ephemeralContainers: [" +
        object.spec.ephemeralContainers
          .filter(c, !c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') && !c.image.startsWith('bar.io/'))
          .map(c, c.name + "=" + c.image)
          .join(", ") +
        "]. Only 'eu.foo.io/' and 'bar.io/' are permitted."
      expression: >-
        !has(object.spec.ephemeralContainers) ||
        object.spec.ephemeralContainers.all(c,
          c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') ||
          c.image.startsWith('bar.io/'))
runs.raw_log:
The policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the design decisions based on the Kyverno 1.16+ docs:

---

**API & kind**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the new CEL-native policy type introduced in Kyverno 1.14+ and stabilised in 1.16.

**`validationActions: [Deny]`**
- Blocks admission immediately rather than just auditing.

**`matchConstraints`**
- Targets core-group (`""`) `pods` on `CREATE` and `UPDATE` so renamed/restarted pods are also checked.

**Three separate `validations`** — one per container list type:

| Validation | CEL expression | Handles absence? |
|---|---|---|
| `containers` | `object.spec.containers.all(c, ...)` | N/A — always present |
| `initContainers` | `!has(object.spec.initContainers) \|\| ...all(c, ...)` | yes |
| `ephemeralContainers` | `!has(object.spec.ephemeralContainers) \|\| ...all(c, ...)` | yes |

Each uses `c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/')` or `c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')` as the allow-list check.

**`messageExpression`**
- Dynamic CEL string that names the offending container(s) and their images, so operators immediately know which image to fix. Falls back to the static `message` if the expression itself errors.

prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_default_labels.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy labels are used as an important source of metadata describing objects in various ways or triggering other functionality. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/cp_add_default_labels.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/

input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-labels
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Labels
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Label
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Labels are used as an important source of metadata describing objects in various ways
      or triggering other functionality. Labels are also a very basic concept and should be
      used throughout Kubernetes. This policy performs a simple mutation which adds a label
      `foo=bar` to Pods, Services, ConfigMaps, and Secrets.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: add-labels
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
          - Service
          - ConfigMap
          - Secret
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        metadata:
          labels:
            foo: bar
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-labels
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Labels
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Label
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Labels are used as an important source of metadata describing objects in various ways
      or triggering other functionality. Labels are also a very basic concept and should be
      used throughout Kubernetes. This policy performs a simple mutation which adds a label
      `foo=bar` to Pods, Services, ConfigMaps, and Secrets.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods", "services", "configmaps", "secrets"]
  mutations:
    - patchType: ApplyConfiguration
      applyConfiguration:
        expression: >
          Object{
            metadata: Object.metadata{
              labels: Object.metadata.labels{
                foo: "bar"
              }
            }
          }

**`apiVersion` / `kind`**
- `kyverno.io/v1` / `ClusterPolicy` → `policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `MutatingPolicy`

**`spec.rules[].match` → `spec.matchConstraints`**
- The new API uses Kubernetes-native `matchConstraints.resourceRules` (same structure as `MutatingAdmissionPolicy`). Each resource kind must be expressed with its `apiGroups`, `apiVersions`, `operations`, and `resources`. Pods, Services, ConfigMaps, and Secrets are all core API group (`""`) `v1` resources.
- `operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]` is added — the equivalent of what the old `patchStrategicMerge` rule implicitly applied on admission.

**`spec.rules[].mutate.patchStrategicMerge` → `spec.mutations[].patchType: ApplyConfiguration`**
- The new API uses CEL expressions returning an `Object` for apply-configuration-style patches. The `foo: "bar"` label is expressed as a CEL `Object` literal, which is the idiomatic equivalent of the old `patchStrategicMerge` approach.

**`annotations`**
- All original annotations (title, category, severity, subject, description) are preserved. `minversion` is updated from `1.6.0` to `1.16.0` to reflect the new API's requirements.

input_snippet:
apiVersion : kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-default-resources
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Default Resources
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.10.0-alpha.2
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.7.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.26"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Pods which don't specify at least resource requests are assigned a QoS class
      of BestEffort which can hog resources for other Pods on Nodes. At a minimum,
      all Pods should specify resource requests in order to be labeled as the QoS
      class Burstable. This sample mutates any container in a Pod which doesn't
      specify memory or cpu requests to apply some sane defaults.
spec:
  background: false
  rules:
  - name: add-default-requests
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    preconditions:
      any:
      - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
        operator: AnyIn
        value:
        - CREATE
        - UPDATE
    mutate:
      foreach:
      - list: "request.object.spec.[ephemeralContainers, initContainers, containers][]"
        patchStrategicMerge:
          spec:
            containers:
            - (name): "{{element.name}}"
              resources:
                requests:
                  +(memory): "100Mi"
                  +(cpu): "100m"

| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: MutatingPolicy` |
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `spec.rules[].match` (JMESPath) | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` (CEL/K8s-native) |
| `spec.rules[].preconditions` (JMESPath) | Operations specified directly in `matchConstraints.resourceRules[].operations` |
| `spec.rules[].mutate.foreach` with `patchStrategicMerge` + `+(field)` anchors | `spec.mutations[].patchType: ApplyConfiguration` with CEL `map()` expressions |
| `spec.background: false` | No equivalent needed; admission-only is the default |

**Design decisions:**

- **Three separate mutations** cover `containers`, `initContainers`, and `ephemeralContainers` individually — mirroring the original `foreach` over all three list types, since the new API requires distinct paths for each.
- **`ApplyConfiguration` patch type** is used (the idiomatic modern approach), using CEL `map()` to iterate over containers and conditionally preserve existing values or fall back to `"100Mi"` / `"100m"`.
- **`has()` guards** replicate the `+(field)` "add-if-not-present" anchors from the original `patchStrategicMerge`, ensuring existing requests are never overwritten.
- **`initContainers` / `ephemeralContainers` wrapped in `has()` checks** so they return `Object{}` (a no-op) when those lists aren't present.

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-default-resources
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Default Resources
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.26"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Pods which don't specify at least resource requests are assigned a QoS class
      of BestEffort which can hog resources for other Pods on Nodes. At a minimum,
      all Pods should specify resource requests in order to be labeled as the QoS
      class Burstable. This policy mutates any container, initContainer, or
      ephemeralContainer in a Pod which doesn't specify memory or cpu requests to
      apply some sane defaults.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources: ['pods']
  variables:
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers
        + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([])
        + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  mutations:
    # Patch containers (regular)
    - patchType: JSONPatch
      jsonPatch:
        expression: >-
          object.spec.containers.map(c, !has(c.resources) || !has(c.resources.requests) || !has(c.resources.requests.memory) ?
            JSONPatch{
              op: "add",
              path: "/spec/containers/" + string(object.spec.containers.indexOf(c)) + "/resources/requests/memory",
              value: "100Mi"
            } : null
          ).filter(p, p != null)
    - patchType: JSONPatch
      jsonPatch:
        expression: >-
          object.spec.containers.map(c, !has(c.resources) || !has(c.resources.requests) || !has(c.resources.requests.cpu) ?
            JSONPatch{
              op: "add",
              path: "/spec/containers/" + string(object.spec.containers.indexOf(c)) + "/resources/requests/cpu",
              value: "100m"
            } : null
          ).filter(p, p != null)
    # Patch initContainers
    - patchType: JSONPatch
      jsonPatch:
        expression: >-
          object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]).map(c, !has(c.resources) || !has(c.resources.requests) || !has(c.resources.requests.memory) ?
            JSONPatch{
              op: "add",
              path: "/spec/initContainers/" + string(object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]).indexOf(c)) + "/resources/requests/memory",
              value: "100Mi"
            } : null
          ).filter(p, p != null)
    - patchType: JSONPatch
      jsonPatch:
        expression: >-
          object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]).map(c, !has(c.resources) || !has(c.resources.requests) || !has(c.resources.requests.cpu) ?
            JSONPatch{
              op: "add",
              path: "/spec/initContainers/" + string(object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]).indexOf(c)) + "/resources/requests/cpu",
              value: "100m"
            } : null
          ).filter(p, p != null)
    # Patch ephemeralContainers
    - patchType: JSONPatch
      jsonPatch:
        expression: >-
          object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([]).map(c, !has(c.resources) || !has(c.resources.requests) || !has(c.resources.requests.memory) ?
            JSONPatch{
              op: "add",
              path: "/spec/ephemeralContainers/" + string(object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([]).indexOf(c)) + "/resources/requests/memory",
              value: "100Mi"
            } : null
          ).filter(p, p != null)
    - patchType: JSONPatch
      jsonPatch:
        expression: >-
          object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([]).map(c, !has(c.resources) || !has(c.resources.requests) || !has(c.resources.requests.cpu) ?
            JSONPatch{
              op: "add",

The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key changes made during conversion:

**API / Kind changes:**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1`
- `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `kind: MutatingPolicy`

**Match rules → `matchConstraints`:**
- Replaced JMESPath-based `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources.kinds` and the `preconditions` block with a CEL-native `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` entry that gates on `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations on `v1/pods`.

**Mutation logic → CEL `JSONPatch` expressions:**
- Replaced the JMESPath `foreach` + `patchStrategicMerge` with `+(key)` (add-if-absent) semantics with CEL `map()` + `filter()` over `JSONPatch` objects.
- Each container type (`containers`, `initContainers`, `ephemeralContainers`) gets its own pair of patches (one for `memory`, one for `cpu`).
- The `+(memory)` / `+(cpu)` "add if not present" behaviour is preserved by checking `!has(c.resources) || !has(c.resources.requests) || !has(c.resources.requests.memory)` (same for `cpu`) before emitting the patch, and `filter(p, p != null)` drops no-op entries.
- `object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([])` / `?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])` safely handles pods that omit those optional lists, matching the original policy's behaviour.

prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_ndots.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy the ndots value controls where DNS lookups are first performed in a cluster and needs to be set to a lower value than the default of 5 in some cases. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/cp_add_ndots.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-ndots
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add ndots
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The ndots value controls where DNS lookups are first performed in a cluster
      and needs to be set to a lower value than the default of 5 in some cases.
      This policy mutates all Pods to add the ndots option with a value of 1.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: add-ndots
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        spec:
          dnsConfig:
            options:
              - name: ndots
                value: "1"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-ndots
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add ndots
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The ndots value controls where DNS lookups are first performed in a cluster
      and needs to be set to a lower value than the default of 5 in some cases.
      This policy mutates all Pods to add the ndots option with a value of 1.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources: ['pods']
  mutations:
    - patchType: ApplyConfiguration
      applyConfiguration:
        expression: >
          Object{
            spec: Object.spec{
              dnsConfig: Object.spec.dnsConfig{
                options: (
                  has(object.spec.dnsConfig) && has(object.spec.dnsConfig.options) ?
                  object.spec.dnsConfig.options.filter(o, o.name != "ndots") :
                  []
                ) + [
                  Object.spec.dnsConfig.options{
                    name: "ndots",
                    value: "1"
                  }
                ]
              }
            }
          }

- `spec.rules[].mutate.patchStrategicMerge` → `spec.mutations[].patchType: ApplyConfiguration` with a CEL expression — the new API uses CEL-based `ApplyConfiguration` patches instead of YAML strategic merge patches.

**CEL expression logic:**
The expression uses a conditional to handle two cases correctly:
1. If `dnsConfig.options` already exists — filters out any existing `ndots` entry (avoiding duplicates) and appends the new one.
2. If `dnsConfig.options` doesn't exist — creates the list with just the `ndots` entry.

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-ndots
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add ndots
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The ndots value controls where DNS lookups are first performed in a cluster
      and needs to be set to a lower value than the default of 5 in some cases.
      This policy mutates all Pods to add the ndots option with a value of 1.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  mutations:
    - patchType: ApplyConfiguration
      applyConfiguration:
        expression: >
          Object{
            spec: Object.spec{
              dnsConfig: Object.spec.dnsConfig{
                options: (has(object.spec.dnsConfig) && has(object.spec.dnsConfig.options)) ?
                  object.spec.dnsConfig.options.filter(o, o.name != "ndots") + [Object.spec.dnsConfig.options{name: "ndots", value: "1"}] :
                  [Object.spec.dnsConfig.options{name: "ndots", value: "1"}]
              }
            }
          }

The `patchStrategicMerge` was converted to a CEL `ApplyConfiguration` expression that:
1. Checks if `dnsConfig.options` already exists on the pod
2. If so, filters out any existing `ndots` entry to avoid duplicates, then appends the new `ndots: "1"` option
3. If not, sets the options list with just the `ndots: "1"` entry

description: to better control the number of resources that can be created in a given Namespace and provide default resource consumption limits for Pods, ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources are recommended
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_ns_quota.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ GeneratingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy to better control the number of resources that can be created in a given Namespace and provide default resource consumption limits for Pods, ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources are recommended. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/cp_add_ns_quota.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-ns-quota
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Quota
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Multi-Tenancy, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: ResourceQuota, LimitRange
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      To better control the number of resources that can be created in a given
      Namespace and provide default resource consumption limits for Pods,
      ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources are recommended.
      This policy will generate ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources when
      a new Namespace is created.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: generate-resourcequota
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Namespace
    generate:
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: ResourceQuota
      name: default-resourcequota
      synchronize: true
      namespace: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
      data:
        spec:
          hard:
            requests.cpu: '4'
            requests.memory: '16Gi'
            limits.cpu: '4'
            limits.memory: '16Gi'
  - name: generate-limitrange
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Namespace
    generate:
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: LimitRange
      name: default-limitrange
      synchronize: true
      namespace: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
      data:
        spec:
          limits:
          - default:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 1Gi
            defaultRequest:
              cpu: 200m
              memory: 256Mi
            type: Container

description: the Kubernetes cluster autoscaler does not evict pods that  use hostPath or emptyDir volumes
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_safe_to_evict.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy the Kubernetes cluster autoscaler does not evict pods that  use hostPath or emptyDir volumes. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/cp_add_safe_to_evict.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-safe-to-evict
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Safe To Evict
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Annotation
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The Kubernetes cluster autoscaler does not evict pods that 
      use hostPath or emptyDir volumes. To allow eviction of these pods, the annotation 
      cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict=true must be added to the pods. 
spec: 
  rules: 
  - name: annotate-empty-dir
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        metadata:
          annotations:
            +(cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict): "true"
        spec:          
          volumes: 
          - <(emptyDir): {}
  - name: annotate-host-path
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        metadata:
          annotations:
            +(cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict): "true"
        spec:          
          volumes: 
          - hostPath:
              <(path): "*"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-safe-to-evict
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Safe To Evict
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Annotation
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The Kubernetes cluster autoscaler does not evict pods that
      use hostPath or emptyDir volumes. To allow eviction of these pods, the
      annotation cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict=true must be
      added to the pods.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  matchConditions:
    - name: has-emptydir-or-hostpath
      expression: |
        has(object.spec.volumes) &&
        object.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.emptyDir) || has(v.hostPath))
    - name: annotation-not-present
      expression: |
        !has(object.metadata.annotations) ||
        !object.metadata.annotations.exists(k, k == 'cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict')
  mutations:
    - patchType: ApplyConfiguration
      applyConfiguration:
        expression: |
          Object{
            metadata: Object.metadata{
              annotations: {
                "cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict": "true"
              }
            }
          }

The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key changes made during the conversion:

**API / Kind changes:**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1`
- `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `kind: MutatingPolicy`

**Spec restructure** — the `rules[].match` + `rules[].mutate.patchStrategicMerge` pattern is replaced by the CEL-native `MutatingAdmissionPolicy`-style structure:

| Old (`ClusterPolicy`) | New (`MutatingPolicy`) |
|---|---|
| `spec.rules[].match` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` |
| `spec.rules[].mutate.patchStrategicMerge` anchors (`<(...)`, `+(...)`) | `spec.matchConditions` (CEL) + `spec.mutations[].applyConfiguration` (CEL) |

- A second condition that skips pods already annotated (preserving the `+()` "add if missing" semantics of the original)
- A single `ApplyConfiguration` mutation that sets the annotation

The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key changes made during the conversion:

**API and Kind**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1`
- `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `kind: MutatingPolicy`

**Match rules** — replaced the Kyverno-specific `spec.rules[].match` syntax with the CEL-based `spec.matchConstraints` (Kubernetes `MutatingAdmissionPolicy` style), explicitly listing `apiGroups`, `apiVersions`, `operations`, and `resources`.

**Conditions** — the two separate rules with `patchStrategicMerge` anchor patterns (`<(emptyDir)`, `<(path)`) were unified into a single policy using `matchConditions` with CEL expressions:
- `has-emptydir-or-hostpath` — checks that the pod has at least one `emptyDir` or `hostPath` volume
- `annotation-not-present` — prevents overwriting an existing explicit annotation (preserving the `+()` "if-not-present" semantics from the original)

**Mutation** — replaced `patchStrategicMerge` with a `mutations[].patchType: ApplyConfiguration` block using a CEL `Object{}` expression, which is the idiomatic approach for `MutatingPolicy`.

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-safe-to-evict
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Safe To Evict
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Annotation
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The Kubernetes cluster autoscaler does not evict pods that
      use hostPath or emptyDir volumes. To allow eviction of these pods, the annotation
      cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict=true must be added to the pods.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources: ['pods']
  variables:
    - name: hasEmptyDir
      expression: >-
        has(object.spec.volumes) &&
        object.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.emptyDir))
    - name: hasHostPath
      expression: >-
        has(object.spec.volumes) &&
        object.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.hostPath))
    - name: needsAnnotation
      expression: >-
        (variables.hasEmptyDir || variables.hasHostPath) &&
        !(
          has(object.metadata.annotations) &&
          has(object.metadata.annotations["cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict"])
        )
  mutations:
    - patchType: JSONPatch
      jsonPatch:
        expression: |
          variables.needsAnnotation ?
            (
              has(object.metadata.annotations) ?
              [
                JSONPatch{
                  op: "add",
                  path: "/metadata/annotations/cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io~1safe-to-evict",
                  value: "true"
                }
              ] :
              [
                JSONPatch{
                  op: "add",
                  path: "/metadata/annotations",
                  value: {"cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict": "true"}
                }
              ]
            ) :
            []

The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the changes made:

**API/Kind changes:**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1`
- `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `kind: MutatingPolicy`
- Updated `minversion` annotation to `1.16.0`

**Structure changes:**

| Old (`ClusterPolicy`) | New (`MutatingPolicy`) |
|---|---|
| `spec.rules[].match.any` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` |
| `spec.rules[].mutate.patchStrategicMerge` | `spec.mutations[].patchType: JSONPatch` with CEL |
| Two separate rules | Single mutation with `variables` + one `JSONPatch` mutation |

**Logic translation:**

- The `<(emptyDir): {}` and `<(path): "*"` anchor patterns from `patchStrategicMerge` (which trigger the mutation only when those volume fields exist) are replaced with CEL `variables`:
  - `hasEmptyDir` — checks `object.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.emptyDir))`
  - `hasHostPath` — checks `object.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.hostPath))`
  - `needsAnnotation` — combines the above and also guards against overwriting an existing annotation (replicating the `+(...)` "add if not present" anchor behavior)

- The `JSONPatch` mutation handles both cases: when `metadata.annotations` already exists (add just the key) vs. when it doesn't exist yet (add the whole map).

description: a Pod securityContext entry defines fields such as the user and group which should be used to run the Pod
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_securitycontext.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy a Pod securityContext entry defines fields such as the user and group which should be used to run the Pod. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/cp_add_securitycontext.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-default-securitycontext
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Default securityContext
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A Pod securityContext entry defines fields such as the user and group which should be used to run the Pod.
      Sometimes choosing default values for users rather than blocking is a better alternative to not impede
      such Pod definitions. This policy will mutate a Pod to set `runAsNonRoot`, `runAsUser`, `runAsGroup`, and 
      `fsGroup` fields within the Pod securityContext if they are not already set.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: add-default-securitycontext
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        spec:
          securityContext:
            +(runAsNonRoot): true
            +(runAsUser): 1000
            +(runAsGroup): 3000
            +(fsGroup): 2000

kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-default-securitycontext
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Default securityContext

    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A Pod securityContext entry defines fields such as the user and group which should be used to run the Pod.
      Sometimes choosing default values for users rather than blocking is a better alternative to not impede
      such Pod definitions. This policy will mutate a Pod to set `runAsNonRoot`, `runAsUser`, `runAsGroup`, and
      `fsGroup` fields within the Pod securityContext if they are not already set.
spec:
  matchConstraints:

The converted policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what changed and why:

**Structural changes:**
- `apiVersion`: `kyverno.io/v1` → `policies.kyverno.io/v1`
- `kind`: `ClusterPolicy` → `MutatingPolicy`
- `spec.rules[].match` → `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` (standard Kubernetes admission-style resource selector)
- `operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]` added explicitly (required by `matchConstraints`)
- `spec.rules[].mutate.patchStrategicMerge` → `spec.mutations[].patchType: ApplyConfiguration` with a CEL expression

**Semantic equivalence for `+(field)` (add-if-not-present):**
The old policy used Kyverno's `+(field)` anchor to only set a field when absent. In the new CEL-based API there is no built-in anchor, so each field is expressed as a conditional:
```
has(object.spec.securityContext) && has(object.spec.securityContext.runAsNonRoot)
  ? object.spec.securityContext.runAsNonRoot   # keep existing value
  : true                                        # set default
```
This precisely replicates the "only write if missing" behaviour for all four fields: `runAsNonRoot`, `runAsUser`, `runAsGroup`, and `fsGroup`.

description: pod tolerations are used to schedule on Nodes which have a matching taint
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_tolerations.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy pod tolerations are used to schedule on Nodes which have a matching taint. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/cp_add_tolerations.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-tolerations
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Tolerations
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.7.1
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >- 
      Pod tolerations are used to schedule on Nodes which have
      a matching taint. This policy adds the toleration `org.com/role=service:NoSchedule`
      if existing tolerations do not contain the key `org.com/role`.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: service-toleration
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    preconditions:
      any:
      - key: "org.com/role"
        operator: AnyNotIn
        value: "{{ request.object.spec.tolerations[].key || `[]` }}"
    mutate:
      patchesJson6902: |-
        - op: add
          path: "/spec/tolerations/-"
          value:
            key: org.com/role
            operator: Equal
            value: service
            effect: NoSchedule
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-tolerations
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Tolerations
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Pod tolerations are used to schedule on Nodes which have
      a matching taint. This policy adds the toleration `org.com/role=service:NoSchedule`
      if existing tolerations do not contain the key `org.com/role`.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  matchConditions:
    - name: skip-if-toleration-exists
      expression: |
        !has(object.spec.tolerations) ||
        object.spec.tolerations == null ||
        !object.spec.tolerations.exists(t, t.key == "org.com/role")
  mutations:
    - patchType: JSONPatch
      jsonPatch:
        expression: |
          (!has(object.spec.tolerations) || object.spec.tolerations == null) ?
            [
              JSONPatch{
                op: "add",
                path: "/spec/tolerations",
                value: [dyn({
                  "key": "org.com/role",
                  "operator": "Equal",
                  "value": "service",
                  "effect": "NoSchedule"
                })]
              }
            ] :
            [
              JSONPatch{
                op: "add",
                path: "/spec/tolerations/-",
                value: dyn({
                  "key": "org.com/role",
                  "operator": "Equal",
                  "value": "service",
                  "effect": "NoSchedule"
                })
              }
            ]

The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key changes made during conversion:

**`apiVersion` / `kind`**
- `kyverno.io/v1` / `ClusterPolicy` → `policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `MutatingPolicy`

**`spec.rules[].match`** → **`spec.matchConstraints`**
- The old `match.any[].resources.kinds` block is replaced with the CEL-native `matchConstraints.resourceRules`, explicitly listing `apiGroups`, `apiVersions`, `operations` (CREATE, UPDATE), and `resources`.

**`spec.rules[].preconditions`** → **`spec.matchConditions`**
- The JMESPath precondition (`AnyNotIn` operator checking toleration keys) is converted to a CEL `matchConditions` expression that skips the mutation if the `org.com/role` toleration already exists.

**`spec.rules[].mutate.patchesJson6902`** → **`spec.mutations[].patchType: JSONPatch`**
- The old YAML-string patch is replaced with a CEL expression returning a `JSONPatch` list. It also handles the edge case where `spec.tolerations` doesn't exist yet (initializing the array vs. appending with `-`), which the original policy assumed was always present.

runs.raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key changes made during the conversion:

**API/Kind**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: MutatingPolicy`

**Match constraints**
- The old `spec.rules[].match.any` block using Kyverno's JMESPath-style resource selectors is replaced by `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules`, which follows the Kubernetes `MutatingAdmissionPolicy` structure with explicit `apiGroups`, `apiVersions`, `operations`, and `resources`.
- `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations are both included (the original implicitly applied to both).

**Preconditions → matchConditions**
- The old `preconditions` block with the JMESPath/Kyverno template expression (`AnyNotIn` operator against `request.object.spec.tolerations[].key`) is replaced by a CEL `matchConditions` expression:
  ```
  !has(object.spec.tolerations) || !object.spec.tolerations.exists(t, t.key == "org.com/role")
  ```
  This checks that the `org.com/role` toleration key is absent before applying the mutation.

**Mutation**
- The old `patchesJson6902` string (Kyverno-specific) is replaced by `mutations[].patchType: JSONPatch` with a CEL `jsonPatch.expression`. The CEL expression handles both cases: pods with no existing tolerations (sets the field to a new list) and pods with existing tolerations (appends with `-`).

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-tolerations
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Tolerations
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Pod tolerations are used to schedule on Nodes which have
      a matching taint. This policy adds the toleration `org.com/role=service:NoSchedule`
      if existing tolerations do not contain the key `org.com/role`.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  matchConditions:
    - name: skip-if-toleration-exists
      expression: |
        !has(object.spec.tolerations) ||
        object.spec.tolerations == null ||
        !object.spec.tolerations.exists(t, t.key == "org.com/role")
  mutations:
    - patchType: JSONPatch
      jsonPatch:
        expression: |
          (!has(object.spec.tolerations) || object.spec.tolerations == null) ?
            [
              JSONPatch{
                op: "add",
                path: "/spec/tolerations",
                value: [dyn({
                  "key": "org.com/role",
                  "operator": "Equal",
                  "value": "service",
                  "effect": "NoSchedule"
                })]
              }
            ] :
            [
              JSONPatch{
                op: "add",
                path: "/spec/tolerations/-",
                value: dyn({
                  "key": "org.com/role",
                  "operator": "Equal",
                  "value": "service",
                  "effect": "NoSchedule"
                })
              }
            ]

apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: always-pull-images
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Always Pull Images
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      By default, images that have already been pulled can be accessed by other
      Pods without re-pulling them if the name and tag are known. In multi-tenant scenarios,
      this may be undesirable. This policy mutates all incoming Pods to set their
      imagePullPolicy to Always. An alternative to the Kubernetes admission controller
      AlwaysPullImages.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: always-pull-images
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        spec:
          containers:
          - (name): "?*"
            imagePullPolicy: Always

    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      By default, images that have already been pulled can be accessed by other
      Pods without re-pulling them if the name and tag are known. In multi-tenant scenarios,
      this may be undesirable. This policy mutates all incoming Pods to set their
      imagePullPolicy to Always. An alternative to the Kubernetes admission controller
      AlwaysPullImages.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources: ['pods']
  mutations:
    - patchType: ApplyConfiguration
      applyConfiguration:
        expression: >
          Object{
            spec: Object.spec{
              containers: object.spec.containers.map(c, Object.spec.containers{
                name: c.name,
                imagePullPolicy: "Always"
              }),
              initContainers: object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]).map(c, Object.spec.initContainers{
                name: c.name,
                imagePullPolicy: "Always"
              }),
              ephemeralContainers: object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([]).map(c, Object.spec.ephemeralContainers{
                name: c.name,
                imagePullPolicy: "Always"
              })
            }
          }

yaml_preview:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: block-stale-images
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Block Stale Images
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.25"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Images that are old usually have some open security vulnerabilities which are not patched.
      This policy checks the contents of every container image and inspects them for the create time.
      If it finds any image which was built more than 6 months ago this policy blocks the deployment.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources: ['pods']
  variables:
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers
        + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([])
        + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
    - name: sixMonthsAgo
      expression: >-
        timestamp(string(now() - duration("4380h")))
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(c,
          image.GetMetadata(c.image).config.created == "" ||
          timestamp(image.GetMetadata(c.image).config.created) > variables.sixMonthsAgo
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        "Images built more than 6 months ago are prohibited. Stale container(s): " +
        variables.allContainers.filter(c,
          image.GetMetadata(c.image).config.created != "" &&
          timestamp(image.GetMetadata(c.image).config.created) <= variables.sixMonthsAgo
        ).map(c, c.name + " (" + c.image + ")").join(", ")
      message: "Images built more than 6 months ago are prohibited."
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: block-stale-images
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Block Stale Images
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Images that are old usually have some open security vulnerabilities which are not patched.
      This policy checks the contents of every container image and inspects them for the create time.
      If it finds any image which was built more than 6 months ago this policy blocks the deployment.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit 
  rules:
    - name: block-stale-images
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
            - Pod
      validate:
        message: "Images built more than 6 months ago are prohibited."
        foreach:
        - list: "request.object.spec.containers"
          context:
          - name: imageData
            imageRegistry:
              reference: "{{ element.image }}"
          deny:
            conditions:
              all:
                - key: "{{ time_since('', '{{ imageData.configData.created }}', '') }}"
                  operator: GreaterThan
                  value: 4380h

raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what changed and why:

**API/Kind changes:**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1`
- `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `kind: ValidatingPolicy`

**Spec structure changes:**
- `validationFailureAction: Audit` → `validationActions: [Audit]` (the new list-based field)
- `spec.rules[].match` + `spec.rules[].validate.foreach` → `matchConstraints.resourceRules` (standard Kubernetes VAP structure)

**Logic rewritten from JMESPath/Kyverno DSL to CEL:**
- The old policy used `foreach` with `imageRegistry` context and `time_since()` JMESPath functions to fetch and compare image creation timestamps
- The new policy uses Kyverno's **ImageData CEL library** — `image.GetMetadata(c.image).config.created` — to fetch OCI image metadata, and standard CEL `timestamp()` / `duration()` / `now()` functions for the age comparison
- `variables` are used to pre-compute the container list (including init/ephemeral containers) and the 6-month cutoff timestamp (`4380h = 6 × 30 × 24h`)
- A `messageExpression` dynamically names which specific containers/images failed, replacing the static message

runs.raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key changes made during conversion:

**API / Kind**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ValidatingPolicy`

**Enforcement action**
- `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` → `spec.validationActions: [Audit]`

**Match constraints**
- The old `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources` block is replaced by the standard Kubernetes-style `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules`, which also explicitly covers `UPDATE` (not just `CREATE`).

**Image metadata lookup**
- The old JMESPath-based `imageRegistry` context entry (`imageData.configData.created`) is replaced by Kyverno's CEL `ImageData` library: `image.GetMetadata(c.image).config.created`.

**Foreach → CEL `.all()` macro**
- The old `validate.foreach` loop is replaced by a single CEL `variables` block plus a `.all()` expression that iterates over all containers (including init and ephemeral containers, which the original missed).

**Time comparison**
- The old `time_since(...)` JMESPath function is replaced by CEL's `timestamp()` and `now()` built-ins, computing `now() - duration("4380h")` (≈ 6 months) as the cutoff.

**Dynamic error message**
- The `messageExpression` uses `.filter()` and `.map()` to list the specific offending container names and images, making violations much easier to diagnose.

runs.raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what changed and why:

**Key conversions:**

| Old (`kyverno.io/v1` ClusterPolicy) | New (`policies.kyverno.io/v1` ValidatingPolicy) |
|---|---|
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` |
| `spec.rules[].validate.foreach` with JMESPath `context` | `spec.variables` + `spec.validations` with CEL |
| `imageRegistry: reference: "{{ element.image }}"` | `image.GetMetadata(c.image)` (Kyverno ImageData CEL library) |
| `time_since('', '{{ imageData.configData.created }}', '')` (JMESPath) | `timestamp(...) > now() - duration("4380h")` (CEL) |

**Notable design decisions:**
- The `variables.allContainers` variable collects containers + initContainers + ephemeralContainers, which is more complete than the original (which only iterated `spec.containers`).
- The `image.GetMetadata(c.image).config.created == ""` guard handles images that don't publish a creation timestamp (treating them as valid rather than blocking them).
- A dynamic `messageExpression` is added to name the specific offending image(s) in the denial message, which is a best practice in the new CEL-based policy format.

      messageExpression: >-
        "Images built more than 6 months ago are prohibited. Stale container(s): " +
        variables.allContainers.filter(c,
          image.GetMetadata(c.image).config.created != "" &&
          timestamp(image.GetMetadata(c.image).config.created) <= variables.sixMonthsAgo
        ).map(c, c.name + " (" + c.image + ")").join(", ")
      message: "Images built more than 6 months ago are prohibited."

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: block-stale-images
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Block Stale Images
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.25"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Images that are old usually have some open security vulnerabilities which are not patched.
      This policy checks the contents of every container image and inspects them for the create time.
      If it finds any image which was built more than 6 months ago this policy blocks the deployment.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources: ['pods']
  variables:
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers
        + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([])
        + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
    - name: sixMonthsAgo
      expression: >-
        timestamp(string(now() - duration("4380h")))
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(c,
          image.GetMetadata(c.image).config.created == "" ||
          timestamp(image.GetMetadata(c.image).config.created) > variables.sixMonthsAgo
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        "Images built more than 6 months ago are prohibited. Stale container(s): " +
        variables.allContainers.filter(c,
          image.GetMetadata(c.image).config.created != "" &&
          timestamp(image.GetMetadata(c.image).config.created) <= variables.sixMonthsAgo
        ).map(c, c.name + " (" + c.image + ")").join(", ")
      message: "Images built more than 6 months ago are prohibited."

input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: check-nvidia-gpus
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Check NVIDIA GPUs
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Containers which request use of an NVIDIA GPU often need to
      be authored to consume them via a CUDA environment variable called
      NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES. This policy checks the containers which
      request a GPU to ensure they have been authored with this environment
      variable.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  rules:
  - name: check-nvidia-gpus
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    preconditions:
      all:
      - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
        operator: NotEquals
        value: DELETE
    validate:
      message: "Images which reserve NVIDIA GPUs must be built to use them."
      foreach:
      - list: "request.object.spec.containers"
        context: 
        - name: imageData
          imageRegistry: 
            reference: "{{ element.image }}"
        deny:
          conditions:
            all:
              # If a container image calls for an NVIDIA GPU in its resources.limits, it must also
              # have been built with the CUDA environment variable `NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES`.
              - key: "NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=*?"
                operator: AnyNotIn
                value: "{{ imageData.configData.config.Env || '' }}"
              - key: "{{ element.resources.limits.\"nvidia.com/gpu\" || '' }}"
                operator: GreaterThan
                value: 0
