yaml_preview:
# Converted from kyverno.io/v1 ClusterPolicy to policies.kyverno.io/v1 GeneratingPolicy
# for Kyverno 1.16+.
#
# NOTE: The Kyverno background-controller requires additional RBAC to create Kasten
# resources. Apply the ClusterRole below before deploying this policy.
#
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:
# - apiGroups:
#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-example-backup-policy
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Backup Policy Based on Resource Label
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24-1.30"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a namespace that includes any Deployment or
      StatefulSet with a "dataprotection=kasten-example" label, if the policy does
      not already exist. This Kyverno policy can be used in combination with the
      "kasten-data-protection-by-label" policy to require "dataprotection" labeling
      on workloads.
spec:
  evaluation:
    generateExisting:
      enabled: true
    synchronize:
      enabled: true

  # Trigger: any Deployment or StatefulSet in any namespace
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["deployments", "statefulsets"]

  # Only proceed when the resource carries the target label AND no Kasten policy
  # generated by this Kyverno policy already exists for the trigger namespace.
  matchConditions:
    # Label guard: resource must have dataprotection=kasten-example
    - name: has-dataprotection-label
      expression: >-
        has(object.metadata.labels) &&
        object.metadata.?labels[?'dataprotection'].orValue('') == 'kasten-example'
    # Idempotency guard: skip generation when a Kasten Policy for this namespace
    # (identified by the standard Kyverno owner label) already exists in kasten-io.
    - name: no-existing-kasten-policy
      expression: >-
        resource.List(
          "config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1",
          "policies",
          "kasten-io"
        ).items.filter(p,
          has(p.metadata.labels) &&
          p.metadata.?labels[?'generate.kyverno.io/policy-name'].orValue('') ==
            'kasten-generate-example-backup-policy' &&
          has(p.spec.selector.matchExpressions) &&
          p.spec.selector.matchExpressions.exists(expr,
            expr.key == 'k10.kasten.io/appNamespace' &&
            expr.operator == 'In' &&
            expr.values.exists(v, v == object.metadata.namespace)
          )
        ).size() == 0

  variables:
    - name: ns
      expression: "object.metadata.namespace"
    - name: dataprotectionLabelValue
      expression: "'kasten-example'"
    - name: policyName
      expression: "variables.ns + '-' + variables.dataprotectionLabelValue + '-policy'"
    - name: downstream
      expression: >-
        [
          {
            "apiVersion": dyn("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1"),
            "kind":       dyn("Policy"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name":      variables.policyName,
              "namespace": "kasten-io",
              "labels": dyn({
                "generate.kyverno.io/policy-name": "kasten-generate-example-backup-policy"
              })
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "comment":   "Auto-generated by Kyverno",
              "frequency": "@daily",
              "retention": dyn({
                "daily":   7,
                "weekly":  4,
                "monthly": 12,
                "yearly":  7
              }),
              "actions": dyn([
                dyn({"action": "backup"}),
                dyn({
                  "action": "export",
                  "exportParameters": dyn({
                    "frequency": "@daily",
                    "profile": dyn({
                      "name":      "test",
                      "namespace": "kasten-io"
                    }),
                    "exportData": dyn({"enabled": true})
                  })
                })
              ]),
              "selector": dyn({
                "matchExpressions": dyn([
                  dyn({
                    "key":      "k10.kasten.io/appNamespace",
                    "operator": "In",
                    "values":   dyn([variables.ns])
                  })
                ])
              })
            })
          }
        ]

  # Generate the Kasten Policy in the kasten-io namespace
  generate:
    - expression: "generator.Apply('kasten-io', variables.downstream)"
input_snippet:
# This is an example rule intended to be cloned & modified to meet organizational requirements.
# The `dataprotetion` label value can be changed to correspond with specific policy templates.
# 
# NOTE: Use of this policy will require granting the Kyverno background-controller additional privileges required to generate Kasten resources. An example ClusterRole to provide required privileges is provided within the comments of the policy manifest.
#
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:
# - apiGroups:
#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-example-backup-policy
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Backup Policy Based on Resource Label
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.12.1
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.12.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24-1.30"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a namespace that includes any Deployment or StatefulSet with a "dataprotection=kasten-example" label, if the policy does not already exist. This Kyverno policy can be used in combination with the "kasten-data-protection-by-label" policy to require "dataprotection" labeling on workloads.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: kasten-generate-example-backup-policy
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
            - Deployment
            - StatefulSet
          selector:
            matchLabels:
              dataprotection: kasten-example
    context:
    - name: dataprotectionLabelValue
      variable:
        value: "kasten-example"
    - name: kyvernoPolicyName
      variable:
        value: "kasten-generate-example-backup-policy"
    - name: existingPolicy
      apiCall:
        urlPath: "/apis/config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1/namespaces/kasten-io/policies" # returns list of Kasten policies from kasten-io namespace
        jmesPath: "items[][[@.metadata.labels.\"generate.kyverno.io/policy-name\"=='{{ kyvernoPolicyName }}'] && [@.spec.selector.matchExpressions[].values[?@=='{{ request.namespace }}']]][][][][] | length(@)" # queries if a Kasten policy protecting the namespace generated by this Kyverno policy already exists 
    preconditions:
      any:
      - key: "{{ existingPolicy }}"
        operator: Equals
        value: 0 # Only generate the policy if it does not already exist
    generate:
      apiVersion: config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1
      kind: Policy
      name: "{{ request.namespace }}-{{ dataprotectionLabelValue }}-policy"
      namespace: kasten-io
      data:   
        metadata: 
          name: "{{ request.namespace }}-{{ dataprotectionLabelValue }}-policy"
          namespace: kasten-io
        spec:
          comment: "Auto-generated by Kyverno"
          frequency: '@daily'
          retention:
            daily: 7
            weekly: 4
            monthly: 12
            yearly: 7
          actions:
          - action: backup
          - action: export
            exportParameters:
              frequency: '@daily'
              profile:
                name: test
                namespace: kasten-io
              exportData:
                enabled: true
          selector:
            matchExpressions:
              - key: k10.kasten.io/appNamespace
                operator: In
                values:
                  - "{{ request.namespace }}"

raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key translation decisions:

**Schema changes**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: GeneratingPolicy`
- The old `spec.rules[].match` / `spec.rules[].generate` structure is replaced by top-level `matchConstraints`, `matchConditions`, `variables`, and `generate` fields.

**Trigger matching**
- `matchConstraints.resourceRules` targets `apps/v1` Deployments and StatefulSets (CREATE or UPDATE), replacing the old `match.any[].resources` block.

**Label selector & preconditions → `matchConditions` (CEL)**
- The old `selector.matchLabels: { dataprotection: kasten-example }` becomes a CEL guard (`has-dataprotection-label`) that checks `object.metadata.labels`.
- The old `context.apiCall` + `preconditions` idempotency check is replaced by a second `matchCondition` (`no-existing-kasten-policy`) that calls `resource.List()` to query existing Kasten policies in `kasten-io` and ensures none already covers the trigger namespace.

**Context variables → CEL `variables`**
- Named variables use CEL string expressions and reference each other via `variables.<name>`.

**`generate` block**
- Uses `generator.Apply('kasten-io', variables.downstream)` — the first argument is the target namespace (`kasten-io`), the second is the list of downstream resource objects defined inline with `dyn()`.
- A `generate.kyverno.io/policy-name` label is added to the downstream Kasten Policy so the idempotency guard can reliably identify it in future checks.

**Lifecycle settings**
- `evaluation.generateExisting.enabled: true` — retroactively applies to existing matching workloads.
- `evaluation.synchronize.enabled: true` — keeps the generated Kasten Policy in sync if the Kyverno policy is updated.

runs.yaml_preview:
# Converted from kyverno.io/v1 ClusterPolicy to policies.kyverno.io/v1 GeneratingPolicy
# (Kyverno 1.17+, stable in v1.17)
#
# NOTE: Use of this policy requires granting the Kyverno background-controller additional
# privileges to generate Kasten resources. An example ClusterRole is provided below.
#
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:
# - apiGroups:
#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-example-backup-policy
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Backup Policy Based on Resource Label
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24-1.30"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a namespace that includes any Deployment or StatefulSet
      with a "dataprotection=kasten-example" label, if the policy does not already exist.
      This Kyverno policy can be used in combination with the "kasten-data-protection-by-label"
      policy to require "dataprotection" labeling on workloads.
spec:
  evaluation:
    generateExisting:
      enabled: true
    synchronize:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["Deployment", "StatefulSet"]
  matchConditions:
    # Only trigger when the resource has the dataprotection=kasten-example label
    - name: has-dataprotection-label
      expression: >-
        has(object.metadata.labels) &&
        object.metadata.?labels[?"dataprotection"].orValue("") == "kasten-example"
    # Only generate if a Kasten policy for this namespace does not already exist
    - name: kasten-policy-does-not-exist
      expression: >-
        !resource.List(
          "config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1",
          "policies",
          "kasten-io"
        ).items.exists(p,
          has(p.metadata.labels) &&
          p.metadata.?labels[?"generate.kyverno.io/policy-name"].orValue("") == "kasten-generate-example-backup-policy" &&
          p.spec.?selector.?matchExpressions.orValue([]).exists(expr,
            expr.key == "k10.kasten.io/appNamespace" &&
            expr.operator == "In" &&
            expr.values.exists(v, v == object.metadata.namespace)
          )
        )
  variables:
    - name: targetNamespace
      expression: "object.metadata.namespace"
    - name: policyName
      expression: "object.metadata.namespace + '-kasten-example-policy'"
    - name: downstream
      expression: >-
        [
          {
            "kind": dyn("Policy"),
            "apiVersion": dyn("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name": string(variables.policyName),
              "namespace": "kasten-io",
              "labels": dyn({
                "generate.kyverno.io/policy-name": "kasten-generate-example-backup-policy"
              })
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "comment": "Auto-generated by Kyverno",
              "frequency": "@daily",
              "retention": dyn({
                "daily": 7,
                "weekly": 4,
                "monthly": 12,
                "yearly": 7
              }),
              "actions": dyn([
                dyn({"action": "backup"}),
                dyn({
                  "action": "export",
                  "exportParameters": dyn({
                    "frequency": "@daily",
                    "profile": dyn({
                      "name": "test",
                      "namespace": "kasten-io"
                    }),
                    "exportData": dyn({
                      "enabled": true
                    })
                  })
                })
              ]),
              "selector": dyn({
                "matchExpressions": dyn([
                  dyn({
                    "key": "k10.kasten.io/appNamespace",
                    "operator": "In",
                    "values": dyn([string(variables.targetNamespace)])
                  })
                ])
              })
            })
          }
        ]
  generate:
    - expression: generator.Apply("kasten-io", variables.downstream)

runs.raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key changes made during conversion:

**API changes:**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1`
- `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `kind: GeneratingPolicy`
- Updated `kyverno-version` and `minversion` annotations to `1.17.0`

**Structural changes (JMESPath → CEL):**

| Old (`ClusterPolicy`) | New (`GeneratingPolicy`) |
|---|---|
| `spec.rules[].match.any[]` with `selector.matchLabels` | `matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` + `matchConditions[]` |
| `spec.rules[].context[]` with `variable` / `apiCall` | `spec.variables[]` with CEL `expression` |
| `spec.rules[].preconditions` (JMESPath) | `matchConditions[]` (CEL) |
| `spec.rules[].generate` block (JMESPath templating) | `spec.generate[]` with `generator.Apply()` |

**Logic preservation:**
- The `matchConditions` CEL expression filters for `dataprotection=kasten-example` label on Deployment/StatefulSet triggers
- The "policy does not already exist" check from the old `preconditions`/`apiCall` is reproduced as a `matchCondition` using `resource.List()` on the `kasten-io` namespace, checking both the `generate.kyverno.io/policy-name` label and the `matchExpressions` selector for the namespace
- The `evaluation.generateExisting.enabled: true` flag ensures the policy is applied to pre-existing matching workloads, mirroring the original `background: true` default
- `evaluation.synchronize.enabled: true` keeps generated Kasten policies in sync with the triggering resource lifecycle
- The generated Kasten `Policy` resource structure (frequency, retention, actions, export, selector) is preserved verbatim

spec:

                "monthly": 12,
                "yearly": 7
              }),
              "actions": dyn([
                {"action": "backup"},
                {
                  "action": "export",
                  "exportParameters": dyn({
                    "frequency": "@daily",
                    "profile": dyn({

The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key changes and design decisions:

**API/Kind changes:**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1`
- `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `kind: GeneratingPolicy`

**Structure changes (`spec`):**

| Old (`ClusterPolicy`) | New (`GeneratingPolicy`) |
|---|---|
| `spec.rules[].match` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` |
| `spec.rules[].context` (JMESPath `apiCall`) | `spec.variables[]` using CEL `resource.List(...)` |
| `spec.rules[].preconditions` | Inline conditional in `generate[].expression` |
| `spec.rules[].generate` | `spec.generate[].expression` using `generator.Apply(namespace, resources)` |
| Label selector in `match.any[].resources.selector` | `spec.matchConditions[]` CEL expression |

**Key CEL patterns used:**
- `matchConditions` filters for the `dataprotection=kasten-example` label using `object.metadata.labels.?dataprotection.orValue("") == "kasten-example"`
- `resource.List(...)` fetches existing Kasten policies to check for duplicates (replacing the old `apiCall` + JMESPath approach)
- The "only generate if not already exists" precondition becomes a ternary in the `generate` expression: `variables.existingKastenPolicies.size() == 0 ? generator.Apply(...) : true`
- Resource bodies use `dyn(...)` wrappers as required by the GeneratingPolicy CEL data source mode
- `evaluation.generateExisting.enabled: true` ensures the policy applies to Deployments/StatefulSets that already exist when the policy is first applied

# Converted from kyverno.io/v1 ClusterPolicy to policies.kyverno.io/v1 GeneratingPolicy
# for Kyverno 1.16+.
#
# NOTE: The Kyverno background-controller requires additional RBAC to create Kasten
# resources. Apply the ClusterRole below before deploying this policy.
#
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:
# - apiGroups:
#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-example-backup-policy
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Backup Policy Based on Resource Label
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24-1.30"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a namespace that includes any Deployment or
      StatefulSet with a "dataprotection=kasten-example" label, if the policy does
      not already exist. This Kyverno policy can be used in combination with the
      "kasten-data-protection-by-label" policy to require "dataprotection" labeling
      on workloads.
spec:
  evaluation:
    generateExisting:
      enabled: true
    synchronize:
      enabled: true

  # Trigger: any Deployment or StatefulSet in any namespace
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["deployments", "statefulsets"]

  # Only proceed when the resource carries the target label AND no Kasten policy
  # generated by this Kyverno policy already exists for the trigger namespace.
  matchConditions:
    # Label guard: resource must have dataprotection=kasten-example
    - name: has-dataprotection-label
      expression: >-
        has(object.metadata.labels) &&
        object.metadata.?labels[?'dataprotection'].orValue('') == 'kasten-example'
    # Idempotency guard: skip generation when a Kasten Policy for this namespace
    # (identified by the standard Kyverno owner label) already exists in kasten-io.
    - name: no-existing-kasten-policy
      expression: >-
        resource.List(
          "config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1",
          "policies",
          "kasten-io"
        ).items.filter(p,
          has(p.metadata.labels) &&
          p.metadata.?labels[?'generate.kyverno.io/policy-name'].orValue('') ==
            'kasten-generate-example-backup-policy' &&
          has(p.spec.selector.matchExpressions) &&
          p.spec.selector.matchExpressions.exists(expr,
            expr.key == 'k10.kasten.io/appNamespace' &&
            expr.operator == 'In' &&
            expr.values.exists(v, v == object.metadata.namespace)
          )
        ).size() == 0

  variables:
    - name: ns
      expression: "object.metadata.namespace"
    - name: dataprotectionLabelValue
      expression: "'kasten-example'"
    - name: policyName
      expression: "variables.ns + '-' + variables.dataprotectionLabelValue + '-policy'"
    - name: downstream
      expression: >-
        [
          {
            "apiVersion": dyn("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1"),
            "kind":       dyn("Policy"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name":      variables.policyName,
              "namespace": "kasten-io",
              "labels": dyn({
                "generate.kyverno.io/policy-name": "kasten-generate-example-backup-policy"
              })
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "comment":   "Auto-generated by Kyverno",
              "frequency": "@daily",
              "retention": dyn({
                "daily":   7,
                "weekly":  4,
                "monthly": 12,
                "yearly":  7
              }),
              "actions": dyn([
                dyn({"action": "backup"}),
                dyn({
                  "action": "export",
                  "exportParameters": dyn({
                    "frequency": "@daily",
                    "profile": dyn({
                      "name":      "test",
                      "namespace": "kasten-io"
                    }),
                    "exportData": dyn({"enabled": true})
                  })
                })
              ]),
              "selector": dyn({
                "matchExpressions": dyn([
                  dyn({
                    "key":      "k10.kasten.io/appNamespace",
                    "operator": "In",
                    "values":   dyn([variables.ns])
                  })
                ])
              })
            })
          }
        ]

  # Generate the Kasten Policy in the kasten-io namespace
  generate:
    - expression: "generator.Apply('kasten-io', variables.downstream)"

runs.raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key translation decisions:

**Schema changes**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: GeneratingPolicy`
- The old `spec.rules[].match` / `spec.rules[].generate` structure is replaced by top-level `matchConstraints`, `matchConditions`, `variables`, and `generate` fields.

**Trigger matching**
- `matchConstraints.resourceRules` targets `apps/v1` Deployments and StatefulSets (CREATE or UPDATE), replacing the old `match.any[].resources` block.

**Label selector & preconditions → `matchConditions` (CEL)**
- The old `selector.matchLabels: { dataprotection: kasten-example }` becomes a CEL guard (`has-dataprotection-label`) that checks `object.metadata.labels`.
- The old `context.apiCall` + `preconditions` idempotency check is replaced by a second `matchCondition` (`no-existing-kasten-policy`) that calls `resource.List()` to query existing Kasten policies in `kasten-io` and ensures none already covers the trigger namespace.

**Context variables → CEL `variables`**
- Named variables use CEL string expressions and reference each other via `variables.<name>`.

**`generate` block**
- Uses `generator.Apply('kasten-io', variables.downstream)` — the first argument is the target namespace (`kasten-io`), the second is the list of downstream resource objects defined inline with `dyn()`.
- A `generate.kyverno.io/policy-name` label is added to the downstream Kasten Policy so the idempotency guard can reliably identify it in future checks.

**Lifecycle settings**
- `evaluation.generateExisting.enabled: true` — retroactively applies to existing matching workloads.
- `evaluation.synchronize.enabled: true` — keeps the generated Kasten Policy in sync if the Kyverno policy is updated.

yaml_preview:
# This example assumes that Kasten policy presets named "gold", "silver", and "bronze" have been pre-created and Kasten was deployed into the `kasten-io` namespace.
#
# NOTE: Use of this policy will require granting the Kyverno background-controller additional privileges required to generate Kasten resources. An example ClusterRole to provide required privileges is provided below.
#
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:
# - apiGroups:
#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-policy-by-preset-label
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Policy from Preset
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.27+"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a new namespace that includes a valid "dataprotection" label, if the policy does not already exist.
      Use with "kasten-validate-ns-by-preset-label" policy to require "dataprotection" labeling on new namespaces.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE']
        resources: ['namespaces']
  variables:
    - name: nsName
      expression: 'object.metadata.name'
    - name: presetName
      expression: "object.metadata.labels['dataprotection']"
    # List existing Kasten policies in the kasten-io namespace to check whether one already
    # exists for this namespace + preset combination
    - name: existingPolicies
      expression: >-
        resource.List("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1", "policies", "kasten-io").items
    - name: existingPolicyCount
      expression: >-
        variables.existingPolicies.filter(p,
          has(p.spec) &&
          has(p.spec.presetRef) &&
          p.spec.presetRef.name == variables.presetName &&
          has(p.spec.selector) &&
          has(p.spec.selector.matchExpressions) &&
          p.spec.selector.matchExpressions.exists(expr,
            expr.key == 'k10.kasten.io/appNamespace' &&
            expr.operator == 'In' &&
            variables.nsName in expr.values
          )
        ).size()
  # matchConditions act as preconditions: only run when both conditions are true
  matchConditions:
    # Trigger only for namespaces labelled with a valid "dataprotection" value
    - name: has-valid-dataprotection-label
      expression: >-
        has(object.metadata.labels) &&
        'dataprotection' in object.metadata.labels &&
        object.metadata.labels['dataprotection'] in ['gold', 'silver', 'bronze']
    # Only generate when no Kasten policy already covers this namespace + preset
    - name: policy-does-not-exist
      expression: 'variables.existingPolicyCount == 0'
  generate:
    - expression: >-
        generator.Apply("kasten-io", [
          {
            "kind": dyn("Policy"),
            "apiVersion": dyn("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name": variables.nsName + "-" + variables.presetName + "-backup",
              "namespace": "kasten-io"
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "comment": "Auto-generated by Kyverno",
              "paused": false,
              "actions": [
                {"action": "backup"}
              ],
              "presetRef": dyn({
                "name": variables.presetName,
                "namespace": "kasten-io"
              }),
              "selector": dyn({
                "matchExpressions": [
                  {
                    "key": "k10.kasten.io/appNamespace",
                    "operator": "In",
                    "values": [variables.nsName]
                  }
                ]
              })
            })
          }
        ])
input_snippet:
# This example assumes that Kasten policy presets named "gold", "silver", and "bronze" have been pre-created and Kasten was deployed into the `kasten-io` namespace.
#
# NOTE: Use of this policy will require granting the Kyverno background-controller additional privileges required to generate Kasten resources. An example ClusterRole to provide required privileges is provided within the comments of the policy manifest.
#
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:
# - apiGroups:
#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-policy-by-preset-label
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Policy from Preset
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.12.1
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.12.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24-1.30"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a new namespace that includes a valid "dataprotection" label, if the policy does not already exist.
      Use with "kasten-validate-ns-by-preset-label" policy to require "dataprotection" labeling on new namespaces.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: kasten-generate-policy-by-preset-label
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
            - Namespace
          selector:
            matchExpressions:
              - key: dataprotection 
                operator: In
                values: 
                - gold
                - silver
                - bronze
    context:
    - name: existingPolicy
      apiCall:
        urlPath: "/apis/config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1/namespaces/kasten-io/policies" # returns list of Kasten policies from kasten-io namespace
        jmesPath: "items[][[@.spec.presetRef][?name=='{{ request.object.metadata.labels.dataprotection }}'] && [@.spec.selector.matchExpressions[].values[?@=='{{ request.namespace }}']]][][][][] | length(@)" # queries if a policy based on the dataprotection label value, covering that app namespace already exists 
    preconditions:
      any:
      - key: "{{ existingPolicy }}"
        operator: Equals
        value: 0 # Only generate the policy if it does not already exist
    generate:
      apiVersion: config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1
      kind: Policy
      name: "{{ request.namespace }}-{{ request.object.metadata.labels.dataprotection }}-backup"
      namespace: kasten-io
      data:
        spec:
          comment: "Auto-generated by Kyverno"
          paused: false
          actions:
            - action: backup
          presetRef:
            name: "{{ request.object.metadata.labels.dataprotection }}"
            namespace: kasten-io
          selector:
            matchExpressions:
              - key: k10.kasten.io/appNamespace
                operator: In
                values:
                  - "{{ request.namespace }}"

                    "operator": "In",

The converted policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of all the changes made and why:

## What changed and why

**`apiVersion` / `kind`**
- `kyverno.io/v1` / `ClusterPolicy` → `policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `GeneratingPolicy`
- The new dedicated `GeneratingPolicy` kind is the 1.17-stable, CEL-first replacement for generate rules inside `ClusterPolicy`.

**`spec.rules[].match` → `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules`**
- The old `match.any[].resources` block becomes a standard `matchConstraints.resourceRules` entry (same shape as Kubernetes `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy`).
- The label selector (`matchExpressions` for `dataprotection in [gold, silver, bronze]`) is moved out of the resource selector and into `matchConditions` as a CEL expression, because the new API uses CEL for all conditional logic.

**`spec.rules[].context` (JMESPath API call) → `spec.variables` (CEL `resource.List()`)**
- The JMESPath `apiCall` that queried existing Kasten policies is replaced with two CEL variables:
  - `existingPolicies` — uses `resource.List()` to fetch all Kasten `Policy` objects from the `kasten-io` namespace.
  - `existingPolicyCount` — filters the list with CEL to count policies whose `presetRef.name` matches the label value and whose selector covers the incoming namespace.

**`spec.rules[].preconditions` → `spec.matchConditions`**
- The old `preconditions.any` block (JMESPath `Equals 0`) becomes two `matchConditions` CEL entries:
  1. `has-valid-dataprotection-label` — guards that the label is present and valid before the variables that read it are evaluated.
  2. `policy-does-not-exist` — checks `variables.existingPolicyCount == 0`, faithfully replicating the original precondition.

**`spec.rules[].generate` → `spec.generate[].expression` with `generator.Apply()`**
- The old `generate` block (static YAML overlay) becomes a `generator.Apply("kasten-io", [...])` CEL expression. The downstream `Policy` object is constructed inline using `dyn()` for all non-scalar fields, as required by the CEL type system. All JMESPath template references (`{{ request.namespace }}`, `{{ request.object.metadata.labels.dataprotection }}`) are replaced with CEL variable references (`variables.nsName`, `variables.presetName`).

runs.yaml_preview:
# This example assumes that Kasten policy presets named "gold", "silver", and "bronze" have been
# pre-created and Kasten was deployed into the `kasten-io` namespace.
#
# NOTE: Use of this policy will require granting the Kyverno background-controller additional
# privileges required to generate Kasten resources. An example ClusterRole to provide required
# privileges is provided below.
#
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:
# - apiGroups:
#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-policy-by-preset-label
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Policy from Preset
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24-1.30"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a new namespace that includes a valid "dataprotection" label,
      if the policy does not already exist.
      Use with "kasten-validate-ns-by-preset-label" policy to require "dataprotection" labeling
      on new namespaces.
spec:
  evaluation:
    synchronize:
      enabled: true
  # Trigger: any Namespace creation or update
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources: ['namespaces']
  # Only run when the namespace carries a valid dataprotection preset label
  matchConditions:
    - name: has-valid-dataprotection-label
      expression: >-
        has(object.metadata.labels) &&
        has(object.metadata.labels.dataprotection) &&
        object.metadata.labels.dataprotection in ['gold', 'silver', 'bronze']
  variables:
    # The name of the triggering namespace (the app namespace to protect)
    - name: appNamespace
      expression: "object.metadata.name"
    # The dataprotection preset value from the namespace label
    - name: preset
      expression: "object.metadata.labels.dataprotection"
    # Fetch all existing Kasten policies from kasten-io to check for duplicates
    - name: existingPolicies
      expression: >-
        resource.List("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1", "policies", "kasten-io")
    # Check whether a Kasten policy already exists for this (namespace, preset) combination.
    # A match requires both: the presetRef.name equals the preset label value AND
    # the selector covers the app namespace.
    - name: policyExists
      expression: >-
        variables.existingPolicies.items.exists(p,
          has(p.spec) &&
          has(p.spec.presetRef) && p.spec.presetRef.name == variables.preset &&
          has(p.spec.selector) &&
          has(p.spec.selector.matchExpressions) &&
          p.spec.selector.matchExpressions.exists(expr,
            expr.key == "k10.kasten.io/appNamespace" &&
            expr.operator == "In" &&
            expr.values.exists(v, v == variables.appNamespace)
          )
        )
    # The downstream Kasten Policy resource to generate, constructed as a CEL object.
    # It is placed in the `kasten-io` namespace (where Kasten lives), not in the app namespace.
    - name: downstream
      expression: >-
        [
          {
            "kind": dyn("Policy"),
            "apiVersion": dyn("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name": variables.appNamespace + "-" + variables.preset + "-backup",
              "namespace": "kasten-io"
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "comment": "Auto-generated by Kyverno",
              "paused": false,
              "actions": dyn([
                dyn({"action": "backup"})
              ]),
              "presetRef": dyn({
                "name": variables.preset,
                "namespace": "kasten-io"
              }),
              "selector": dyn({
                "matchExpressions": dyn([
                  dyn({
                    "key": "k10.kasten.io/appNamespace",
                    "operator": "In",
                    "values": dyn([variables.appNamespace])
                  })
                ])
              })
            })
          }
        ]
  generate:
    # Only generate the Kasten policy if one does not already exist for this
    # (namespace, preset) combination. The resource is placed in kasten-io.
    - expression: >-
        !variables.policyExists ?
          generator.Apply("kasten-io", variables.downstream) :
          true

runs.raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what changed and why:

**API/Kind change:**
- `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ClusterPolicy` → `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: GeneratingPolicy`

**Trigger matching (CEL-first approach):**
- `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources` → `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules[]` (Kubernetes-native VAP style)
- The `labelSelector` (matching namespaces with `dataprotection in [gold, silver, bronze]`) → `matchConditions` using a CEL `in` expression — this replaces both the old `match.selector.matchExpressions` and the `preconditions` block

**Context/API call → `variables` with `resource.List()`:**
- The old `context[].apiCall` (JMESPath query against the Kasten policies API) is replaced by `resource.List("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1", "policies", "kasten-io")` stored in a variable, followed by a CEL `exists()` expression (`policyExists`) that replicates the original JMESPath duplicate-detection logic

**Generate block:**
- Old `generate.data` (static YAML map + JMESPath templating) → a `downstream` variable holding a CEL-constructed object using `dyn()` wrappers (required for non-schema-known types), with dynamic values referencing `variables.appNamespace` and `variables.preset`
- `generator.Apply("kasten-io", variables.downstream)` replaces the old `generate.namespace/name/data` fields; the ternary `!variables.policyExists ? generator.Apply(...) : true` preserves the "only generate if not already existing" precondition

**`evaluation.synchronize`** is enabled to keep generated Kasten policies in sync with policy/trigger changes.

        has(object.metadata.labels.dataprotection) &&
        object.metadata.labels.dataprotection in ["gold", "silver", "bronze"]
    - name: kasten-policy-does-not-exist
      expression: >-
        !resource.List(
          "config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1",
          "policies",
          "kasten-io"
        ).items.exists(p,
          has(p.spec.presetRef) &&
          p.spec.presetRef.name == object.metadata.labels.dataprotection &&
          has(p.spec.selector) &&
          has(p.spec.selector.matchExpressions) &&
          p.spec.selector.matchExpressions.exists(e,
            e.key == "k10.kasten.io/appNamespace" &&
            e.operator == "In" &&
            e.values.exists(v, v == object.metadata.name)
          )
        )

  variables:
    - name: nsName
      expression: "object.metadata.name"
    - name: preset
      expression: "object.metadata.labels.dataprotection"
    - name: downstream
      expression: >-
        [
          {
            "kind": dyn("Policy"),
            "apiVersion": dyn("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name": string(variables.nsName) + "-" + string(variables.preset) + "-backup",
              "namespace": "kasten-io"
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "comment": "Auto-generated by Kyverno",
              "paused": false,
              "actions": [{"action": "backup"}],
              "presetRef": dyn({
                "name": string(variables.preset),
                "namespace": "kasten-io"
              }),
              "selector": dyn({
                "matchExpressions": [
                  {
                    "key": "k10.kasten.io/appNamespace",
                    "operator": "In",
                    "values": [string(variables.nsName)]
                  }
                ]
              })
            })
          }
        ]

  generate:
    - expression: "generator.Apply('kasten-io', variables.downstream)"
runs.raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of all the translation decisions made:

## Conversion Summary

| Original (`kyverno.io/v1` ClusterPolicy) | Converted (`policies.kyverno.io/v1` GeneratingPolicy) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: GeneratingPolicy` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources` with label `selector.matchExpressions` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` (kind/apiVersion/operations) + `matchConditions` CEL for label filtering |
| `context[].apiCall` (JMESPath query for existing Kasten policies) | `matchConditions` CEL using `resource.List("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1", "policies", "kasten-io")` |
| `preconditions` (existingPolicy == 0) | Folded into the `kasten-policy-does-not-exist` `matchCondition` — the policy only fires when the `!...exists(...)` check is true |
| `generate.data` with JMESPath variable interpolation | `variables.downstream` CEL list with `dyn()` typed fields + `generate[].expression: generator.Apply('kasten-io', variables.downstream)` |

**Key design points:**
- **`matchConditions`** replaces both the label `selector` match filter and the `preconditions` check for an existing policy — the policy skips execution entirely if either condition is false
- **`resource.List()`** (CEL library) replaces the `apiCall` context, querying existing Kasten policies in `kasten-io` namespace and checking for a matching `presetRef.name` and namespace selector
- **`generator.Apply('kasten-io', ...)`** generates the resource directly into the `kasten-io` namespace (the Kasten Policy is namespace-scoped there, not in the triggering namespace)
- The generated resource name preserves the original pattern: `<nsName>-<preset>-backup`

runs.yaml_preview:
# This example assumes that Kasten policy presets named "gold", "silver", and "bronze" have been pre-created and Kasten was deployed into the `kasten-io` namespace.
#
# NOTE: Use of this policy will require granting the Kyverno background-controller additional privileges required to generate Kasten resources. An example ClusterRole to provide required privileges is provided below.
#
# apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
# kind: ClusterRole
# metadata:
#   labels:
#     app.kubernetes.io/component: background-controller
#     app.kubernetes.io/instance: kyverno
#     app.kubernetes.io/part-of: kyverno
#   name: kyverno:create-kasten-policies
# rules:
# - apiGroups:
#   - config.kio.kasten.io
#   resources:
#   - policies
#   verbs:
#   - create
#   - update
#   - delete
#
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  name: kasten-generate-policy-by-preset-label
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Generate Kasten Policy from Preset
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Veeam Kasten
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Policy
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.27+"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a Kasten policy for a new namespace that includes a valid "dataprotection" label, if the policy does not already exist.
      Use with "kasten-validate-ns-by-preset-label" policy to require "dataprotection" labeling on new namespaces.
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE']
        resources: ['namespaces']
  variables:
    - name: nsName
      expression: 'object.metadata.name'
    - name: presetName
      expression: "object.metadata.labels['dataprotection']"
    # List existing Kasten policies in the kasten-io namespace to check whether one already
    # exists for this namespace + preset combination
    - name: existingPolicies
      expression: >-
        resource.List("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1", "policies", "kasten-io").items
    - name: existingPolicyCount
      expression: >-
        variables.existingPolicies.filter(p,
          has(p.spec) &&
          has(p.spec.presetRef) &&
          p.spec.presetRef.name == variables.presetName &&
          has(p.spec.selector) &&
          has(p.spec.selector.matchExpressions) &&
          p.spec.selector.matchExpressions.exists(expr,
            expr.key == 'k10.kasten.io/appNamespace' &&
            expr.operator == 'In' &&
            variables.nsName in expr.values
          )
        ).size()
  # matchConditions act as preconditions: only run when both conditions are true
  matchConditions:
    # Trigger only for namespaces labelled with a valid "dataprotection" value
    - name: has-valid-dataprotection-label
      expression: >-
        has(object.metadata.labels) &&
        'dataprotection' in object.metadata.labels &&
        object.metadata.labels['dataprotection'] in ['gold', 'silver', 'bronze']
    # Only generate when no Kasten policy already covers this namespace + preset
    - name: policy-does-not-exist
      expression: 'variables.existingPolicyCount == 0'
  generate:
    - expression: >-
        generator.Apply("kasten-io", [
          {
            "kind": dyn("Policy"),
            "apiVersion": dyn("config.kio.kasten.io/v1alpha1"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name": variables.nsName + "-" + variables.presetName + "-backup",
              "namespace": "kasten-io"
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "comment": "Auto-generated by Kyverno",
              "paused": false,
              "actions": [
                {"action": "backup"}
              ],
              "presetRef": dyn({
                "name": variables.presetName,
                "namespace": "kasten-io"
              }),
              "selector": dyn({
                "matchExpressions": [
                  {
                    "key": "k10.kasten.io/appNamespace",
                    "operator": "In",
                    "values": [variables.nsName]
                  }
                ]
              })
            })
          }
        ])

runs.raw_log:
The converted policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of all the changes made and why:

## What changed and why

**`apiVersion` / `kind`**
- `kyverno.io/v1` / `ClusterPolicy` → `policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `GeneratingPolicy`
- The new dedicated `GeneratingPolicy` kind is the 1.17-stable, CEL-first replacement for generate rules inside `ClusterPolicy`.

**`spec.rules[].match` → `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules`**
- The old `match.any[].resources` block becomes a standard `matchConstraints.resourceRules` entry (same shape as Kubernetes `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy`).
- The label selector (`matchExpressions` for `dataprotection in [gold, silver, bronze]`) is moved out of the resource selector and into `matchConditions` as a CEL expression, because the new API uses CEL for all conditional logic.

**`spec.rules[].context` (JMESPath API call) → `spec.variables` (CEL `resource.List()`)**
- The JMESPath `apiCall` that queried existing Kasten policies is replaced with two CEL variables:
  - `existingPolicies` — uses `resource.List()` to fetch all Kasten `Policy` objects from the `kasten-io` namespace.
  - `existingPolicyCount` — filters the list with CEL to count policies whose `presetRef.name` matches the label value and whose selector covers the incoming namespace.

**`spec.rules[].preconditions` → `spec.matchConditions`**
- The old `preconditions.any` block (JMESPath `Equals 0`) becomes two `matchConditions` CEL entries:
  1. `has-valid-dataprotection-label` — guards that the label is present and valid before the variables that read it are evaluated.
  2. `policy-does-not-exist` — checks `variables.existingPolicyCount == 0`, faithfully replicating the original precondition.

**`spec.rules[].generate` → `spec.generate[].expression` with `generator.Apply()`**
- The old `generate` block (static YAML overlay) becomes a `generator.Apply("kasten-io", [...])` CEL expression. The downstream `Policy` object is constructed inline using `dyn()` for all non-scalar fields, as required by the CEL type system. All JMESPath template references (`{{ request.namespace }}`, `{{ request.object.metadata.labels.dataprotection }}`) are replaced with CEL variable references (`variables.nsName`, `variables.presetName`).

description: this policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific ConfigMaps by ServiceAccounts
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_limit_configmap_for_sa.yaml to a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy this policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific ConfigMaps by ServiceAccounts. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/cp_limit_configmap_for_sa.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: limit-configmap-for-sa
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Limit ConfigMap to ServiceAccounts for a User
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.20-1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: ConfigMap, ServiceAccount
    policies.kyverno.io/description: This policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific ConfigMaps by ServiceAccounts.
spec:
  background: false
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  rules:
  - name: limit-configmap-for-sa-developer
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - ConfigMap
        # subjects:
        # - kind: ServiceAccount
        #   name: developer
        #   namespace: kube-system
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - ConfigMap
        subjects:
        - kind: ServiceAccount
          name: another-developer
          namespace: another-namespace
    preconditions:
      all:
      - key: "{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}"
        operator: AnyIn
        value:
        - "any-namespace"
        - "another-namespace"
      - key: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
        operator: AnyIn
        value:
        - "any-configmap-name-good"
        - "another-configmap-name"
    validate:
      message: "{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}/{{request.object.kind}}/{{request.object.metadata.name}} resource is protected. Admin or allowed users can change the resource"
      deny:
        conditions:
          all:
          - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
            operator: AnyIn
            value:
            - UPDATE
            - CREATE
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: limit-configmap-for-sa
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Limit ConfigMap to ServiceAccounts for a User
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: ConfigMap, ServiceAccount
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      This policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific
      ConfigMaps by ServiceAccounts.
spec:
  # Converted from validationFailureAction: Audit
  validationActions:
    - Audit

  # Converted from background: false
  evaluation:
    admission:
      enabled: true
    background:
      enabled: false

  # Matches ConfigMap CREATE and UPDATE operations, translating the
  # `match.any[*].resources.kinds: [ConfigMap]` entries from the original
  # ClusterPolicy rule.
  #
  # The original match.any had two branches:
  #   - Branch 1: any ConfigMap (ServiceAccount subject block was commented out,
  #     meaning no subject restriction — all requestors are matched)
  #   - Branch 2: ConfigMaps accessed by ServiceAccount "another-developer" in
  #     "another-namespace"
  # Because branch 1 is unrestricted, the union covers all requestors and no
  # additional subject-based matchCondition is needed here.
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources: ['configmaps']

  # Translates the original `preconditions.all` block.
  # Requests not meeting all conditions are skipped (not evaluated by
  # validations), equivalent to preconditions not being satisfied in the
  # ClusterPolicy engine.
  matchConditions:
    - name: target-namespace
      expression: >-
        object.metadata.namespace in ["any-namespace", "another-namespace"]
    - name: target-configmap-name
      expression: >-
        object.metadata.name in ["any-configmap-name-good", "another-configmap-name"]

  validations:
    # The original validate.deny rule denied when operation was CREATE or UPDATE.
    # matchConstraints already restricts to those operations, so the validation
    # unconditionally denies every matched request (expression: false).
    - expression: "false"
      messageExpression: >-
        object.metadata.namespace + "/" + object.kind + "/" + object.metadata.name +
        " resource is protected. Admin or allowed users can change the resource"
raw_log:
The converted policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every conversion decision made:

---

**Conversion mapping**

| Original (`kyverno.io/v1 ClusterPolicy`) | Converted (`policies.kyverno.io/v1 ValidatingPolicy`) | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` | New API group for Kyverno 1.16+ policy types |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` | Cluster-scoped validating policy |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` | Direct equivalent; list form per new API |
| `spec.background: false` | `spec.evaluation.admission.enabled: true` + `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: false` | Structured evaluation configuration replaces the flat boolean |
| `match.any[*].resources.kinds: [ConfigMap]` + `operations` (implicit admission) | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` with `apiGroups: ['']`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]`, `resources: [configmaps]` | `ValidatingPolicy` uses the `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy` `matchConstraints` structure; operations moved here from the deny conditions |
| `match.any` branches (one unrestricted + one SA-scoped) | No `matchConditions` needed for subject | Branch 1 had its subject block commented out, making it unrestricted. The union of both branches covers all requestors, so no subject filter is required |
| `preconditions.all` (namespace + name checks) | `spec.matchConditions` (two CEL conditions) | `matchConditions` in `ValidatingPolicy` act as pre-filters — requests not matching are skipped, exactly like preconditions |
| `validate.deny.conditions.all` (operation in CREATE/UPDATE) | `expression: "false"` | `matchConstraints.operations` already restricts to CREATE/UPDATE; within those, the original rule always denied — expressed as a CEL expression that always returns `false` |
| `validate.message` (JMESPath template) | `messageExpression` (CEL string concatenation) | Jinja/JMESPath `{{request.object.*}}` replaced with CEL `object.metadata.*` string concatenation |
| Kyverno-specific version annotations removed | — | `kyverno.io/kyverno-version` and `kyverno.io/kubernetes-version` are old-style annotations not used in the new API |

| `match.any` branches (one unrestricted + one SA-scoped) | No `matchConditions` needed for subject | Branch 1 had its subject block commented out, making it unrestricted. The union of both branches covers all requestors, so no subject filter is required |
| `preconditions.all` (namespace + name checks) | `spec.matchConditions` (two CEL conditions) | `matchConditions` in `ValidatingPolicy` act as pre-filters — requests not matching are skipped, exactly like preconditions |
| `validate.deny.conditions.all` (operation in CREATE/UPDATE) | `expression: "false"` | `matchConstraints.operations` already restricts to CREATE/UPDATE; within those, the original rule always denied — expressed as a CEL expression that always returns `false` |
| `validate.message` (JMESPath template) | `messageExpression` (CEL string concatenation) | Jinja/JMESPath `{{request.object.*}}` replaced with CEL `object.metadata.*` string concatenation |
| Kyverno-specific version annotations removed | — | `kyverno.io/kyverno-version` and `kyverno.io/kubernetes-version` are old-style annotations not used in the new API |

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: limit-configmap-for-sa
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Limit ConfigMap to ServiceAccounts for a User
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: ConfigMap, ServiceAccount
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      This policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific
      ConfigMaps by ServiceAccounts.
spec:
  # Audit mode: record violations in PolicyReports without blocking requests.
  validationActions:
    - Audit

  # Disable background scanning; this policy is admission-only, mirroring
  # the original `background: false`.
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false

  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["configmaps"]

  # matchConditions narrow which requests the policy actually evaluates.
  # They replace the original `match.any` subjects block and `preconditions`.
  #
  # The original policy matched requests that satisfied ANY of:
  #   (a) any ConfigMap   (the commented-out developer SA block)
  #   (b) ConfigMap + SA  another-developer / another-namespace
  #
  # Combined with preconditions (all must hold):
  #   - namespace in [any-namespace, another-namespace]
  #   - name     in [any-configmap-name-good, another-configmap-name]
  #
  # The net effect: apply the deny check when:
  #   • the ConfigMap is one of the protected names AND in one of the
  #     protected namespaces, AND
  #   • the request comes from *any* ServiceAccount  OR  specifically from
  #     another-developer in another-namespace.
  matchConditions:
    # Precondition: namespace must be one of the protected namespaces.
    - name: protected-namespace
      expression: >-
        object.metadata.namespace in ["any-namespace", "another-namespace"]

    # Precondition: name must be one of the protected ConfigMap names.
    - name: protected-configmap-name
      expression: >-
        object.metadata.name in ["any-configmap-name-good", "another-configmap-name"]

    # Subject filter: mirror the original `match.any` rules.
    # Condition (a) – any ServiceAccount (the un-commented block matched all
    # ConfigMaps without a subject filter, so we include every SA here).
    # Condition (b) – or specifically another-developer in another-namespace.
    # If neither condition is true the request is outside policy scope and
    # is allowed through.
    - name: matched-service-account
      expression: >-
        parseServiceAccount(request.userInfo.username).Namespace != "" ||
        (
          parseServiceAccount(request.userInfo.username).Name      == "another-developer" &&
          parseServiceAccount(request.userInfo.username).Namespace == "another-namespace"
        )

  variables:
    - name: sa
      expression: parseServiceAccount(request.userInfo.username)

  validations:
    # The original rule denied CREATE and UPDATE. Because matchConstraints
    # already limits operations to CREATE and UPDATE, the expression simply
    # needs to return false to trigger a violation.
    - expression: "false"
      messageExpression: >-
        object.metadata.namespace + "/" + object.kind + "/" +
        object.metadata.name +
        " resource is protected. Admin or allowed users can change the resource"

The converted policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of every change made and why:

---

## Conversion summary

| Original (ClusterPolicy) | Converted (ValidatingPolicy) | Reason |
|---|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` | New API group for the 1.16+ policy types |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` | New cluster-scoped validating type |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` | Field renamed; now a list |
| `spec.background: false` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: false` | Moved into the structured `evaluation` block |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].resources` | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` | Standard `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy`-style resource selector; `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: ["v1"]`, `resources: ["configmaps"]`, `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[].subjects` | `spec.matchConditions` (CEL, `matched-service-account`) | Subjects are replaced by a CEL condition using Kyverno's `parseServiceAccount()` User library function |
| `spec.rules[].preconditions.all` (namespace & name checks) | `spec.matchConditions` (`protected-namespace`, `protected-configmap-name`) | `matchConditions` gate whether the policy runs at all — a perfect semantic match for preconditions |
| `spec.rules[].validate.deny.conditions` (AnyIn CREATE/UPDATE) | `spec.validations[].expression: "false"` | Operations are already constrained by `matchConstraints`; returning `false` unconditionally is the CEL equivalent of a deny-all |
| `spec.rules[].validate.message` (JMESPath template) | `spec.validations[].messageExpression` (CEL string) | Dynamic message rebuilt in CEL using `object.metadata.namespace`, `object.kind`, `object.metadata.name` |

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: limit-configmap-for-sa
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Limit ConfigMap to ServiceAccounts for a User
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.26+"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: ConfigMap, ServiceAccount
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      This policy shows how to restrict certain operations on specific
      ConfigMaps by ServiceAccounts.
spec:
  # Equivalent to spec.validationFailureAction: Audit
  validationActions:
    - Audit

  # Equivalent to spec.background: false
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false

  # Match CREATE and UPDATE requests on ConfigMaps (core API group, v1).
  # The original validate.deny only fired for CREATE and UPDATE operations, which is
  # expressed directly here via matchConstraints rather than as a deny condition.
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["configmaps"]

  # matchConditions replace the original spec.rules[].preconditions (all semantics).
  #
  # Subject-matching note: the original match.any had two branches:
  #   Branch 1 – ConfigMaps with no subject filter (the "developer" SA entry was
  #               commented out), meaning any caller satisfies this branch.
  #   Branch 2 – ConfigMaps requested by SA "another-developer/another-namespace".
  # Because branch 1 has no subject restriction it makes the policy applicable to
  # every caller, so no subject matchCondition is required here.
  # To restrict to only the named ServiceAccount (branch 2 intent), add:
  #   - name: match-subject
  #     expression: >-
  #       parseServiceAccount(request.userInfo.username).Name == "another-developer" &&
  #       parseServiceAccount(request.userInfo.username).Namespace == "another-namespace"
  matchConditions:
    # Precondition: restrict to the protected namespaces (original preconditions[0]).
    - name: protected-namespace
      expression: >-
        object.metadata.namespace in ["any-namespace", "another-namespace"]

    # Precondition: restrict to the protected ConfigMap names (original preconditions[1]).
    - name: protected-configmap-name
      expression: >-
        object.metadata.name in ["any-configmap-name-good", "another-configmap-name"]

  # The original validate.deny unconditionally denied matching requests.
  # ValidatingPolicy validation expressions are inverted: true = allow, false = deny.
  # Returning false replicates the deny behaviour of the original rule.
  validations:
    - expression: "false"
      messageExpression: >-
        object.metadata.namespace + "/" + object.kind + "/" + object.metadata.name +
        " resource is protected. Admin or allowed users can change the resource"

The converted policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the conversion:

---

## Conversion summary

| Original (`ClusterPolicy`) | Converted (`ValidatingPolicy`) |
|---|---|
| `apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1` | `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` |
| `kind: ClusterPolicy` | `kind: ValidatingPolicy` |
| `spec.validationFailureAction: Audit` | `spec.validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `spec.background: false` | `spec.evaluation.background.enabled: false` |
| `spec.rules[].match` on ConfigMap kinds | `spec.matchConstraints.resourceRules` — `apiGroups: [""]`, `apiVersions: ["v1"]`, `resources: ["configmaps"]` |
| `spec.rules[].validate.deny.conditions` on `CREATE\|UPDATE` | Moved into `matchConstraints.resourceRules.operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` |
| `spec.rules[].preconditions.all` (namespace & name filters) | `spec.matchConditions` — two CEL expressions replacing the JMESPath preconditions |
| `spec.rules[].validate.deny` (unconditional deny) | `spec.validations[].expression: "false"` (inverted logic — `false` = deny in CEL) |
| `{{request.object.metadata.namespace}}/{{request.object.kind}}/...` message | `messageExpression` using CEL string concatenation with `object.metadata.*` |
| `spec.rules[].match.any[1].subjects` (SA `another-developer`) | Preserved as a commented-out `matchCondition` using `parseServiceAccount(request.userInfo.username)` — the branch 1 (no subject filter) subsumes it, so it is not enforced by default but is documented for easy activation |

yaml_preview:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: pdb-minavailable-check
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Check PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.27"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: PodDisruptionBudget, Deployment, StatefulSet
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      When a Pod controller which can run multiple replicas is subject to an active PodDisruptionBudget,
      if the replicas field has a value equal to the minAvailable value of the PodDisruptionBudget
      it may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains which may impact routine maintenance
      tasks and disrupt operations. This policy checks incoming Deployments and StatefulSets which have
      a matching PodDisruptionBudget to ensure these two values do not match.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [apps]
        apiVersions: [v1]
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: [deployments, statefulsets]
  # Skip resources with 0 replicas (not subject to disruption concerns)
  matchConditions:
    - name: has-replicas
      expression: >-
        !has(object.spec.replicas) || object.spec.replicas > 0
  variables:
    - name: pdbs
      expression: >-
        resource.List("policy/v1", "poddisruptionbudgets", object.metadata.namespace).items
    - name: podLabels
      expression: >-
        object.spec.template.metadata.?labels.orValue({})
    # Find the first PDB whose selector.matchLabels is a subset of the pod template labels
    - name: matchingPdb
      expression: >-
        variables.pdbs.filter(pdb,
          has(pdb.spec.selector) &&
          has(pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels) &&
          pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels.all(k,
            k in variables.podLabels && pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels[k] == variables.podLabels[k]
          )
        )
    - name: minAvailable
      expression: >-
        variables.matchingPdb.size() > 0 && has(variables.matchingPdb[0].spec.minAvailable)
          ? variables.matchingPdb[0].spec.minAvailable
          : 0
    - name: replicas
      expression: >-
        has(object.spec.replicas) ? object.spec.replicas : 1
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.minAvailable == 0 || int(variables.minAvailable) != variables.replicas
      message: >-
        The matching PodDisruptionBudget for this resource has its minAvailable value equal to the
        replica count which is not permitted.
      messageExpression: >-
        "The matching PodDisruptionBudget has minAvailable=" + string(variables.minAvailable) +
        " which equals the replica count (" + string(variables.replicas) + "). " +
        "This may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains."
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: pdb-minavailable-check
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Check PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.9.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.24"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: PodDisruptionBudget, Deployment, StatefulSet
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      When a Pod controller which can run multiple replicas is subject to an active PodDisruptionBudget,
      if the replicas field has a value equal to the minAvailable value of the PodDisruptionBudget
      it may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains which may impact routine maintenance
      tasks and disrupt operations. This policy checks incoming Deployments and StatefulSets which have
      a matching PodDisruptionBudget to ensure these two values do not match.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: false
  rules:
    - name: pdb-minavailable
      match:
        any:
          - resources:
              kinds:
                - Deployment
                - StatefulSet
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: AnyIn
          value:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        - key: "{{ request.object.spec.replicas || `1` }}"
          operator: GreaterThan
          value: 0
      context:
        - name: minavailable
          apiCall:
            urlPath: "/apis/policy/v1/namespaces/{{request.namespace}}/poddisruptionbudgets"
            jmesPath: "items[?label_match(spec.selector.matchLabels, `{{request.object.spec.template.metadata.labels}}`)] | [0] | spec.minAvailable || `0`"
      validate:
        message: >-
          The matching PodDisruptionBudget for this resource has its minAvailable value equal to the replica count
          which is not permitted.
        deny:
          conditions:
            any:
              - key: "{{ request.object.spec.replicas }}"
                operator: Equals
                value: "{{ minavailable }}"

raw_log:
The converted policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of all the translation decisions made:

**Structural changes:**

| Old (`kyverno.io/v1 ClusterPolicy`) | New (`policies.kyverno.io/v1 ValidatingPolicy`) |
|---|---|
| `validationFailureAction: Audit` | `validationActions: [Audit]` |
| `background: false` | `evaluation.background.enabled: false` |
| `match.any[].resources.kinds` | `matchConstraints.resourceRules` with `apiGroups: [apps]`, `apiVersions: [v1]`, `operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]` |
| `preconditions` (JMESPath + operators) | `matchConditions` (CEL expressions) |
| `context[].apiCall` with `jmesPath` | `variables` using `resource.List()` from Kyverno's CEL Resource library |
| `validate.deny.conditions` | `validations[].expression` (CEL boolean) |

**Logic translation highlights:**

- The JMESPath `label_match(spec.selector.matchLabels, ...)` is replaced with a pure CEL expression that checks each key in `pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels` is present and matches in the pod template labels.
- The `precondition` guarding `replicas > 0` becomes a `matchConditions` CEL expression (`!has(object.spec.replicas) || object.spec.replicas > 0`), which short-circuits policy evaluation entirely when not applicable.
- `minAvailable` defaults to `0` when no matching PDB is found (same semantics as the original `|| \`0\``).
- A `messageExpression` is added for a dynamic, contextual failure message showing the actual values that conflicted.

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: pdb-minavailable-check
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Check PodDisruptionBudget minAvailable
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.27"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: PodDisruptionBudget, Deployment, StatefulSet
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      When a Pod controller which can run multiple replicas is subject to an active PodDisruptionBudget,
      if the replicas field has a value equal to the minAvailable value of the PodDisruptionBudget
      it may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains which may impact routine maintenance
      tasks and disrupt operations. This policy checks incoming Deployments and StatefulSets which have
      a matching PodDisruptionBudget to ensure these two values do not match.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments", "statefulsets"]
  matchConditions:
    - name: has-replicas
      expression: "object.spec.?replicas.orValue(1) > 0"
  variables:
    - name: replicas
      expression: "object.spec.?replicas.orValue(1)"
    - name: podLabels
      expression: "object.spec.template.metadata.?labels.orValue({})"
    - name: pdbs
      expression: >-
        resource.List("policy/v1", "poddisruptionbudgets", object.metadata.namespace).items
    - name: matchingPdb
      expression: >-
        variables.pdbs.filter(pdb,
          has(pdb.spec.selector) &&
          has(pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels) &&
          pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels.all(k,
            k in variables.podLabels &&
            pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels[k] == variables.podLabels[k]
          )
        )
    - name: minAvailable
      expression: >-
        variables.matchingPdb.size() > 0 && has(variables.matchingPdb[0].spec.minAvailable)
          ? int(variables.matchingPdb[0].spec.minAvailable)
          : 0
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.matchingPdb.size() == 0 ||
        !has(variables.matchingPdb[0].spec.minAvailable) ||
        variables.replicas != variables.minAvailable
      message: >-
        The matching PodDisruptionBudget for this resource has its minAvailable value equal to the replica count
        which is not permitted.
      messageExpression: >-
        "The matching PodDisruptionBudget '" +
        (variables.matchingPdb.size() > 0 ? variables.matchingPdb[0].metadata.name : "") +
        "' has minAvailable=" + string(variables.minAvailable) +
        " equal to replicas=" + string(variables.replicas) +
        ", which may prevent voluntary disruptions including Node drains."
