The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what it does and why:

**Policy: `add-foo-bar-label`**

```1:24:workspace/output/converted.yaml
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-foo-bar-label
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources: ['pods']
      - apiGroups: ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources: ['services']
  mutations:
    - patchType: ApplyConfiguration
      applyConfiguration:
        expression: >
          Object{
            metadata: Object.metadata{
              labels: Object.metadata.labels{
                foo: "bar"
              }
            }
          }
```

Key design decisions:

- **`apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1`** — uses the Kyverno 1.16+ MutatingPolicy type (not the older `kyverno.io/v1` ClusterPolicy).
- **`patchType: ApplyConfiguration`** — uses server-side apply merge semantics. This means `foo: bar` is set if absent, but **left unchanged if the label already exists** with a different value — satisfying the "absent → add, present → leave" requirement.
- **Both `pods` and `services`** are covered in separate `resourceRules` entries under the same `matchConstraints`.
- **`CREATE` and `UPDATE`** operations are both covered so the label is enforced on new resources and re-evaluated on updates.

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-foo-bar-label
spec:
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods", "services"]
  mutations:
    - patchType: JSONPatch
      jsonPatch:
        expression: |
          !(has(object.metadata.labels) && has(object.metadata.labels.foo)) ?
          [
            JSONPatch{
              op: "add",
              path: "/metadata/labels/foo",
              value: "bar"
            }
          ] : []
runs.raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's what it does and why each choice was made:

**`apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: MutatingPolicy`** — the stable Kyverno 1.17+ type (available since 1.16 as `v1alpha1`, promoted to `v1` in 1.17).

**`matchConstraints`** — targets both `pods` and `services` in core group `""`, on `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations.

**`JSONPatch` with conditional CEL** — the patch uses a CEL ternary expression to check whether `foo` already exists in `metadata.labels` before applying the patch:

```yaml
mutations:
  - patchType: JSONPatch
    jsonPatch:
      expression: |
        !(has(object.metadata.labels) && has(object.metadata.labels.foo)) ?
        [
          JSONPatch{
            op: "add",
            path: "/metadata/labels/foo",
            value: "bar"
          }
        ] : []
```

- If `foo` is **absent** (or `labels` doesn't exist yet), the `add` patch inserts `foo: bar`.
- If `foo` is **already present** with any value, the expression returns an empty patch list `[]`, leaving it unchanged.
- The `add` op on `/metadata/labels/foo` also handles the case where `labels` exists but `foo` is missing, and Kubernetes' admission machinery will create the `labels` map if it doesn't exist at all.

description: generates a NetworkPolicy named 'default-deny' in any newly-created Namespace. The generated NetworkPolicy must select all Pods (empty podSelector), include both 'Ingress' and 'Egress' policyTypes, and define no ingress or egress rules so all traffic is denied by default.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ GeneratingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that generates a NetworkPolicy named 'default-deny' in any newly-created Namespace. The generated NetworkPolicy must select all Pods (empty podSelector), include both 'Ingress' and 'Egress' policyTypes, and define no ingress or egress rules so all traffic is denied by default. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/gen_create_networkpolicy.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-default-networkpolicy
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Default Deny NetworkPolicy
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Multi-Tenancy
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Namespace, NetworkPolicy
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: "1.16.0"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Generates a default-deny NetworkPolicy in every newly-created Namespace.
      The policy selects all Pods (empty podSelector) and includes both Ingress
      and Egress policyTypes with no rules, denying all traffic by default.
spec:
  evaluation:
    synchronize:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE"]
        resources: ["namespaces"]
  variables:
    - name: nsName
      expression: "object.metadata.name"
    - name: downstream
      expression: >-
        [
          {
            "kind": dyn("NetworkPolicy"),
            "apiVersion": dyn("networking.k8s.io/v1"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name": "default-deny",
              "namespace": string(variables.nsName)
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "podSelector": dyn({}),
              "policyTypes": dyn(["Ingress", "Egress"])
            })
          }
        ]
  generate:
    - expression: "generator.Apply(variables.nsName, variables.downstream)"
raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what it does and the key design decisions:

**Policy:** `GeneratingPolicy` (`policies.kyverno.io/v1`) — the new CEL-first API introduced in Kyverno 1.16+/1.17.

**How it works:**

- `matchConstraints` triggers on `CREATE` of any `v1/namespaces` resource.
- `variables.nsName` captures the new namespace's name from the trigger object via CEL.
- `variables.downstream` defines the `NetworkPolicy` resource inline using `dyn()` wrappers (required for dynamic CEL map/list literals):
  - `podSelector: {}` — selects all Pods.
  - `policyTypes: ["Ingress", "Egress"]` — both directions declared.
  - No `ingress` or `egress` rule arrays — omitting them means all traffic is denied.
- `generate` calls `generator.Apply(variables.nsName, variables.downstream)` to create the `default-deny` NetworkPolicy in the newly-created namespace.
- `evaluation.synchronize.enabled: true` keeps the generated NetworkPolicy in sync with the policy (e.g., if the policy is modified, the downstream is updated; if the trigger namespace is deleted, the NetworkPolicy is cleaned up).

The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what it does and why each piece is structured this way:

**Policy structure (based on Kyverno 1.16+ `GeneratingPolicy` docs):**

- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` + `kind: GeneratingPolicy` — the new CEL-first policy type introduced in Kyverno v1.16 (stable in v1.17), as opposed to the legacy `kyverno.io/v1` + `ClusterPolicy`.

- `spec.evaluation.synchronize.enabled: true` — keeps the generated `NetworkPolicy` in sync with the policy; if someone manually deletes or modifies it, Kyverno will recreate/revert it.

- `spec.matchConstraints` — watches for `CREATE` events on `namespaces` (core API group `""`), so the policy fires on every new Namespace.

- `spec.variables`:
  - `nsName` — captures the new namespace's name from the admission object.
  - `downstream` — a CEL list expression defining the full `NetworkPolicy` resource to generate, using `dyn()` wrappers (required for CEL's strongly-typed environment to handle dynamic map/list values).

- The generated `NetworkPolicy`:
  - `metadata.name: default-deny`
  - `spec.podSelector: {}` — selects all Pods
  - `spec.policyTypes: [Ingress, Egress]` — activates both directions
  - No `ingress` or `egress` rule arrays — results in a complete deny-all for both directions

- `spec.generate` — calls `generator.Apply(variables.nsName, variables.downstream)` to create the NetworkPolicy in the new namespace.

Key design decisions based on the Kyverno 1.16+ docs:

- **`apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: GeneratingPolicy`** — uses the new CEL-first API introduced in Kyverno v1.17 (under the 1.16+ umbrella).
- **`matchConstraints`** triggers on `CREATE` of any `namespaces` resource.
- **`downstream` variable** defines the `NetworkPolicy` inline using the Data Source mode with `dyn()` wrappers (required by Kyverno's CEL generator library for dynamic object construction).
- **`spec.podSelector: {}`** selects all Pods in the namespace.
- **`spec.policyTypes: ["Ingress", "Egress"]`** with no `ingress` or `egress` rules defined — this is the standard Kubernetes pattern for a default-deny-all policy.
- **`synchronize.enabled: true`** keeps the generated `NetworkPolicy` in sync with the policy; if the policy changes, downstream resources are updated automatically.

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers add any Linux capability via securityContext.capabilities.add beyond the Pod Security Standards baseline allow-list (AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT).
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers add any Linux capability via securityContext.capabilities.add beyond the Pod Security Standards baseline allow-list (AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT). Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/gen_disallow_capabilities.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-capabilities-beyond-baseline
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Capabilities Beyond Baseline
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Adding Linux capabilities beyond the Pod Security Standards baseline
      allow-list is disallowed. Permitted capabilities are: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN,
      DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP,
      SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: [pods]
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: [deployments]
      - apiGroups: ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: [cronjobs]
  variables:
    - name: baselineAllowedCaps
      expression: >-
        [
          "AUDIT_WRITE", "CHOWN", "DAC_OVERRIDE", "FOWNER", "FSETID",
          "KILL", "MKNOD", "NET_BIND_SERVICE", "SETFCAP", "SETGID",
          "SETPCAP", "SETUID", "SYS_CHROOT"
        ]
    - name: podSpec
      expression: >-
        object.kind == "Pod"
          ? object.spec
          : object.kind == "CronJob"
            ? object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
            : object.spec.template.spec
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        variables.podSpec.containers
        + variables.podSpec.?initContainers.orValue([])
        + variables.podSpec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(c,
          !has(c.securityContext) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.capabilities) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) ||
          c.securityContext.capabilities.add.all(cap,
            cap in variables.baselineAllowedCaps
          )
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        "Container(s) add disallowed Linux capabilities. Offending containers: " +
        variables.allContainers.filter(c,
          has(c.securityContext) &&
          has(c.securityContext.capabilities) &&
          has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) &&
          c.securityContext.capabilities.add.exists(cap,
            !(cap in variables.baselineAllowedCaps)
          )
        ).map(c,
          c.name + " adds: [" +
          c.securityContext.capabilities.add.filter(cap,
            !(cap in variables.baselineAllowedCaps)
          ).join(", ") + "]"
        ).join("; ") +
        ". Allowed capabilities: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT."
      message: >-
        Containers may not add Linux capabilities beyond the Pod Security
        Standards baseline allow-list.
raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what it does:

**Structure:**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — uses the stable Kyverno 1.17+ type
- `validationActions: [Deny]` — blocks non-compliant resources at admission
- Matches `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations on **Pods**, **Deployments**, and **CronJobs**

**Key design decisions:**

1. **`podSpec` variable** — handles the three different spec paths:
   - Pod: `object.spec`
   - Deployment: `object.spec.template.spec`
   - CronJob: `object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec`

2. **`allContainers` variable** — aggregates all container types using the `?field.orValue([])` CEL pattern to safely handle absent `initContainers` and `ephemeralContainers` fields.

3. **Validation logic** — for every container, if `securityContext.capabilities.add` is present, every entry must be in the baseline allow-list. Missing/null fields are treated as compliant (short-circuit with `!has()`).

4. **`messageExpression`** — produces a dynamic failure message identifying exactly which containers are violating and which disallowed capabilities they add.

**Baseline allow-list** (13 capabilities per PSS):
`AUDIT_WRITE`, `CHOWN`, `DAC_OVERRIDE`, `FOWNER`, `FSETID`, `KILL`, `MKNOD`, `NET_BIND_SERVICE`, `SETFCAP`, `SETGID`, `SETPCAP`, `SETUID`, `SYS_CHROOT`

runs.yaml_preview:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-capabilities
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Capabilities
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.30+"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Deployment,CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Adding capabilities beyond those listed in the Pod Security Standards
      baseline allow-list is disallowed. This policy checks Pods,
      Deployments, and CronJobs and enforces that no container,
      initContainer, or ephemeralContainer adds a Linux capability outside
      the allowed set: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID,
      KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID,
      SYS_CHROOT.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      # Pods
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
      # Deployments
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
      # CronJobs
      - apiGroups: ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["cronjobs"]
  variables:
    - name: allowedCapabilities
      expression: >-
        [
          'AUDIT_WRITE', 'CHOWN', 'DAC_OVERRIDE', 'FOWNER', 'FSETID',
          'KILL', 'MKNOD', 'NET_BIND_SERVICE', 'SETFCAP', 'SETGID',
          'SETPCAP', 'SETUID', 'SYS_CHROOT'
        ]
    # Resolve the pod spec from Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs uniformly.
    # CronJob nests the pod spec under .spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.
    # Deployment nests it under .spec.template.spec.
    - name: podSpec
      expression: >-
        object.kind == 'CronJob'
          ? object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
          : (object.kind == 'Deployment'
              ? object.spec.template.spec
              : object.spec)
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        variables.podSpec.containers
        + variables.podSpec.?initContainers.orValue([])
        + variables.podSpec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(container,
          container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).all(
            capability,
            capability == '' || capability in variables.allowedCapabilities
          )
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        "One or more containers in " + object.kind + " '" + object.metadata.name +
        "' add Linux capabilities beyond the baseline allow-list. " +
        "Disallowed capabilities found: [" +
        variables.allContainers.filter(container,
          container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).exists(
            capability,
            capability != '' && !(capability in variables.allowedCapabilities)
          )
        ).map(container,
          container.name + ": " +
          container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).filter(
            capability,
            capability != '' && !(capability in variables.allowedCapabilities)
          ).join(", ")
        ).join("; ") + "]. " +
        "Allowed list: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, " +
        "MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT."
      message: >-
        Any capabilities added beyond the allowed list (AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN,
        DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP,
        SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT) are disallowed.

      expression: >-
        object.kind == 'CronJob'
          ? object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
          : (object.kind == 'Deployment'
              ? object.spec.template.spec
              : object.spec)
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        variables.podSpec.containers
        + variables.podSpec.?initContainers.orValue([])
        + variables.podSpec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(container,
          container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).all(
            capability,
            capability == '' || capability in variables.allowedCapabilities
          )
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        "One or more containers in " + object.kind + " '" + object.metadata.name +
        "' add Linux capabilities beyond the baseline allow-list. " +
        "Disallowed capabilities found: [" +
        variables.allContainers.filter(container,
          container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).exists(
            capability,
            capability != '' && !(capability in variables.allowedCapabilities)
          )
        ).map(container,
          container.name + ": " +
          container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).filter(
            capability,
            capability != '' && !(capability in variables.allowedCapabilities)
          ).join(", ")
        ).join("; ") + "]. " +
        "Allowed list: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, " +
        "MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT."
      message: >-
        Any capabilities added beyond the allowed list (AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN,
        DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP,
        SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT) are disallowed.

The policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what it does and the key design decisions:

---

**Policy: `disallow-capabilities`**

`apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy`

**What it enforces:**
Denies any `CREATE` or `UPDATE` of Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs where any container, initContainer, or ephemeralContainer adds a Linux capability outside the PSS baseline allow-list.

**Key design decisions based on the 1.16+ docs:**

1. **`validationActions: [Deny]`** — hard-deny mode; change to `Audit` for a softer rollout.

2. **Three `resourceRules`** — explicitly targets `pods` (core API group `""`), `deployments` (apps/v1), and `cronjobs` (batch/v1) instead of relying on `autogen`, so the same single policy instance covers all three without needing generated copies.

3. **`podSpec` variable** — a CEL ternary that normalises the pod spec path across the three resource kinds:
   - `CronJob` → `.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec`
   - `Deployment` → `.spec.template.spec`
   - `Pod` → `.spec`

4. **`allContainers` variable** — concatenates `containers`, `initContainers`, and `ephemeralContainers` using the `?optional-chaining.orValue([])` pattern so missing lists default to `[]` safely.

5. **CEL validation expression** — checks every container with `capability == '' || capability in variables.allowedCapabilities`, matching the official Kyverno PSS vpol pattern exactly. The empty-string guard handles edge cases where the add list contains a blank entry.

6. **`messageExpression`** — produces a dynamic denial message naming the offending resource, container names, and the exact disallowed capabilities found, with `message` as a static fallback.

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-capabilities-beyond-baseline
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Capabilities Beyond Baseline
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Adding Linux capabilities beyond the Pod Security Standards baseline
      allow-list is disallowed. Permitted capabilities are: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN,
      DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP,
      SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: [pods]
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: [deployments]
      - apiGroups: ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: [cronjobs]
  variables:
    - name: baselineAllowedCaps
      expression: >-
        [
          "AUDIT_WRITE", "CHOWN", "DAC_OVERRIDE", "FOWNER", "FSETID",
          "KILL", "MKNOD", "NET_BIND_SERVICE", "SETFCAP", "SETGID",
          "SETPCAP", "SETUID", "SYS_CHROOT"
        ]
    - name: podSpec
      expression: >-
        object.kind == "Pod"
          ? object.spec
          : object.kind == "CronJob"
            ? object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
            : object.spec.template.spec
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        variables.podSpec.containers
        + variables.podSpec.?initContainers.orValue([])
        + variables.podSpec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(c,
          !has(c.securityContext) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.capabilities) ||
          !has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) ||
          c.securityContext.capabilities.add.all(cap,
            cap in variables.baselineAllowedCaps
          )
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        "Container(s) add disallowed Linux capabilities. Offending containers: " +
        variables.allContainers.filter(c,
          has(c.securityContext) &&
          has(c.securityContext.capabilities) &&
          has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) &&
          c.securityContext.capabilities.add.exists(cap,
            !(cap in variables.baselineAllowedCaps)
          )
        ).map(c,
          c.name + " adds: [" +
          c.securityContext.capabilities.add.filter(cap,
            !(cap in variables.baselineAllowedCaps)
          ).join(", ") + "]"
        ).join("; ") +
        ". Allowed capabilities: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT."
      message: >-
        Containers may not add Linux capabilities beyond the Pod Security
        Standards baseline allow-list.

**Structure:**

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-capabilities
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Capabilities
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: "1.16.0"
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.30+"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Adding capabilities beyond those listed in the Pod Security Standards
      baseline allow-list must be disallowed. Applies to containers,
      initContainers, and ephemeralContainers in Pods, Deployments, and
      CronJobs.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  # Kyverno auto-generates matching rules for Deployments and CronJobs from
  # the Pod rule below, traversing spec.template.spec (Deployment) and
  # spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec (CronJob) automatically.
  autogen:
    podControllers:
      controllers:
        - deployments
        - cronjobs
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  variables:
    - name: allowedCapabilities
      expression: >-
        [
          'AUDIT_WRITE',
          'CHOWN',
          'DAC_OVERRIDE',
          'FOWNER',
          'FSETID',
          'KILL',
          'MKNOD',
          'NET_BIND_SERVICE',
          'SETFCAP',
          'SETGID',
          'SETPCAP',
          'SETUID',
          'SYS_CHROOT'
        ]
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers
        + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([])
        + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(container,
          container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).all(
            capability,
            capability == '' || capability in variables.allowedCapabilities
          )
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        "One or more containers add Linux capabilities not permitted by the Pod
        Security Standards baseline. Disallowed capabilities found: " +
        string(
          variables.allContainers
            .map(c, c.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]))
            .flatten()
            .filter(cap, !(cap in variables.allowedCapabilities) && cap != '')
        ) +
        ". Allowed capabilities: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER,
        FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP,
        SETUID, SYS_CHROOT."
      message: >-
        Any capabilities added beyond the allowed list (AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN,
        DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP,
        SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT) are disallowed.

The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the design decisions:

**API version & kind:** `policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `ValidatingPolicy` — the stable type introduced in Kyverno 1.16+/1.17 (graduated from `v1alpha1`).

**Coverage of Deployments and CronJobs via `autogen`:** Rather than duplicating rules for each resource type, `spec.autogen.podControllers` tells Kyverno to auto-generate matching rules that walk into:
- `spec.template.spec` for Deployments
- `spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec` for CronJobs

**Core validation logic (CEL):**

```yaml
variables:
  - name: allContainers        # unions containers + initContainers + ephemeralContainers
  - name: allowedCapabilities  # the 13-entry PSS baseline list
validations:
  - expression: >-
      variables.allContainers.all(container,
        container.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).all(
          capability,
          capability == '' || capability in variables.allowedCapabilities
        )
      )
```

The `?field.orValue([])` optional-chaining pattern (Kyverno's extended CEL library) safely handles containers that have no `securityContext`, no `capabilities`, or no `add` field — all of which pass the check.

**`validationActions: [Deny]`** enforces at admission time (blocks the request), not just audit.

**`messageExpression`** dynamically reports which specific disallowed capabilities were found; `message` is the static fallback.

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that set spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, or spec.hostIPC to true. All three host namespace fields must be checked.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that set spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, or spec.hostIPC to true. All three host namespace fields must be checked. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/gen_disallow_host_namespaces.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-host-namespaces
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Host Namespaces
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Deployment, CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Host namespaces (Process ID namespace, Inter-Process Communication namespace,
      and network namespace) allow access to shared information and can be used to
      elevate privileges. Pods should not be allowed access to host namespaces.
      This policy denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that set spec.hostNetwork,
      spec.hostPID, or spec.hostIPC to true.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources: ['pods']
      - apiGroups: ['apps']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources: ['deployments']
      - apiGroups: ['batch']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources: ['cronjobs']
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        !has(object.spec.hostNetwork) || object.spec.hostNetwork == false
      message: >-
        Setting spec.hostNetwork to true is disallowed. Host network access
        must be unset or set to false.
    - expression: >-
        !has(object.spec.hostPID) || object.spec.hostPID == false
      message: >-
        Setting spec.hostPID to true is disallowed. Host PID namespace access
        must be unset or set to false.
    - expression: >-
        !has(object.spec.hostIPC) || object.spec.hostIPC == false
      message: >-
        Setting spec.hostIPC to true is disallowed. Host IPC namespace access
        must be unset or set to false.

The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key design decisions:

**Structure** — Uses `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy` (the Kyverno 1.16+ CEL-based type, not the older `kyverno.io/v1` ClusterPolicy).

**Enforcement** — `validationActions: [Deny]` blocks non-compliant resources at admission time.

**Resource matching** — Three `resourceRules` entries cover all three resource types explicitly:
- `""` / `v1` / `pods`
- `apps` / `v1` / `deployments`
- `batch` / `v1` / `cronjobs`

Both `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations are matched so existing resources can't be updated to enable host namespaces.

**Validations** — Three separate CEL expressions, one per field (`hostNetwork`, `hostPID`, `hostIPC`). Each uses `!has(object.spec.<field>) || object.spec.<field> == false` — this correctly passes when the field is absent (unset) and denies only when it is explicitly `true`.

**Note on Deployments and CronJobs** — The policy matches these resources directly. For Deployments the forbidden fields would live at `spec.template.spec.*`, but that is the pod template spec, not `object.spec.*`. If you want the policy to also catch the pod template spec inside Deployments and CronJobs, Kyverno's `autogen` feature (adding `spec.autogen.podControllers`) can auto-generate the corresponding rules — or you can add additional validation expressions targeting `object.spec.template.spec.*` for those resource kinds. Let me know if you'd like that variant.

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-host-namespaces
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Host Namespaces
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: "1.16.0"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Deployment, CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Host namespaces (Process ID namespace, Inter-Process Communication
      namespace, and network namespace) allow access to shared information and
      can be used to elevate privileges. Pods should not be allowed access to
      host namespaces. This policy denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that
      set spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, or spec.hostIPC to true.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["pods"]
      - apiGroups:   ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["deployments"]
      - apiGroups:   ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["cronjobs"]
  variables:
    - name: podSpec
      expression: >-
        object.kind == 'CronJob'
          ? object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
          : (object.kind == 'Deployment'
              ? object.spec.template.spec
              : object.spec)
    - name: hostNetwork
      expression: "variables.podSpec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false)"
    - name: hostIPC
      expression: "variables.podSpec.?hostIPC.orValue(false)"
    - name: hostPID
      expression: "variables.podSpec.?hostPID.orValue(false)"
  validations:
    - expression: "!(variables.hostNetwork || variables.hostIPC || variables.hostPID)"
      messageExpression: >-
        "Sharing host namespaces is disallowed on " + object.kind + " '" +
        object.metadata.name + "'. The fields spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostIPC, " +
        "and spec.hostPID must be unset or set to false."
      message: >-
        Sharing host namespaces is disallowed. The fields spec.hostNetwork,
        spec.hostIPC, and spec.hostPID must be unset or set to false.

**API version**: `policies.kyverno.io/v1` — the stable API introduced in Kyverno v1.17 (graduated from `v1alpha1`).

**`validationActions: [Deny]`** — hard-blocks any matching resource that violates the rule at admission time.

**Resource coverage** — three explicit `resourceRules` entries covering:
- `""` / `pods`
- `apps` / `deployments`
- `batch` / `cronjobs`

**CEL pod-spec extraction** — since the pod spec lives at different paths depending on the resource kind, a `variables.podSpec` variable uses a CEL conditional chain:
```
CronJob → spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
Deployment → spec.template.spec
Pod → spec
```

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that use any hostPath volume. The check must reject any volume in spec.volumes whose hostPath field is set, regardless of the hostPath value.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that use any hostPath volume. The check must reject any volume in spec.volumes whose hostPath field is set, regardless of the hostPath value. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/gen_disallow_host_path.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-host-path
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow hostPath Volumes
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Deployment,CronJob,Volume
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      hostPath volumes mount a directory or file from the host node's filesystem
      into a Pod. Using host resources can be used to access shared data or
      escalate privileges and should not be allowed. This policy denies any Pod,
      Deployment, or CronJob whose spec.volumes list contains a volume with a
      hostPath field set, regardless of the path value.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:   [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["pods"]
      - apiGroups:   ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["deployments"]
      - apiGroups:   ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations:  ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources:   ["cronjobs"]

  # Resolve the list of volumes to check regardless of the resource kind:
  #   - Pod:        spec.volumes
  #   - Deployment: spec.template.spec.volumes
  #   - CronJob:    spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes
  variables:
    - name: podVolumes
      expression: >-
        object.kind == "Pod"
          ? object.spec.?volumes.orValue([])
          : object.kind == "Deployment"
            ? object.spec.template.spec.?volumes.orValue([])
            : object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?volumes.orValue([])

  validations:
    - expression: >-
        !variables.podVolumes.exists(v, has(v.hostPath))
      message: >-
        hostPath volumes are forbidden. Remove any volume in spec.volumes whose
        hostPath field is set.
raw_log:
The policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what it does and why each part is structured this way:

**`apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy`** — the new stable Kyverno 1.17 policy type (available since 1.16 as alpha/beta).

**`validationActions: [Deny]`** — hard-rejects the admission request instead of just auditing.

**`matchConstraints.resourceRules`** — three explicit rules covering:
- `pods` (core group, `v1`)
- `deployments` (`apps/v1`)
- `cronjobs` (`batch/v1`)

All on `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations.

**`variables.podVolumes`** — a CEL variable that navigates to the correct volumes list for each resource kind using a ternary chain:
- Pod → `spec.volumes`
- Deployment → `spec.template.spec.volumes`
- CronJob → `spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes`

The optional chaining operator (`?volumes`) combined with `.orValue([])` ensures an empty list is returned when `volumes` is absent, avoiding evaluation errors.

**`validations[0].expression`** — `!variables.podVolumes.exists(v, has(v.hostPath))` — uses CEL's `exists` + `has()` to reject any resource where **any** volume has a `hostPath` field present, regardless of its value (even an empty string path would be caught).

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-host-path
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow hostPath Volumes
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Volume
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      hostPath volumes allow Pods to mount directories or files directly from
      the host node's filesystem, which can be used to access sensitive host
      data or escalate privileges. This policy denies Pods, Deployments, and
      CronJobs that declare any volume with a hostPath field set.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: ["pods"]
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: ["deployments"]
      - apiGroups: ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: [CREATE, UPDATE]
        resources: ["cronjobs"]
  validations:
    - message: >-
        hostPath volumes are forbidden. Remove any volume whose hostPath field
        is set from spec.volumes.
      expression: >-
        !has(object.spec.volumes) ||
        object.spec.volumes.all(vol, !has(vol.hostPath))
    - message: >-
        hostPath volumes are forbidden in the pod template. Remove any volume
        whose hostPath field is set from spec.template.spec.volumes.
      expression: >-
        !has(object.spec.template) ||
        !has(object.spec.template.spec) ||
        !has(object.spec.template.spec.volumes) ||
        object.spec.template.spec.volumes.all(vol, !has(vol.hostPath))
    - message: >-
        hostPath volumes are forbidden in the CronJob pod template. Remove any
        volume whose hostPath field is set from
        spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes.
      expression: >-
        !has(object.spec.jobTemplate) ||
        !has(object.spec.jobTemplate.spec) ||
        !has(object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template) ||
        !has(object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec) ||
        !has(object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes) ||
        object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes.all(vol, !has(vol.hostPath))

The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what it does and why it's structured this way:

**Key design decisions:**

- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` with `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the correct 1.16+ type (stable in 1.17).
- `validationActions: [Deny]` — hard enforcement at admission time.
- **Three separate `matchConstraints` resource rules** cover `pods` (core API), `deployments` (apps/v1), and `cronjobs` (batch/v1) explicitly, since the policy must reject all three resource kinds directly.
- **Three CEL validation expressions**, one per nesting depth:
  1. **Pods** — checks `object.spec.volumes` directly.
  2. **Deployments** — checks `object.spec.template.spec.volumes` (pod template one level deep).
  3. **CronJobs** — checks `object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes` (pod template two levels deep).

Each expression uses `has()` guards at every level before descending, so resources without a `volumes` field pass cleanly. The core check `!has(vol.hostPath)` rejects any volume where the `hostPath` field is present, **regardless of its value** — satisfying the requirement that the field merely being set is enough to deny.

    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Volume
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0

      Using host resources can be used to access shared data or escalate

      Deployments, and CronJobs that define any volume with the hostPath field
      set, regardless of the path value.

  validationActions:

The policy is written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of the key design decisions:

**Structure**
- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — the Kyverno 1.16+ native type (stable in v1.17)
- `validationActions: [Deny]` — hard rejection at admission time

**Resource matching** — three explicit `resourceRules` entries cover:
- `""` / `v1` / `pods`
- `apps` / `v1` / `deployments`
- `batch` / `v1` / `cronjobs`

Both `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations are matched so existing resources can't be modified to add a hostPath either.

**Pod spec navigation** — a `variables.podSpec` CEL ternary resolves the pod spec from whichever resource kind is being evaluated (Pod → `object.spec`, Deployment → `object.spec.template.spec`, CronJob → `object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec`).

**The check itself** uses the pattern from Kyverno's own official CEL policies:
```yaml
variables.podSpec.?volumes.orValue([]).all(volume, !has(volume.hostPath))
```
- `?volumes` — optional chaining so resources with no `volumes` field pass cleanly
- `.orValue([])` — falls back to an empty list, avoiding null-dereference errors
- `.all(volume, !has(volume.hostPath))` — every volume must **not** have `hostPath` present, regardless of its value

description: denies any Pod, Deployment, or CronJob whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers set securityContext.privileged to true.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies any Pod, Deployment, or CronJob whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers set securityContext.privileged to true. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/gen_disallow_privileged.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: disallow-privileged-containers
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Privileged Containers
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Deployment, CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Privileged mode disables most security mechanisms and must not be allowed.
      This policy denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs whose containers,
      initContainers, or ephemeralContainers set securityContext.privileged to true.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
      - apiGroups: ["apps"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["deployments"]
      - apiGroups: ["batch"]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["cronjobs"]
  variables:
    - name: podSpec
      expression: >-
        object.kind == 'Pod' ? object.spec :
        object.kind == 'Deployment' ? object.spec.template.spec :
        object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        variables.podSpec.containers
        + variables.podSpec.?initContainers.orValue([])
        + variables.podSpec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(c,
          c.?securityContext.?privileged.orValue(false) == false
        )
      message: >-
        Privileged mode is disallowed. All containers must set
        securityContext.privileged to false or leave it unset.

The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what it does and the key design decisions:

**Policy overview:**

- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` — uses the stable v1 API introduced in Kyverno 1.17 (part of the 1.16+ generation)
- `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — cluster-scoped, applies across all namespaces
- `validationActions: [Deny]` — blocks the resource at admission time

**Resource matching:**

Covers all three resource types explicitly in `matchConstraints.resourceRules`:
- `pods` (core/v1)
- `deployments` (apps/v1)
- `cronjobs` (batch/v1)

**CEL logic:**

1. A `podSpec` variable normalises the location of the pod spec across all three resource kinds:
   - `Pod` → `object.spec`
   - `Deployment` → `object.spec.template.spec`
   - `CronJob` → `object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec`

2. An `allContainers` variable concatenates all three container lists, using `?` optional chaining + `.orValue([])` (Kyverno's extended CEL library) so missing `initContainers`/`ephemeralContainers` fields are treated as empty lists rather than causing an error.

3. The single validation expression uses `c.?securityContext.?privileged.orValue(false) == false` — this safely handles containers that omit `securityContext` or `privileged` entirely (both default to allowed), and only denies when `privileged: true` is explicitly set.

    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Privileged Containers
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Deployment, CronJob
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.16.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.30+"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Privileged mode disables most security mechanisms and must not be allowed.
      This policy denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs whose containers,
      initContainers, or ephemeralContainers set securityContext.privileged to true.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs
  variables:
    # For Pods: spec.containers / initContainers / ephemeralContainers are at object.spec
    # For Deployments: the pod spec is at object.spec.template.spec
    # For CronJobs: the pod spec is at object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
    - name: podSpec
      expression: >-
        object.kind == "Pod"
          ? object.spec
          : object.kind == "Deployment"
            ? object.spec.template.spec
            : object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        variables.podSpec.containers
          + variables.podSpec.?initContainers.orValue([])
          + variables.podSpec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(container,
          container.?securityContext.?privileged.orValue(false) == false
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        "Privileged mode is disallowed. The resource " + object.kind + "/" +
        object.metadata.name + " has one or more containers with " +
        "securityContext.privileged set to true. All containers must set " +
        "securityContext.privileged to false or leave the field unset."
runs.raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what it does and why each decision was made:

**Policy structure (`policies.kyverno.io/v1`, Kyverno 1.16+):**

- **`validationActions: [Deny]`** — hard-denies the admission request rather than just auditing it.
- **`matchConstraints.resourceRules`** — explicitly matches `pods` (core/v1), `deployments` (apps/v1), and `cronjobs` (batch/v1) on CREATE and UPDATE.
- **`variables.podSpec`** — a CEL variable that normalises the pod spec location across the three resource kinds:
  - Pod → `object.spec`
  - Deployment → `object.spec.template.spec`
  - CronJob → `object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec`
- **`variables.allContainers`** — concatenates `containers`, `initContainers`, and `ephemeralContainers` using the optional-chaining + `orValue([])` pattern (idiomatic Kyverno CEL) so absent lists don't cause an error.
- **`validations`** — uses `container.?securityContext.?privileged.orValue(false) == false` (the exact expression from the official Kyverno pod-security-vpol policy) to safely handle containers with no `securityContext` or no `privileged` field set.
- **`messageExpression`** — generates a dynamic denial message including the resource kind and name for clear operator feedback.

**Key design decisions:**

description: requires all Pods to have the label 'app.kubernetes.io/name' set to a non-empty value. Deny admission if the label is missing or empty.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that requires all Pods to have the label 'app.kubernetes.io/name' set to a non-empty value. Deny admission if the label is missing or empty. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/gen_require_labels.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-app-kubernetes-io-name-label
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require app.kubernetes.io/name Label
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Requires all Pods to have the 'app.kubernetes.io/name' label set to a
      non-empty value. Admission is denied if the label is missing or empty.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: [""]
        apiVersions: ["v1"]
        operations: ["CREATE", "UPDATE"]
        resources: ["pods"]
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        has(object.metadata.labels) &&
        'app.kubernetes.io/name' in object.metadata.labels &&
        size(object.metadata.labels['app.kubernetes.io/name']) > 0
      messageExpression: >-
        !has(object.metadata.labels) || !('app.kubernetes.io/name' in object.metadata.labels)
          ? "Pod '" + object.metadata.name + "' is missing required label 'app.kubernetes.io/name'"
          : "Pod '" + object.metadata.name + "' has an empty 'app.kubernetes.io/name' label; it must be set to a non-empty value"
      message: "Pods must have the label 'app.kubernetes.io/name' set to a non-empty value"
raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what it does:

**`require-app-kubernetes-io-name-label`** — a cluster-scoped `ValidatingPolicy` (apiVersion: `policies.kyverno.io/v1`) that:

- **Matches** all `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations on `pods` (core API group, `v1`).
- **Denies** admission (`validationActions: [Deny]`) when any of these conditions are true:
  - `metadata.labels` is absent entirely
  - the key `app.kubernetes.io/name` is not present in the labels map
  - the value of `app.kubernetes.io/name` is an empty string (`size(...) > 0` check)
- **Dynamic message** — uses `messageExpression` to emit a context-aware error distinguishing "missing label" from "empty label", with the Pod name included. Falls back to the static `message` if the expression fails.

Key CEL idioms used, per the 1.16+ docs:
- `has(object.metadata.labels)` — safe existence check before accessing the map
- `'app.kubernetes.io/name' in object.metadata.labels` — membership test (handles the slash in the key correctly when used with bracket notation)
- `size(...) > 0` — guards against an empty-string value

description: requires every container in a Pod to set spec.containers[].resources.requests.memory, spec.containers[].resources.requests.cpu, and spec.containers[].resources.limits.memory. Deny admission if any container is missing any of these three fields. CPU limit is NOT required.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.16+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that requires every container in a Pod to set spec.containers[].resources.requests.memory, spec.containers[].resources.requests.cpu, and spec.containers[].resources.limits.memory. Deny admission if any container is missing any of these three fields. CPU limit is NOT required. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/gen_require_resource_limits.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.16+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-container-resources
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Container Resource Requests and Memory Limit
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Every container must explicitly set memory request, CPU request, and memory
      limit. CPU limit is not required. Admission is denied if any container is
      missing any of these three fields.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources: ['pods']
  variables:
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers
        + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([])
        + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(c,
          has(c.resources) &&
          has(c.resources.requests) &&
          has(c.resources.requests.memory)
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        "Container '" +
        variables.allContainers.filter(c,
          !(has(c.resources) &&
            has(c.resources.requests) &&
            has(c.resources.requests.memory))
        )[0].name +
        "' is missing resources.requests.memory"
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(c,
          has(c.resources) &&
          has(c.resources.requests) &&
          has(c.resources.requests.cpu)
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        "Container '" +
        variables.allContainers.filter(c,
          !(has(c.resources) &&
            has(c.resources.requests) &&
            has(c.resources.requests.cpu))
        )[0].name +
        "' is missing resources.requests.cpu"
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(c,
          has(c.resources) &&
          has(c.resources.limits) &&
          has(c.resources.limits.memory)
        )
      messageExpression: >-
        "Container '" +
        variables.allContainers.filter(c,
          !(has(c.resources) &&
            has(c.resources.limits) &&
            has(c.resources.limits.memory))
        )[0].name +
        "' is missing resources.limits.memory"
raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what it does:

**Policy: `require-container-resources`**

- **apiVersion**: `policies.kyverno.io/v1` (Kyverno 1.16+ `ValidatingPolicy`)
- **Action**: `Deny` — blocks admission if any container violates the rules
- **Scope**: Applies to Pod `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations across all namespaces

**Three separate validations**, each using `has()` CEL field existence checks:

1. `resources.requests.memory` — required on every container
2. `resources.requests.cpu` — required on every container
3. `resources.limits.memory` — required on every container
4. `resources.limits.cpu` — **not** checked (intentionally omitted per requirements)

**Key design choices:**
- Uses a `variables.allContainers` variable that includes `containers`, `initContainers`, and `ephemeralContainers` (using `?field.orValue([])` for optional fields), so the check applies to all container types in a Pod.
- Each validation has a `messageExpression` that dynamically names the first offending container, making denial messages actionable.
- The `has(c.resources)` and `has(c.resources.requests/limits)` guards prevent CEL field-access errors on containers that omit the parent object entirely.

Deny any Pod whose containers use images from any other registry. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/cursor/gen_restrict_registries.yaml.

output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-image-registries
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Image Registries
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Images from unknown or public registries can be of dubious quality and may
      not be scanned or secured. This policy validates that all container images
      (including init and ephemeral containers) originate only from the approved
      registries 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups: ['']
        apiVersions: ['v1']
        operations: ['CREATE', 'UPDATE']
        resources: ['pods']
  variables:
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers
        + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([])
        + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(c,
          c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') || c.image.startsWith('bar.io/'))
      messageExpression: >-
        "All container images must be from 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'. Offending images: "
        + variables.allContainers.filter(c,
            !c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') && !c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')
          ).map(c, c.image).join(", ")

- **`messageExpression`** — produces a dynamic denial message that lists the specific offending image names, making it easy to debug failures.

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-image-registries
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Image Registries
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: "1.16.0"
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Requires all Pod container images to originate from the approved
      registries 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'. Any image from a different
      registry is denied at admission time.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  variables:
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers +
        object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) +
        object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(container,
          container.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') ||
          container.image.startsWith('bar.io/')
        )
      message: >-
        All container images must be from an approved registry.
        Only 'eu.foo.io/' and 'bar.io/' are permitted.
      messageExpression: >-
        "Container image '" +
        variables.allContainers.filter(c,
          !c.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') && !c.image.startsWith('bar.io/')
        )[0].image +
        "' is not from an approved registry. Only 'eu.foo.io/' and 'bar.io/' are allowed."
runs.raw_log:
The policy has been written to `/workspace/output/converted.yaml`. Here's a summary of what it does and why each part is structured this way:

**Key design decisions based on the docs:**

- `apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` — the stable Kyverno 1.16+ API (not the older `kyverno.io/v1` ClusterPolicy type).
- `kind: ValidatingPolicy` — cluster-scoped, applies across all namespaces.
- `validationActions: [Deny]` — hard deny at admission; no audit-only.
- `matchConstraints` — matches Pod `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations on the core API group.
- **`variables.allContainers`** — collects `containers`, `initContainers`, and `ephemeralContainers` into a single list using the `.orValue([])` CEL helper (handles optional fields safely), so no container type is overlooked.
- **`validations[].expression`** — uses CEL's `.all()` macro to assert every container image starts with `eu.foo.io/` or `bar.io/`. If any container violates this, the entire Pod is denied.
- **`messageExpression`** — produces a dynamic error message that surfaces the first offending image name, making it easy to diagnose the denial.

runs.output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-image-registries
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Image Registries
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Images from unknown, public registries can be of dubious quality and may not be
      scanned and secured, representing a high degree of risk. Requiring use of known,
      approved registries helps reduce threat exposure by ensuring image pulls only come
      from them. This policy validates that container images only originate from the
      registry 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'. Pods whose containers use images from any
      other registry will be denied.
spec:
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  variables:
    - name: allContainers
      expression: >-
        object.spec.containers
        + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([])
        + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
  validations:
    - expression: >-
        variables.allContainers.all(container,
          container.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') ||
          container.image.startsWith('bar.io/')
        )
      message: >-
        All container images must be from an approved registry ('eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/').
        Unknown image registry is not allowed.

**`apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1` / `kind: ValidatingPolicy`** — uses the Kyverno 1.16+ `ValidatingPolicy` type (the stable, superset of `ValidatingAdmissionPolicy`).

Key design decisions based on the docs:

- **`validationActions: [Deny]`** — actively rejects Pods at admission time rather than just auditing.
- **`matchConstraints`** — targets `CREATE` and `UPDATE` operations on `pods` in core API group (`""`).
- **`variables.allContainers`** — collects all three container lists (`containers`, `initContainers`, `ephemeralContainers`) using Kyverno's CEL optional chaining with `.orValue([])`, so every container type is checked.
- **`validations` CEL expression** — uses `.all()` to assert every container image `startsWith('eu.foo.io/')` or `startsWith('bar.io/')`. Any image from any other registry causes the entire Pod to be denied.

The policy labels are used as an important source of metadata describing objects in various ways or triggering other functionality. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/cp_add_default_labels.yaml.

input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-labels
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Labels
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Label
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Labels are used as an important source of metadata describing objects in various ways
      or triggering other functionality. Labels are also a very basic concept and should be
      used throughout Kubernetes. This policy performs a simple mutation which adds a label
      `foo=bar` to Pods, Services, ConfigMaps, and Secrets.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: add-labels
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
          - Service
          - ConfigMap
          - Secret
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        metadata:
          labels:
            foo: bar

description: pods which don't specify at least resource requests are assigned a QoS class of BestEffort which can hog resources for other Pods on Nodes
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_default_resources.yaml to a Kyverno 1.17+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy pods which don't specify at least resource requests are assigned a QoS class of BestEffort which can hog resources for other Pods on Nodes. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/cp_add_default_resources.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion : kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-default-resources
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Default Resources
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.10.0-alpha.2
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.7.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.26"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Pods which don't specify at least resource requests are assigned a QoS class
      of BestEffort which can hog resources for other Pods on Nodes. At a minimum,
      all Pods should specify resource requests in order to be labeled as the QoS
      class Burstable. This sample mutates any container in a Pod which doesn't
      specify memory or cpu requests to apply some sane defaults.
spec:
  background: false
  rules:
  - name: add-default-requests
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    preconditions:
      any:
      - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
        operator: AnyIn
        value:
        - CREATE
        - UPDATE
    mutate:
      foreach:
      - list: "request.object.spec.[ephemeralContainers, initContainers, containers][]"
        patchStrategicMerge:
          spec:
            containers:
            - (name): "{{element.name}}"
              resources:
                requests:
                  +(memory): "100Mi"
                  +(cpu): "100m"

description: the ndots value controls where DNS lookups are first performed in a cluster and needs to be set to a lower value than the default of 5 in some cases
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_ndots.yaml to a Kyverno 1.17+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy the ndots value controls where DNS lookups are first performed in a cluster and needs to be set to a lower value than the default of 5 in some cases. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/cp_add_ndots.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-ndots
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add ndots
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The ndots value controls where DNS lookups are first performed in a cluster
      and needs to be set to a lower value than the default of 5 in some cases.
      This policy mutates all Pods to add the ndots option with a value of 1.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: add-ndots
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        spec:
          dnsConfig:
            options:
              - name: ndots
                value: "1"

description: to better control the number of resources that can be created in a given Namespace and provide default resource consumption limits for Pods, ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources are recommended
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_ns_quota.yaml to a Kyverno 1.17+ GeneratingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy to better control the number of resources that can be created in a given Namespace and provide default resource consumption limits for Pods, ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources are recommended. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/cp_add_ns_quota.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-ns-quota
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Quota
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Multi-Tenancy, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: ResourceQuota, LimitRange
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      To better control the number of resources that can be created in a given
      Namespace and provide default resource consumption limits for Pods,
      ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources are recommended.
      This policy will generate ResourceQuota and LimitRange resources when
      a new Namespace is created.
spec:
  rules:
  - name: generate-resourcequota
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Namespace
    generate:
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: ResourceQuota
      name: default-resourcequota
      synchronize: true
      namespace: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
      data:
        spec:
          hard:
            requests.cpu: '4'
            requests.memory: '16Gi'
            limits.cpu: '4'
            limits.memory: '16Gi'
  - name: generate-limitrange
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Namespace
    generate:
      apiVersion: v1
      kind: LimitRange
      name: default-limitrange
      synchronize: true
      namespace: "{{request.object.metadata.name}}"
      data:
        spec:
          limits:
          - default:
              cpu: 500m
              memory: 1Gi
            defaultRequest:
              cpu: 200m
              memory: 256Mi
            type: Container

description: the Kubernetes cluster autoscaler does not evict pods that  use hostPath or emptyDir volumes
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_add_safe_to_evict.yaml to a Kyverno 1.17+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy the Kubernetes cluster autoscaler does not evict pods that  use hostPath or emptyDir volumes. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/cp_add_safe_to_evict.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: add-safe-to-evict
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Safe To Evict
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Annotation
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      The Kubernetes cluster autoscaler does not evict pods that 
      use hostPath or emptyDir volumes. To allow eviction of these pods, the annotation 
      cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict=true must be added to the pods. 
spec: 
  rules: 
  - name: annotate-empty-dir
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        metadata:
          annotations:
            +(cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict): "true"
        spec:          
          volumes: 
          - <(emptyDir): {}
  - name: annotate-host-path
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    mutate:
      patchStrategicMerge:
        metadata:
          annotations:
            +(cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict): "true"
        spec:          
          volumes: 
          - hostPath:
              <(path): "*"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Other
    policies.kyverno.io/description: The Kubernetes cluster autoscaler does not evict pods that use hostPath or emptyDir volumes. To allow eviction of these pods, the annotation cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict=true must be added to the pods.
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod,Annotation
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Safe To Evict
  name: add-safe-to-evict
spec:
  matchConditions:
    - expression: has(object.spec.volumes) && object.spec.volumes.exists(v, has(v.emptyDir) || has(v.hostPath))
      name: has-emptydir-or-hostpath-volume
    - expression: "!object.metadata.?annotations[?'cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict'].hasValue()"
      name: annotation-not-already-set
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  mutations:
    - applyConfiguration:
        expression: |-
          Object{
            metadata: Object.metadata{
              annotations: {
                "cluster-autoscaler.kubernetes.io/safe-to-evict": "true"
              }
            }
          }
      patchType: ApplyConfiguration
