  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false
  matchConditions:
    - expression: object.spec.?replicas.orValue(1) >= 3
      name: min-replicas
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
        resources:
          - deployments
          - statefulsets
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: size(variables.matchingPdbs) >= 1
      message: There is no corresponding PodDisruptionBudget found for this Deployment or StatefulSet.
  variables:
    - expression: object.spec.template.metadata.?labels.orValue({})
      name: podLabels
    - expression: "resource.List('policy/v1', 'poddisruptionbudgets', object.metadata.namespace).items.orValue([])"
      name: pdbs
    - expression: variables.pdbs.filter(pdb, pdb.spec.?selector.?matchLabels.orValue({}).all(k, k in variables.podLabels && variables.podLabels[k] == pdb.spec.selector.matchLabels[k]))
      name: matchingPdbs

description: liveness and readiness probes need to be configured to correctly manage a Pod's lifecycle during deployments, restarts, and upgrades
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_probes.yaml to a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy liveness and readiness probes need to be configured to correctly manage a Pod's lifecycle during deployments, restarts, and upgrades. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/cp_require_probes.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-pod-probes
  annotations:
    pod-policies.kyverno.io/autogen-controllers: DaemonSet,Deployment,StatefulSet
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Pod Probes
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Liveness and readiness probes need to be configured to correctly manage a Pod's
      lifecycle during deployments, restarts, and upgrades. For each Pod, a periodic
      `livenessProbe` is performed by the kubelet to determine if the Pod's containers
      are running or need to be restarted. A `readinessProbe` is used by Services
      and Deployments to determine if the Pod is ready to receive network traffic.
      This policy validates that all containers have one of livenessProbe, readinessProbe,
      or startupProbe defined.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-probes
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    preconditions:
      all:
      - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
        operator: AnyIn
        value:
        - CREATE
        - UPDATE
    validate:
      message: "Liveness, readiness, or startup probes are required for all containers."
      foreach:
      - list: request.object.spec.containers[]
        deny:
          conditions:
            all:
            - key: livenessProbe
              operator: AllNotIn
              value: "{{ element.keys(@)[] }}"
            - key: startupProbe
              operator: AllNotIn
              value: "{{ element.keys(@)[] }}"
            - key: readinessProbe
              operator: AllNotIn
              value: "{{ element.keys(@)[] }}"
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/description: "Liveness and readiness probes need to be configured to correctly manage a Pod's lifecycle during deployments, restarts, and upgrades. For each Pod, a periodic `livenessProbe` is performed by the kubelet to determine if the Pod's containers are running or need to be restarted. A `readinessProbe` is used by Services and Deployments to determine if the Pod is ready to receive network traffic. This policy validates that all containers have one of livenessProbe, readinessProbe, or startupProbe defined."
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Pod Probes
  name: require-pod-probes
spec:
  autogen:
    podControllers:
      controllers:
        - DaemonSet
        - Deployment
        - StatefulSet
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: variables.allContainers.all(c, has(c.livenessProbe) || has(c.readinessProbe) || has(c.startupProbe))
      messageExpression: "'Liveness, readiness, or startup probes are required for all containers. Missing probes in containers: ' + variables.allContainers.filter(c, !(has(c.livenessProbe) || has(c.readinessProbe) || has(c.startupProbe))).map(c, c.name).join(', ')"
  variables:
    - expression: object.spec.containers + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
      name: allContainers

description: as application workloads share cluster resources, it is important to limit resources requested and consumed by each Pod
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_resource_limits.yaml to a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy as application workloads share cluster resources, it is important to limit resources requested and consumed by each Pod. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/cp_require_resource_limits.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-requests-limits
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Limits and Requests
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      As application workloads share cluster resources, it is important to limit resources
      requested and consumed by each Pod. It is recommended to require resource requests and
      limits per Pod, especially for memory and CPU. If a Namespace level request or limit is specified,
      defaults will automatically be applied to each Pod based on the LimitRange configuration.
      This policy validates that all containers have something specified for memory and CPU
      requests and memory limits.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-resources
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "CPU and memory resource requests and memory limits are required for containers."
      pattern:
        spec:
          containers:
          - resources:
              requests:
                memory: "?*"
                cpu: "?*"
              limits:
                memory: "?*"
          =(initContainers):
          - resources:
              requests:
                memory: "?*"
                cpu: "?*"
              limits:
                memory: "?*"
          =(ephemeralContainers):
          - resources:
              requests:
                memory: "?*"
                cpu: "?*"
              limits:
                memory: "?*"

description: a read-only root file system helps to enforce an immutable infrastructure strategy; the container only needs to write on the mounted volume that persists the state
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_require_ro_rootfs.yaml to a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy a read-only root file system helps to enforce an immutable infrastructure strategy; the container only needs to write on the mounted volume that persists the state. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/cp_require_ro_rootfs.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: require-ro-rootfs
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Read-Only Root Filesystem
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices, PSP Migration
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A read-only root file system helps to enforce an immutable infrastructure strategy;
      the container only needs to write on the mounted volume that persists the state.
      An immutable root filesystem can also prevent malicious binaries from writing to the
      host system. This policy validates that containers define a securityContext
      with `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-readOnlyRootFilesystem
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Root filesystem must be read-only."
      pattern:
        spec:
          containers:
          - securityContext:
              readOnlyRootFilesystem: true
output_yaml:

An immutable root filesystem can also prevent malicious binaries from writing to the host system. This policy validates that containers define a securityContext with `readOnlyRootFilesystem: true`.'

  name: require-ro-rootfs

  matchConstraints:

    - expression: object.spec.containers + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
      name: allContainers

description: adding capabilities is a way for containers in a Pod to request higher levels of ability than those with which they may be provisioned
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_restrict_capabilities.yaml to a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy adding capabilities is a way for containers in a Pod to request higher levels of ability than those with which they may be provisioned. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/cp_restrict_capabilities.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: psp-restrict-adding-capabilities
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Adding Capabilities
    policies.kyverno.io/category: PSP Migration
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    kyverno.io/kyverno-version: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.23"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Adding capabilities is a way for containers in a Pod to request higher levels
      of ability than those with which they may be provisioned. Many capabilities
      allow system-level control and should be prevented. Pod Security Policies (PSP)
      allowed a list of "good" capabilities to be added. This policy checks
      ephemeralContainers, initContainers, and containers to ensure the only
      capabilities that can be added are either NET_BIND_SERVICE or CAP_CHOWN.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
    - name: allowed-capabilities
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Pod
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{ request.operation || 'BACKGROUND' }}"
          operator: NotEquals
          value: DELETE
      validate:
        message: >-
          Any capabilities added other than NET_BIND_SERVICE or CAP_CHOWN are disallowed.
        foreach:
          - list: request.object.spec.[ephemeralContainers, initContainers, containers][]
            deny:
              conditions:
                all:
                - key: "{{ element.securityContext.capabilities.add[] || '' }}"
                  operator: AnyNotIn
                  value:
                  - NET_BIND_SERVICE
                  - CAP_CHOWN
                  - ''
output_yaml:

    policies.kyverno.io/category: PSP Migration
    policies.kyverno.io/description: Adding capabilities is a way for containers in a Pod to request higher levels of ability than those with which they may be provisioned. Many capabilities allow system-level control and should be prevented. Pod Security Policies (PSP) allowed a list of "good" capabilities to be added. This policy checks ephemeralContainers, initContainers, and containers to ensure the only capabilities that can be added are either NET_BIND_SERVICE or CAP_CHOWN.
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Adding Capabilities
  name: psp-restrict-adding-capabilities
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: variables.allContainers.all(c, !has(c.securityContext) || !has(c.securityContext.capabilities) || !has(c.securityContext.capabilities.add) || c.securityContext.capabilities.add.all(cap, cap in variables.allowedCaps))

      name: allowedCaps
    - expression: object.spec.containers + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
      name: allContainers

description: images from unknown, public registries can be of dubious quality and may not be scanned and secured, representing a high degree of risk
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_restrict_image_registries.yaml to a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy images from unknown, public registries can be of dubious quality and may not be scanned and secured, representing a high degree of risk. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/cp_restrict_image_registries.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-image-registries
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Image Registries
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, EKS Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    kyverno.io/kubernetes-version: "1.26"
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      Images from unknown, public registries can be of dubious quality and may not be
      scanned and secured, representing a high degree of risk. Requiring use of known, approved
      registries helps reduce threat exposure by ensuring image pulls only come from them. This
      policy validates that container images only originate from the registry `eu.foo.io` or
      `bar.io`. Use of this policy requires customization to define your allowable registries.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-registries
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Pod
    validate:
      message: "Unknown image registry."
      pattern:

          containers:

description: an Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_restrict_ingress_host.yaml to a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy an Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/cp_restrict_ingress_host.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: unique-ingress-host
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Unique Ingress Host
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Ingress
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      An Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally. In most cases,
      these hosts should be unique across the cluster to ensure no routing conflicts occur.
      This policy checks an incoming Ingress resource to ensure its hosts are unique to the cluster.
      It also ensures that only a single host may be specified in a given manifest.      
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: false
  rules:
    - name: check-single-host-create
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Ingress
      context:
        - name: hosts
          apiCall:
            urlPath: "/apis/networking.k8s.io/v1/ingresses"
            jmesPath: "items[].spec.rules[].host"
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: Equals
          value: CREATE
      validate:
        message: "The Ingress host name must be unique."
        deny:
          conditions:
            all:
              - key: "{{ request.object.spec.rules[].host }}"
                operator: AnyIn
                value: "{{ hosts }}"
    - name: check-single-host-update
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Ingress
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: Equals
          value: UPDATE
      context:
        - name: allhosts
          apiCall:
            urlPath: "/apis/networking.k8s.io/v1/ingresses"
            jmesPath: "items[?metadata.uid!='{{ request.object.metadata.uid }}'].spec.rules[].host"
      validate:
        message: "The Ingress host name must be unique."
        deny:
          conditions:
            all:
              - key: "{{ request.object.spec.rules[].host }}"
                operator: AnyIn
                value: "{{ allhosts }}"
    - name: deny-multiple-hosts
      match:
        any:
        - resources:
            kinds:
              - Ingress
      preconditions:
        all:
        - key: "{{request.operation || 'BACKGROUND'}}"
          operator: AnyIn
          value:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        - key: "{{ request.object.spec.rules[].host | length(@)}}"
          operator: GreaterThan
          value: 1
      validate:
        message: "An Ingress resource may only contain a single host entry."
        deny: {}
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/description: An Ingress host is a URL at which services may be made available externally. In most cases, these hosts should be unique across the cluster to ensure no routing conflicts occur. This policy checks an incoming Ingress resource to ensure its hosts are unique to the cluster. It also ensures that only a single host may be specified in a given manifest.
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.14.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Ingress
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Unique Ingress Host
  name: unique-ingress-host
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: false
  matchConditions:
    - expression: has(object.spec.rules) && size(object.spec.rules) > 0
      name: has-rules
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - networking.k8s.io
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - ingresses
  validationActions:
    - Audit
  validations:
    - expression: "request.operation != 'CREATE' || variables.incomingHosts.all(h, !(h in variables.existingHostsCreate))"
      message: The Ingress host name must be unique.
    - expression: "request.operation != 'UPDATE' || variables.incomingHosts.all(h, !(h in variables.existingHostsUpdate))"
      message: The Ingress host name must be unique.
    - expression: size(object.spec.?rules.orValue([])) <= 1
      message: An Ingress resource may only contain a single host entry.
  variables:
    - expression: object.spec.?rules.orValue([]).filter(r, has(r.host)).map(r, r.host)
      name: incomingHosts
    - expression: "resource.List('networking.k8s.io/v1', 'ingresses', '').items.orValue([])"
      name: allIngresses
    - expression: variables.allIngresses.map(ing, ing.spec.?rules.orValue([]).filter(r, has(r.host)).map(r, r.host)).flatten()
      name: existingHostsCreate
    - expression: variables.allIngresses.filter(ing, ing.metadata.uid != object.metadata.uid).map(ing, ing.spec.?rules.orValue([]).filter(r, has(r.host)).map(r, r.host)).flatten()
      name: existingHostsUpdate

description: a Kubernetes Service of type NodePort uses a host port to receive traffic from any source
prompt:
Convert the Kyverno ClusterPolicy in /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/dataset/imported/kyverno-policies/cp_restrict_nodeport.yaml to a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1). The policy a Kubernetes Service of type NodePort uses a host port to receive traffic from any source. Write the converted policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/cp_restrict_nodeport.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
input_snippet:
apiVersion: kyverno.io/v1
kind: ClusterPolicy
metadata:
  name: restrict-nodeport
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow NodePort
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.6.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Service
    policies.kyverno.io/description: >-
      A Kubernetes Service of type NodePort uses a host port to receive traffic from
      any source. A NetworkPolicy cannot be used to control traffic to host ports.
      Although NodePort Services can be useful, their use must be limited to Services
      with additional upstream security checks. This policy validates that any new Services
      do not use the `NodePort` type.
spec:
  validationFailureAction: Audit
  background: true
  rules:
  - name: validate-nodeport
    match:
      any:
      - resources:
          kinds:
          - Service
    validate:
      message: "Services of type NodePort are not allowed."
      pattern:
        spec:
          =(type): "!NodePort"

description: mutates Pods and Services to add the label 'foo: bar' to metadata.labels. The label must be added if absent and left unchanged if already present.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.17+ MutatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that mutates Pods and Services to add the label 'foo: bar' to metadata.labels. The label must be added if absent and left unchanged if already present. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/gen_add_default_labels.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: MutatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Sample
    policies.kyverno.io/description: "Adds the label 'foo: bar' to Pods and Services if the label is not already present. Resources that already have the 'foo' label are excluded via matchConditions and left unchanged."
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: low
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod, Service
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Foo Label
  name: add-foo-label
spec:
  matchConditions:
    - expression: "!object.metadata.?labels[?'foo'].hasValue()"
      name: missing-foo-label
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
          - services
  mutations:
    - applyConfiguration:
        expression: |-
          Object{
            metadata: Object.metadata{
              labels: Object.metadata.labels{
                foo: "bar"
              }
            }
          }
      patchType: ApplyConfiguration

  Key design decisions:
  
  • apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1 — used verbatim as requested
  • **"Add only if absent"** — implemented via a top-level matchConditions guard (!object.metadata.?labels[?'foo'].hasValue()) that skips resources already carrying the foo label, rather than using a ternary in the mutation expression. description: generates a NetworkPolicy named 'default-deny' in any newly-created Namespace. The generated NetworkPolicy must select all Pods (empty podSelector), include both 'Ingress' and 'Egress' policyTypes, and define no ingress or egress rules so all traffic is denied by default.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.17+ GeneratingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that generates a NetworkPolicy named 'default-deny' in any newly-created Namespace. The generated NetworkPolicy must select all Pods (empty podSelector), include both 'Ingress' and 'Egress' policyTypes, and define no ingress or egress rules so all traffic is denied by default. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/gen_create_networkpolicy.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: GeneratingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Multi-Tenancy, Network Security
    policies.kyverno.io/description: "By default, Kubernetes allows communications across all Pods within a cluster. A default-deny NetworkPolicy should be configured for each Namespace to deny all ingress and egress traffic to the Pods in the Namespace by default. This policy generates a NetworkPolicy named 'default-deny' that selects all Pods and denies all ingress and egress traffic whenever a new Namespace is created. Application teams can then add additional NetworkPolicy resources to selectively allow desired traffic."
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Add Default Deny NetworkPolicy
  name: add-default-deny-networkpolicy
spec:
  evaluation:
    synchronize:
      enabled: true
  generate:
    - expression: generator.Apply(variables.targetNs, variables.downstream)
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
        resources:
          - namespaces
  variables:
    - expression: object.metadata.name
      name: targetNs
    - expression: |-
        [
          {
            "kind": dyn("NetworkPolicy"),
            "apiVersion": dyn("networking.k8s.io/v1"),
            "metadata": dyn({
              "name": "default-deny",
              "namespace": string(variables.targetNs)
            }),
            "spec": dyn({
              "podSelector": dyn({}),
              "policyTypes": dyn(["Ingress", "Egress"])
            })
          }
        ]
      name: downstream

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers add any Linux capability via securityContext.capabilities.add beyond the Pod Security Standards baseline allow-list (AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT).
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers add any Linux capability via securityContext.capabilities.add beyond the Pod Security Standards baseline allow-list (AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT). Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/gen_disallow_capabilities.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security
    policies.kyverno.io/description: 'Adding Linux capabilities beyond those listed in the Pod Security Standards baseline allow-list is disallowed. Only the following capabilities are permitted: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT. This policy explicitly matches Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs and checks all container types (containers, initContainers, ephemeralContainers).'
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Capabilities Beyond Baseline
  name: disallow-capabilities-beyond-baseline
spec:
  autogen:
    podControllers:
      controllers: []
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: "!(object.apiVersion == 'v1' && object.kind == 'Pod') || variables.podContainers.all(c, c.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).all(cap, cap in variables.allowedCapabilities))"
      message: 'Pod containers may only add capabilities from the baseline allow-list: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT. Remove any capability not in this list from securityContext.capabilities.add.'
    - expression: "!(object.kind == 'Deployment') || variables.deploymentContainers.all(c, c.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).all(cap, cap in variables.allowedCapabilities))"
      message: 'Deployment containers may only add capabilities from the baseline allow-list: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT. Remove any capability not in this list from securityContext.capabilities.add.'
    - expression: "!(object.kind == 'CronJob') || variables.cronJobContainers.all(c, c.?securityContext.?capabilities.?add.orValue([]).all(cap, cap in variables.allowedCapabilities))"
      message: 'CronJob containers may only add capabilities from the baseline allow-list: AUDIT_WRITE, CHOWN, DAC_OVERRIDE, FOWNER, FSETID, KILL, MKNOD, NET_BIND_SERVICE, SETFCAP, SETGID, SETPCAP, SETUID, SYS_CHROOT. Remove any capability not in this list from securityContext.capabilities.add.'
  variables:
    - expression: "['AUDIT_WRITE','CHOWN','DAC_OVERRIDE','FOWNER','FSETID','KILL','MKNOD','NET_BIND_SERVICE','SETFCAP','SETGID','SETPCAP','SETUID','SYS_CHROOT']"
      name: allowedCapabilities
    - expression: object.spec.containers + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
      name: podContainers
    - expression: object.spec.template.spec.containers + object.spec.template.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.template.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
      name: deploymentContainers
    - expression: object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.containers + object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
      name: cronJobContainers

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that set spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, or spec.hostIPC to true. All three host namespace fields must be checked.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that set spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, or spec.hostIPC to true. All three host namespace fields must be checked. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/gen_disallow_host_namespaces.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security
    policies.kyverno.io/description: Host namespaces (Process ID namespace, Inter-Process Communication namespace, and network namespace) allow access to shared information and can be used to elevate privileges. Pods should not be allowed access to host namespaces. This policy ensures that spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostIPC, and spec.hostPID are unset or set to false for Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs.
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Host Namespaces
  name: disallow-host-namespaces
spec:
  autogen:
    podControllers:
      controllers: []
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: "request.kind.kind != 'Pod' || !(object.spec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false) || object.spec.?hostPID.orValue(false) || object.spec.?hostIPC.orValue(false))"
      message: Sharing host namespaces is disallowed. The fields spec.hostNetwork, spec.hostPID, and spec.hostIPC must be unset or set to false.
    - expression: "request.kind.kind != 'Deployment' || !(object.spec.template.spec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false) || object.spec.template.spec.?hostPID.orValue(false) || object.spec.template.spec.?hostIPC.orValue(false))"
      message: Sharing host namespaces is disallowed in Deployments. The fields spec.template.spec.hostNetwork, spec.template.spec.hostPID, and spec.template.spec.hostIPC must be unset or set to false.
    - expression: "request.kind.kind != 'CronJob' || !(object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?hostNetwork.orValue(false) || object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?hostPID.orValue(false) || object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?hostIPC.orValue(false))"
      message: Sharing host namespaces is disallowed in CronJobs. The fields spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.hostNetwork, spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.hostPID, and spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.hostIPC must be unset or set to false.

description: denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that use any hostPath volume. The check must reject any volume in spec.volumes whose hostPath field is set, regardless of the hostPath value.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs that use any hostPath volume. The check must reject any volume in spec.volumes whose hostPath field is set, regardless of the hostPath value. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/gen_disallow_host_path.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/description: HostPath volumes let Pods use host directories and volumes in containers. Using host resources can be used to access shared data or escalate privileges and should not be allowed. This policy ensures no hostPath volumes are in use across Pods, Deployments, and CronJobs.
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow hostPath Volumes
  name: disallow-host-path
spec:
  autogen:
    podControllers:
      controllers: []
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: variables.podVolumes.all(volume, !has(volume.hostPath))
      messageExpression: "'HostPath volumes are forbidden in Pods. Remove any spec.volumes[*].hostPath entries from ' + object.metadata.name + '.'"
    - expression: variables.deploymentVolumes.all(volume, !has(volume.hostPath))
      messageExpression: "'HostPath volumes are forbidden in Deployments. Remove any spec.template.spec.volumes[*].hostPath entries from ' + object.metadata.name + '.'"
    - expression: variables.cronJobVolumes.all(volume, !has(volume.hostPath))
      messageExpression: "'HostPath volumes are forbidden in CronJobs. Remove any spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes[*].hostPath entries from ' + object.metadata.name + '.'"
  variables:
    - expression: "object.kind == 'Pod' ? object.spec.?volumes.orValue([]) : []"
      name: podVolumes
    - expression: "object.kind == 'Deployment' ? object.spec.template.spec.?volumes.orValue([]) : []"
      name: deploymentVolumes
    - expression: "object.kind == 'CronJob' ? object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?volumes.orValue([]) : []"
      name: cronJobVolumes

        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: variables.podVolumes.all(volume, !has(volume.hostPath))
      messageExpression: "'HostPath volumes are forbidden in Pods. Remove any spec.volumes[*].hostPath entries from ' + object.metadata.name + '.'"
    - expression: variables.deploymentVolumes.all(volume, !has(volume.hostPath))
      messageExpression: "'HostPath volumes are forbidden in Deployments. Remove any spec.template.spec.volumes[*].hostPath entries from ' + object.metadata.name + '.'"
    - expression: variables.cronJobVolumes.all(volume, !has(volume.hostPath))
      messageExpression: "'HostPath volumes are forbidden in CronJobs. Remove any spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.volumes[*].hostPath entries from ' + object.metadata.name + '.'"
  variables:
    - expression: "object.kind == 'Pod' ? object.spec.?volumes.orValue([]) : []"
      name: podVolumes
    - expression: "object.kind == 'Deployment' ? object.spec.template.spec.?volumes.orValue([]) : []"
      name: deploymentVolumes
    - expression: "object.kind == 'CronJob' ? object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?volumes.orValue([]) : []"
      name: cronJobVolumes

description: denies any Pod, Deployment, or CronJob whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers set securityContext.privileged to true.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that denies any Pod, Deployment, or CronJob whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers set securityContext.privileged to true. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/gen_disallow_privileged.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Pod Security Standards (Baseline)
    policies.kyverno.io/description: Privileged mode disables most security mechanisms and grants the container near-unrestricted access to the host. This policy denies any Pod, Deployment, or CronJob whose containers, initContainers, or ephemeralContainers set securityContext.privileged to true.
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Disallow Privileged Containers
  name: disallow-privileged-containers
spec:
  autogen:
    podControllers:
      controllers:
        - Deployment
        - StatefulSet
        - DaemonSet
        - Job
        - CronJob
        - ReplicaSet
        - ReplicationController
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
      - apiGroups:
          - apps
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - deployments
      - apiGroups:
          - batch
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - cronjobs
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: variables.allContainers.all(container, container.?securityContext.?privileged.orValue(false) == false)
      messageExpression: "'Privileged mode is disallowed. The following containers have securityContext.privileged set to true: ' + variables.allContainers.filter(container, container.?securityContext.?privileged.orValue(false) == true).map(container, container.name).join(', ')"
  variables:
    - expression: 'has(object.spec.containers) ? object.spec.containers + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([]) : has(object.spec.template) ? object.spec.template.spec.containers + object.spec.template.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.template.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([]) : object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.containers + object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.jobTemplate.spec.template.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])'
      name: allContainers

prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that requires all Pods to have the label 'app.kubernetes.io/name' set to a non-empty value. Deny admission if the label is missing or empty. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/gen_require_labels.yaml.

- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies

apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/description: "Requires all Pods to have the label 'app.kubernetes.io/name' set to a non-empty value. Admission is denied if the label is missing or empty."

description: requires every container in a Pod to set spec.containers[].resources.requests.memory, spec.containers[].resources.requests.cpu, and spec.containers[].resources.limits.memory. Deny admission if any container is missing any of these three fields. CPU limit is NOT required.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that requires every container in a Pod to set spec.containers[].resources.requests.memory, spec.containers[].resources.requests.cpu, and spec.containers[].resources.limits.memory. Deny admission if any container is missing any of these three fields. CPU limit is NOT required. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/gen_require_resource_limits.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices
    policies.kyverno.io/description: Requires every container in a Pod to set memory requests, CPU requests, and memory limits. CPU limits are not required. Admission is denied if any container is missing any of these three fields.
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: medium
    policies.kyverno.io/subject: Pod
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Require Container Resource Requests and Memory Limits
  name: require-container-resources
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: variables.allContainers.all(c, has(c.resources) && has(c.resources.requests) && has(c.resources.requests.memory))
      messageExpression: "'All containers must set resources.requests.memory. Missing in: ' + variables.allContainers.filter(c, !has(c.resources) || !has(c.resources.requests) || !has(c.resources.requests.memory)).map(c, c.name).join(', ')"
    - expression: variables.allContainers.all(c, has(c.resources) && has(c.resources.requests) && has(c.resources.requests.cpu))
      messageExpression: "'All containers must set resources.requests.cpu. Missing in: ' + variables.allContainers.filter(c, !has(c.resources) || !has(c.resources.requests) || !has(c.resources.requests.cpu)).map(c, c.name).join(', ')"
    - expression: variables.allContainers.all(c, has(c.resources) && has(c.resources.limits) && has(c.resources.limits.memory))
      messageExpression: "'All containers must set resources.limits.memory. Missing in: ' + variables.allContainers.filter(c, !has(c.resources) || !has(c.resources.limits) || !has(c.resources.limits.memory)).map(c, c.name).join(', ')"
  variables:
    - expression: object.spec.containers + object.spec.?initContainers.orValue([]) + object.spec.?ephemeralContainers.orValue([])
      name: allContainers

description: only allows Pod container images that begin with the prefix 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'. Deny any Pod whose containers use images from any other registry.
prompt:
Write a Kyverno 1.17+ ValidatingPolicy (apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1) that only allows Pod container images that begin with the prefix 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'. Deny any Pod whose containers use images from any other registry. Write the policy to /Users/shreyas/Nirmata/policy-bench/output/nctl/gen_restrict_registries.yaml.

Look up Kyverno 1.17+ documentation and examples before writing the policy:
- https://kyverno.io/docs/
- https://github.com/kyverno/kyverno-policies
output_yaml:
apiVersion: policies.kyverno.io/v1
kind: ValidatingPolicy
metadata:
  annotations:
    policies.kyverno.io/category: Best Practices, Supply Chain Security
    policies.kyverno.io/description: "Images from unknown or public registries can be of dubious quality and may not be scanned and secured, representing a high degree of risk. Requiring use of known, approved registries helps reduce threat exposure by ensuring image pulls only come from them. This policy validates that all container images (including initContainers and ephemeralContainers) only originate from the approved registries 'eu.foo.io/' or 'bar.io/'. Any image not prefixed with one of these registries will be denied."
    policies.kyverno.io/minversion: 1.17.0
    policies.kyverno.io/severity: high
    policies.kyverno.io/title: Restrict Image Registries
  name: restrict-image-registries
spec:
  evaluation:
    background:
      enabled: true
  matchConstraints:
    resourceRules:
      - apiGroups:
          - ""
        apiVersions:
          - v1
        operations:
          - CREATE
          - UPDATE
        resources:
          - pods
  validationActions:
    - Deny
  validations:
    - expression: "variables.allContainers.all(container, container.image.startsWith('eu.foo.io/') || container.image.startsWith('bar.io/'))"
